  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Staffing Hr - Version 1'
}

Table candidate.orientation_session {
  orientation_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the orientation session. Primary key.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.assignment. Business justification: Pre-assignment onboarding: orientation sessions are conducted to prepare workers for a specific assignment. Staffing operations track which assignment an orientation session was held for to confirm on']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for client-led or client-site orientation sessions. Null for agency-led sessions.']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Orientation sessions are designed and delivered against a specific client credential package (e.g., a healthcare client\'s onboarding package mandates a facility-specific orientation). This link enable']
  credential_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Safety and compliance orientations are frequently mandated credential requirements (e.g., OSHA safety orientation, client-specific onboarding). Linking orientation_session to credential_requirement al']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Orientation sessions are scheduled at specific client work sites. Capacity planning, worker assignment to sessions, and logistics (parking, badge pickup) require linking sessions to physical locations']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP managed programs standardize onboarding orientation for all contingent workers. Program managers track orientation completion rates per program for compliance and SLA reporting. A staffing domain ']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Many clients require order-specific orientation before assignment start. Linking orientation_session to order_header supports compliance tracking (\'all candidates for order X completed orientation\'), ']
  position_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.position_requirement. Business justification: Position requirements can mandate specific orientation types (safety, compliance, role-specific). Linking orientation_session to position_requirement enables \'orientation completion rate by position r']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Orientation sessions are mandated by compliance requirements (state OSHA safety training, harassment prevention laws, federal contractor onboarding rules). The orientation_session.compliance_category ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Orientation sessions are frequently delivered by third-party training/onboarding suppliers. Linking to supplier enables vendor performance tracking, invoice reconciliation, and compliance auditing of ']
  talent_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to candidate.talent_pool. Business justification: Orientation sessions in staffing are frequently designed and targeted for specific talent pools (e.g., a light industrial pool onboarding session, a healthcare compliance orientation for a specific be']
  task_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.task_checklist. Business justification: In staffing operations, orientation sessions are mandated by onboarding task checklists (task_checklist.requires_orientation_flag=true). Linking orientation_session to the governing task_checklist ena']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the orientation session concluded. Used for duration tracking and compliance reporting.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the orientation session began. May differ from scheduled time.']
  assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether workers must pass an assessment or quiz to complete the orientation.']
  attended_count varchar [note: 'Number of workers who actually attended the orientation session. Updated after session completion.']
  average_satisfaction_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average satisfaction rating from attendee feedback surveys, typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.']
  completed_count varchar [note: 'Number of workers who successfully completed all orientation requirements and passed any assessments.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'Classification of compliance requirements addressed by this orientation (e.g., OSHA safety, client-mandated, industry-specific).']
  cost_per_attendee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost incurred per worker attending the orientation, including facilitator time, materials, and facility costs. Used for internal cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the orientation session record was first created in the system.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned or actual duration of the orientation session in hours. Used for scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  facilitator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the orientation facilitator for coordination and follow-up.']
  facilitator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person conducting or leading the orientation session (internal staff or client representative).']
  facilitator_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the orientation facilitator.']
  feedback_survey_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-orientation feedback survey was sent to attendees.']
  interpreter_provided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether language interpretation services are provided for this orientation session.']
  language varchar [note: 'Primary language in which the orientation is conducted, using ISO 639-2 three-letter language codes (e.g., ENG, SPA, FRA).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the orientation session record was last updated.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category of physical or virtual location where the orientation is conducted.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attendance at this orientation session is mandatory for worker assignment eligibility.']
  materials_provided varchar [note: 'Description of materials distributed during orientation (e.g., Employee Handbook, Safety Manual, Benefits Guide, ID Badge).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the orientation session, including special circumstances, issues encountered, or follow-up actions required.']
  orientation_type varchar [note: 'Delivery method for the orientation session. Determines logistics and technology requirements.']
  passing_score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required to pass the orientation assessment, if applicable.']
  physical_location varchar [note: 'Physical address or facility name where in-person orientation is held (e.g., Main Office - Conference Room A, Client Site - Building 3).']
  recording_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recording of the orientation session is available for workers who missed the live session.']
  recording_url varchar [note: 'URL or storage location of the orientation session recording. Confidential to control access.']
  registered_count varchar [note: 'Number of workers currently registered for this orientation session.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Date the orientation session is scheduled to occur. Used for planning and worker notification.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the orientation session is scheduled to end.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the orientation session is scheduled to begin.']
  session_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of workers that can attend this orientation session. Used for scheduling and logistics planning.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the orientation session, used for external reference and scheduling systems.']
  session_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the orientation session (e.g., New Hire Orientation - Healthcare Division, Client-Site Safety Training).']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the orientation session. Tracks progression from planning through completion.']
  topics_covered varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured list of topics covered in the orientation (e.g., Company Policies, Safety Procedures, Benefits Overview, Timekeeping, Compliance Training).']
  virtual_meeting_link varchar [note: 'URL or meeting link for virtual orientation sessions (e.g., Zoom, Teams, WebEx). Confidential to prevent unauthorized access.']
  virtual_meeting_reference varchar [note: 'Meeting ID or access code for virtual orientation platform. Confidential credential.']

  Note: 'Scheduled or completed orientation session for one or more workers. Tracks orientation type (in-person, virtual, client-site, self-paced), session date, location or virtual link, facilitator, duration, topics covered, and per-worker attendance status. May be staffing-agency-led or client-led. Linked to onboarding_record for milestone completion tracking.'
}

Table candidate.profile {
  profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the candidate profile record. Primary key and anchor for all child entities in the candidate domain. MASTER_PARTY role — all downstream candidate domain products resolve to this record via FK.']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Candidate pipeline reporting and order-matching require categorizing profiles by job category. Recruiters filter candidate pools by job_category_id for open requisitions. A staffing ATS expert would e']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the candidates current residence. Used for tax jurisdiction determination (SUTA, FUTA, state income tax withholding), workers compensation policy assignment, and I-9 documentation.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the candidates residence (apartment, suite, unit number). Supplements address_line1 for complete mailing address used in payroll tax filings and compliance documentation.']
  ats_candidate_ref varchar [note: 'External candidate identifier assigned by the source Applicant Tracking System (ATS), e.g., Bullhorn candidate record ID. Used for cross-system reconciliation and deduplication between the lakehouse and the operational ATS.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Attribution model applied to assign sourcing credit for this candidate. First-touch = credit to initial channel; Last-touch = credit to final channel before application; Multi-touch = distributed credit; Linear = equal credit across all touchpoints. Drives cost-per-source and channel ROI calculations.']
  availability_date date [note: 'Earliest date the candidate is available to begin a new assignment or placement. Critical for Time to Fill (TTF) and Time to Start (TTS) KPI calculations and for matching candidates to job orders with specific start date requirements.']
  birth_date date [note: 'Candidates date of birth in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for age verification, I-9 eligibility, background check identity matching, and EEOC reporting. Stored masked or access-controlled per GDPR/CCPA.']
  candidate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the candidate in the ATS talent pool. Active = available and engaged; Placed = currently on assignment; On Bench = unassigned but available for redeployment; Inactive = not currently pursuing opportunities; Do Not Use = flagged for exclusion; Pending Onboarding = offer accepted, onboarding in progress.']
  candidate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the candidates engagement model with the staffing firm. W-2 = employee on firms payroll; 1099 = independent contractor (IC); EOR = employer of record arrangement; C2C = corp-to-corp; PEO = professional employer organization. Drives payroll tax treatment, workers comp assignment, and co-employment risk assessment.']
  ccpa_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has acknowledged CCPA privacy rights disclosure and provided consent for data processing. Required for California residents. Supports CCPA opt-out and data deletion request workflows.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the candidates current residential address. Used for geographic talent pool analytics, commute radius matching, and state/local tax jurisdiction determination.']
  cost_per_source decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary cost attributed to sourcing this specific candidate, in USD. Includes job board fees, advertising spend, referral bonuses, or RPO campaign costs allocated per candidate. Used for sourcing ROI and cost-per-hire analytics.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the candidates current residential address (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Required for international operations, GDPR applicability determination, and work authorization validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the candidate profile record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category. Used for data lineage, SLA measurement, and pipeline age calculations.']
  data_classification varchar [note: 'Data governance classification level assigned to this candidate profile record. Drives access control policies, data masking rules, and retention schedules. Restricted = highest sensitivity (PII-heavy); Confidential = sensitive business data; Internal = standard operational; Public = no sensitivity.']
  do_not_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has opted out of all recruiter outreach and communications. When True, all automated and manual contact must cease immediately. Enforced per GDPR right to erasure/restriction and CCPA opt-out rights.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Candidates current employment situation at the time of last profile update. Used to prioritize outreach urgency, assess availability, and tailor placement strategy (temp vs. perm vs. contract-to-hire).']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal given name of the candidate as recorded in the ATS. Used for identity verification, onboarding documentation (I-9, W-2), and candidate communications.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has provided explicit GDPR consent for the firm to store and process their personal data. Required for candidates in EU/EEA jurisdictions. Must be True before any data processing occurs for covered individuals.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self-reported gender identity of the candidate. Collected voluntarily for EEOC workforce diversity reporting and OFCCP compliance. Not used in placement decisions per EEOC guidelines.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal family name (surname) of the candidate as recorded in the ATS. Required for identity verification, payroll tax filings (W-2, 1099), and compliance documentation.']
  linkedin_url varchar [note: 'URL to the candidates LinkedIn public profile. Used by recruiters for skills verification, professional history review, and social sourcing attribution. Supports talent pool enrichment and redeployment targeting.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Legal middle name or initial of the candidate. Required for background check (BGC) identity matching via Sterling and for I-9 employment eligibility verification.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile/cell phone number for the candidate. Used for SMS outreach, two-factor authentication during onboarding, and time-sensitive placement notifications. Distinct from primary_phone which may be a landline.']
  portfolio_url varchar [note: 'URL to the candidates professional portfolio, personal website, or GitHub profile. Relevant for creative, technical, and IT roles where work samples are evaluated during screening.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP or postal code of the candidates current residential address. Used for geographic proximity matching to job orders, tax jurisdiction lookup, and workforce density analytics.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Name the candidate prefers to be addressed by in communications and recruiter interactions. May differ from legal name. Used in outreach, interview scheduling, and placement correspondence.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for all recruiter outreach, ATS communications, onboarding workflows, and digital document delivery (DocuSign). Must be unique per active candidate profile.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for candidate contact, stored in E.164 international format. Used for recruiter outreach, interview scheduling, and urgent placement communications.']
  pronouns varchar [note: 'Candidates self-reported preferred pronouns (e.g., he/him, she/her, they/them). Collected voluntarily to support inclusive recruiter communications and workplace culture alignment.']
  source_channel_name varchar [note: 'Specific name of the sourcing channel or platform (e.g., Indeed, LinkedIn Recruiter, ZipRecruiter, Employee Referral, Beeline VMS Portal, Company Career Site). Provides granular sourcing attribution below the channel type level.']
  source_channel_type varchar [note: 'Category of the channel through which the candidate was originally sourced into the ATS. Used for sourcing ROI analysis, cost-per-source reporting, and channel attribution modeling. Supports talent acquisition strategy optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|referral|social_media|direct_outreach|rpo_campaign|vms_portal|career_site|walk_in — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this candidate profile was originally ingested into the lakehouse. Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and master data management. Bullhorn is the primary ATS source.']
  sourcing_date date [note: 'Date the candidate was first sourced and entered into the ATS talent pool. Used to calculate candidate pipeline age, sourcing velocity metrics, and Time to Fill (TTF) baseline measurements.']
  ssn_masked varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the candidates Social Security Number stored in masked format (XXX-XX-NNNN). Used for identity verification, payroll tax processing (W-2), and E-Verify matching. Full SSN is never stored in the lakehouse.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the candidates current residential address (ISO 3166-2 subdivision code, e.g., CA, TX, NY). Critical for SUTA tax assignment, workers compensation rate determination, and FLSA/state labor law compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the candidate profile record in the lakehouse (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'UTM campaign parameter captured from the candidates application URL. Identifies the specific marketing campaign name or code for multi-touch attribution modeling and recruitment marketing ROI reporting.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'UTM medium parameter captured from the candidates application URL (e.g., cpc, organic, email, social). Identifies the marketing medium for digital sourcing attribution analysis.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'UTM source parameter captured from the candidates application URL (e.g., indeed, linkedin, google). Used for digital marketing attribution and cost-per-source analytics in conjunction with utm_medium and utm_campaign.']
  visa_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the candidates work visa or employment authorization document (EAD). Used to trigger re-verification workflows, prevent non-compliant placements, and manage I-9 re-verification timelines.']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Candidates legal authorization to work in the applicable jurisdiction. Determines eligibility for placement, I-9 document requirements, and E-Verify processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: us_citizen|permanent_resident|tn_visa|h1b|opt|stem_opt|other_visa|not_authorized — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'SSOT master record for every talent supply-side individual tracked in the ATS. Stores core identity: legal name, preferred name, date of birth, gender, pronouns, contact details (email, phone, address), SSN (masked), LinkedIn URL, portfolio URL, candidate type (W-2, 1099, EOR), current employment status, ATS record ID, source system, data classification level, GDPR/CCPA consent flags, do-not-contact flag, and audit timestamps. Captures sourcing attribution: source channel type (job board, referral, social media, direct outreach, RPO campaign, VMS portal, career site, walk-in), source channel name, campaign ID, sourcing recruiter, sourcing date, cost-per-source, UTM parameters, and attribution model (first-touch, last-touch, multi-touch). Anchor entity for the entire candidate domain — all child entities resolve to this record via profile_id FK.'
}

Table candidate.resume {
  resume_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each resume record in the Staffing Hr platform. Primary key for the resume data product within the candidate domain.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate profile that owns this resume. Links the resume to the candidate master record in the Bullhorn ATS candidate object.']
  bullhorn_resume_ref varchar [note: 'External identifier for this resume record as assigned by the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system of record. Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracking, and integration with Bullhorn API workflows.']
  candidate_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has provided explicit consent for their resume data to be stored, processed, and used for talent matching and placement activities. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance in international and California-based operations.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate provided consent for resume data processing. Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails. Null if consent has not been obtained or is not applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resume record was first created in the data platform. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and SOC 2 compliance tracking.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the resume is considered outdated and should be flagged for refresh or archival. Typically set based on business rules (e.g., 12–24 months from upload) to ensure talent pool data currency.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the uploaded resume document. Determines compatibility with parsing engines and downstream ATS workflows. Common formats include PDF and DOCX as supported by Bullhorn ATS.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Original file name of the uploaded resume document as provided by the candidate or recruiter. Used for document identification and retrieval from the storage system.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the resume file in bytes. Used for storage management, upload validation, and ensuring compliance with maximum file size limits enforced by the ATS or candidate portal.']
  highest_education_level varchar [note: 'Highest academic degree or credential level attained as extracted from the resume. Used for candidate qualification filtering against job order education requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: High School|Associate|Bachelor|Master|Doctorate|Professional|Certificate|None — promote to reference product]']
  is_active_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this resume version is the current active version used for ATS search, talent pool matching, and recruiter review in Bullhorn. Only one resume per candidate should be flagged as active at any time.']
  is_parsed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the resume has been successfully processed by the parsing engine and structured data has been extracted. False indicates the resume is raw/unprocessed or parsing failed.']
  is_searchable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this resume is indexed and available for full-text search and talent pool matching queries in the ATS. May be set to false for resumes pending parsing, under compliance hold, or for candidates who have opted out of search.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 or BCP 47 language code identifying the primary language in which the resume is written (e.g., en, es, fr). Supports multilingual candidate pools and international staffing operations.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which this resume version was submitted to a client or used in a job order submittal workflow. Supports redeployment tracking and identification of stale resumes requiring candidate outreach.']
  most_recent_employer varchar [note: 'Name of the most recent employer extracted from the resume work history. Used for quick candidate qualification review and employment gap analysis in recruiter workflows.']
  most_recent_job_title varchar [note: 'The most recent or current job title extracted from the resume. Used as the primary candidate headline in ATS search results, talent pool displays, and recruiter-facing candidate summaries.']
  page_count varchar [note: 'Number of pages in the resume document. Used for resume quality assessment and formatting compliance checks during candidate screening workflows.']
  parse_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0.00–100.00) assigned by the parsing engine indicating the quality and completeness of the structured data extracted from the resume. Low scores may trigger manual review workflows in the ATS.']
  parse_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the resume through the parsing pipeline. Indicates whether the resume has been successfully parsed, is awaiting parsing, encountered a failure, or requires manual review by a recruiter.']
  parse_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resume parsing process was completed. Distinct from upload timestamp; captures the processing event time for SLA tracking of the parsing pipeline.']
  parsed_education varchar [note: 'Structured education history extracted from the resume by the parsing engine, including degree levels, institutions, and fields of study. Supports candidate qualification screening and compliance with client education requirements.']
  parsed_employers varchar [note: 'Structured list of employer names extracted from the resume work history by the parsing engine. Used for employment verification cross-referencing, candidate background check initiation via Sterling, and work history completeness assessment.']
  parsed_job_titles varchar [note: 'Structured list of job titles extracted from the resume work history by the parsing engine. Used for candidate classification, talent pool segmentation, and job order matching in Bullhorn ATS.']
  parsed_skills varchar [note: 'Structured representation of skills extracted from the resume by the parsing engine, stored as a delimited string or serialized list. Feeds the candidate skills inventory and ATS job-order matching algorithms. Examples include technical skills, certifications, and soft skills.']
  parsing_engine varchar [note: 'Name of the automated resume parsing engine used to extract structured data from the raw resume document. Enables quality benchmarking across parsing vendors and troubleshooting of parse failures.']
  parsing_engine_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the parsing engine used at the time of resume processing. Supports reproducibility audits and regression analysis when parsing engine versions are upgraded.']
  primary_skill_category varchar [note: 'Top-level skill category or discipline assigned to the candidate based on parsed resume content (e.g., Information Technology, Healthcare, Finance, Engineering). Supports talent pool segmentation and workforce planning analytics.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite quality score (0.00–100.00) assigned to the resume based on completeness, formatting, keyword density, and parse confidence. Used for talent pool ranking and Quality of Submission (QoS) reporting in recruiter dashboards.']
  raw_text varchar [note: 'Full plain-text extraction of the resume document content prior to structured parsing. Stored as a text blob to support full-text search, keyword matching, and AI/ML talent matching workflows in Bullhorn ATS.']
  recruiter_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the recruiter during resume review, including observations about candidate qualifications, formatting issues, or follow-up actions required. Supports recruiter collaboration and ATS workflow documentation.']
  source_job_board varchar [note: 'Name of the external job board or sourcing platform from which the resume was originally obtained (e.g., Indeed, LinkedIn, Monster, CareerBuilder, Dice). Supports sourcing channel attribution and cost-per-hire analytics.']
  storage_uri varchar [note: 'Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) pointing to the physical location of the resume file in the document storage system (e.g., Azure Blob Storage, AWS S3, or Bullhorn file storage). Used for document retrieval and rendering.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resume record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']
  upload_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the resume was submitted or ingested into the system. Supports sourcing channel attribution analytics and ATS workflow routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: candidate_portal|recruiter_upload|email_parse|linkedin_import|job_board|vms_import|api_integration — promote to reference product]']
  upload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the resume file was uploaded or ingested into the system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the resume record lifecycle. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the resume for a given candidate. Increments each time a new resume is uploaded or parsed, enabling version history tracking and identification of the most current document.']
  word_count varchar [note: 'Total number of words in the resume document as extracted during parsing. Used as a proxy for resume completeness and quality scoring in ATS workflows and talent pool management.']
  years_of_experience decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total years of professional work experience calculated or extracted from the resume work history by the parsing engine. Used as a primary screening criterion for job order matching and candidate ranking in ATS workflows.']

  Note: 'Stores parsed and raw resume content for each candidate version. Tracks resume version number, file format (PDF, DOCX), raw text blob, parsed structured content (skills extracted, titles extracted, education extracted), parsing engine used (e.g., Sovren, Textkernel), parse confidence score, upload source (portal, recruiter upload, email parse), upload timestamp, active version flag, and storage URI. Supports ATS resume search and talent pool matching workflows in Bullhorn.'
}

Table candidate.work_history {
  work_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each candidate work history record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Employment verification BGC components produce a credential_instance record that verifies a specific work history entry. Linking work_history to credential_instance allows recruiters and compliance te']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Candidate experience classification by job category drives order-matching analytics and redeployment targeting. Staffing firms categorize each work history entry against the standard job_category taxo']
  prior_client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: Recruiters routinely check whether a candidate has previously worked at a client account (prior placement history) before submitting — this affects RTR eligibility, DNP status, and relationship contex']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose employment history this record describes. Links to the candidate master profile in the Candidate domain.']
  resume_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to candidate.resume. Business justification: Work history records are frequently parsed or derived from a candidate\'s resume. Adding resume_id to work_history creates a traceable provenance link from each structured employment record back to the']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: When is_staffing_agency_placement=true, the prior staffing agency is a known supplier. Linking work_history to supplier enables competitive intelligence reporting, redeployment sourcing analysis, and ']
  bullhorn_work_history_ref varchar [note: 'The native record identifier for this work history entry in the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system. Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracking, and incremental data loads from the source system.']
  compensation_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the compensation reported in this work history record. Defaults to USD for domestic U.S. placements. Required for international candidate records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work history record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the completeness and reliability of this work history record based on field population, verification status, and source system confidence. Used for talent pool quality reporting and AI/ML feature weighting.']
  direct_reports_count varchar [note: 'Number of direct reports the candidate managed in this role. Zero for individual contributor roles. Used to assess leadership experience for management-level placements.']
  employer_location_city varchar [note: 'City where the candidate performed work for this employer. Used for geographic placement matching and commute analysis.']
  employer_location_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the candidate performed work for this employer. Supports international placement matching and cross-border compliance tracking.']
  employer_location_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state (or province/region for international) where the candidate performed work for this employer. Used for geographic placement matching, state-specific compliance, and labor law applicability.']
  employer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization where the candidate was employed during this work history period. Sourced from resume parsing or candidate-submitted data in Bullhorn ATS.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment arrangement during this work history period (e.g., Full-Time Equivalent (FTE), contract, temporary, self-employed). Critical for distinguishing permanent vs. contingent experience. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTE|contract|temp|self_employed|part_time|internship — promote to reference product]']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidates employment at this employer in this role ended. Null if is_current is True. Used for tenure calculation and gap analysis.']
  gap_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the employment gap immediately preceding this work history records start date. Populated only when has_employment_gap is True. Used for tenure analysis and candidate risk scoring.']
  has_employment_gap boolean [note: 'Indicates whether there is a notable gap in employment immediately preceding this work history records start date. Computed during ingestion from chronological work history analysis. Used for candidate screening and placement suitability assessment.']
  industry_sector varchar [note: 'The industry sector of the employer for this work history record (e.g., Healthcare, Technology, Manufacturing, Finance). Used for industry-specific placement matching and talent pool segmentation. Aligned with NAICS or SIC classification.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the candidates current active employer and role. When True, end_date is expected to be null. Used to identify active employment status during placement suitability assessment.']
  is_relevant_experience boolean [note: 'Recruiter-assigned flag indicating whether this work history record is considered relevant experience for current active job orders. Supports targeted talent pool filtering and placement suitability scoring.']
  is_staffing_agency_placement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work history record represents a placement made through a staffing agency (including Staffing Hr). Used to distinguish direct employment from contingent staffing experience and to identify redeployment candidates.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work history record has been independently verified through a background check or employment verification process. Verified records carry higher confidence for placement decisions.']
  job_function varchar [note: 'Standardized functional category of the role held (e.g., Engineering, Sales, Finance, Operations, Healthcare). Used for talent pool segmentation, placement matching, and workforce analytics reporting in Power BI.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The position title held by the candidate at the employer during this work history period. Used for skill inference, placement suitability scoring, and tenure analysis.']
  management_level varchar [note: 'Seniority or management level of the role held during this work history period. Used for placement suitability scoring and headcount planning analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: individual_contributor|team_lead|manager|director|vp|c_suite|executive — promote to reference product]']
  pay_rate_type varchar [note: 'Compensation structure type for this work history record (e.g., hourly, salaried, daily rate, per diem, commission-based). Used for compensation benchmarking and pay rate analysis in placement decisions.']
  reason_for_leaving varchar [note: 'Candidate-provided or recruiter-captured reason for departing this employer. Used for candidate risk assessment, fall-off rate analysis, and placement suitability evaluation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: resignation|layoff|contract_end|termination|retirement|relocation|career_growth|other — promote to reference product]']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this work history record within the data platform (active, deleted by candidate request, flagged as duplicate, or pending verification). Supports GDPR/CCPA right-to-erasure workflows and data quality management.']
  reported_pay_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Candidate-reported compensation rate for this work history record, expressed in the unit defined by pay_rate_type and compensation_currency. Used for pay rate benchmarking and spread analysis in placement pricing. Classified confidential as candidate financial data.']
  responsibilities_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text summary of the candidates key duties, responsibilities, and accomplishments in this role. Sourced from resume parsing or candidate-submitted data in Bullhorn. Used for skill inference, keyword matching, and placement suitability scoring.']
  resume_section_order varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this work history entry as it appeared in the candidates parsed resume (1 = most recent). Used to preserve resume ordering for recruiter review and ATS display.']
  skills_inferred varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of skills inferred or extracted from the responsibilities_summary and job_title for this work history record via resume parsing. Used to populate the candidate skills inventory and drive placement matching in Bullhorn ATS.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code mapped to the job title held in this work history record. Enables standardized occupational analysis, wage benchmarking, and cross-candidate skill comparison.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or ingestion method from which this work history record was originally captured (e.g., Bullhorn ATS resume parsing, candidate portal self-entry, manual recruiter entry). Used for data lineage and quality scoring.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate began employment at this employer in this role. Used for tenure calculation and chronological work history ordering.']
  tenure_months varchar [note: 'Duration of employment at this employer in this role, expressed in whole months. Calculated from start_date and end_date (or current date if is_current). Stored for efficient tenure analysis and placement suitability scoring without repeated computation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work history record was last modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date on which this work history record was verified. Null if is_verified is False. Used for compliance tracking and background check audit trails.']
  verification_source varchar [note: 'The source or method used to verify this work history record (e.g., Sterling Background Check, direct employer contact, reference check). Null or not_verified if is_verified is False.']
  work_arrangement varchar [note: 'The physical work arrangement for this role (on-site, remote, or hybrid). Used for placement preference matching and workforce planning analytics.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Tax and legal classification of the candidates engagement type during this work history period (W-2 employee, 1099 independent contractor, Employer of Record (EOR), Corp-to-Corp (C2C)). Critical for IRS worker classification compliance and co-employment risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Structured employment history records for each candidate, capturing prior employer name, job title held, employment type (FTE, contract, temp, self-employed), start date, end date, is_current flag, industry sector, responsibilities summary, reason for leaving, verified flag, verification source, and gap indicator. Sourced from resume parsing and candidate-submitted data in Bullhorn. Used for tenure analysis, skill inference, and placement suitability scoring.'
}

Table candidate.skill {
  skill_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a candidate skill record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the skill data product within the candidate domain.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate who possesses this skill. Links the skill record to the owning candidate profile in the candidate domain SSOT.']
  billable_rate_premium decimal(10,4) [note: 'Incremental bill rate premium (in USD) associated with this skill when pricing candidate placements. Reflects the market premium clients pay for candidates with this specific skill. Used in rate card configuration and spread/markup calculations. Confidential pricing data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this skill record was first created in the system of record. Audit trail field supporting data governance, compliance reporting, and Silver Layer lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  demand_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative market demand score for this skill based on open job order frequency, client request patterns, and labor market data. Used in workforce planning, bench management, and redeployment prioritization. Higher values indicate higher current market demand.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this skill record is considered valid and active for talent matching and placement purposes. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) patterns in the Silver Layer and enables point-in-time skill profile reconstruction for compliance and audit.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this skill record version expires or is superseded. Null for currently active records. Supports SCD Type 2 history tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse for point-in-time candidate skill profile reconstruction.']
  endorsement_count varchar [note: 'Number of endorsements or validations this skill has received from recruiters, clients, or peers. Higher endorsement counts increase the skills credibility weight in QoS scoring and talent pool ranking algorithms.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this skill is designated as one of the candidates top marketable skills. Primary skills are surfaced prominently in Bullhorn ATS submittal profiles, talent pool search results, and QoS scoring. True = primary/top skill; False = secondary or supporting skill.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this skill has been independently verified through a formal assessment, certification, or recruiter validation process. True = verified; False = unverified (self-reported or resume-parsed only). Verified skills carry higher weight in QoS scoring and submittal quality metrics.']
  language_proficiency_standard varchar [note: 'Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR) proficiency level applicable when skill_category is language. A1/A2: basic user; B1/B2: independent user; C1/C2: proficient user. Null for non-language skills. Enables standardized language skill matching for multilingual placement requirements.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the candidate actively applied this skill in a professional context. Used to assess skill currency and recency for job order matching. A stale last_used_date may reduce QoS score for time-sensitive technical skills.']
  normalized_skill_name varchar [note: 'Canonical, taxonomy-normalized version of the skill name after standardization processing (e.g., MS Excel normalized to Microsoft Excel, JS normalized to JavaScript). Enables consistent cross-candidate skill comparison, deduplication, and job order matching across ATS and VMS platforms.']
  pay_rate_premium decimal(10,4) [note: 'Incremental pay rate premium (in USD) associated with this skill when compensating placed candidates. Reflects the market premium paid to candidates possessing this skill. Used alongside billable_rate_premium to calculate spread and gross margin for skill-based placements.']
  placement_type_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates which worker classification or placement type this skill is relevant for. W-2 (employee), 1099 (independent contractor), EOR (Employer of Record), or all placement types. Used to filter talent pools by worker classification in Bullhorn ATS and Beeline VMS matching workflows.']
  proficiency_level varchar [note: 'Self-assessed or recruiter-assigned proficiency level for this skill. Drives Quality of Submission (QoS) scoring and candidate-to-job-order matching in Bullhorn. Beginner: foundational awareness; Intermediate: working knowledge; Advanced: deep proficiency; Expert: authoritative mastery.']
  proficiency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric proficiency score assigned through a formal assessment or standardized test (e.g., 0.00–100.00 scale). Complements the categorical proficiency_level with a precise quantitative measure for advanced talent matching and QoS scoring. Null when no formal assessment has been conducted.']
  qos_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to this skill for Quality of Submission (QoS) scoring in Bullhorn ATS. Reflects the relative importance of this skill in candidate-to-job-order matching. Higher weights indicate skills that are more critical to placement success. Expressed as a decimal between 0.0000 and 1.0000.']
  redeployment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this skill qualifies the candidate for redeployment consideration in bench management workflows. True = skill is in active demand and candidate should be surfaced for redeployment matching; False = skill does not currently support redeployment eligibility. Supports bench tracking and redeployment workflows in Bullhorn.']
  skill_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the skill type used to segment the talent pool and drive job order matching logic. Values: technical (programming, engineering), soft_skill (communication, leadership), domain_expertise (industry knowledge), language (spoken/written languages), tool_platform (software applications and platforms).']
  skill_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the candidates application of this skill, including specific tools, methodologies, or contexts in which the skill was used. Supports recruiter notes and enhances submittal quality in Bullhorn ATS.']
  skill_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the skill or competency (e.g., Python, Payroll Processing, Conflict Resolution, SAP SuccessFactors). Normalized to a controlled taxonomy where available to support consistent talent pool search and job order matching in Bullhorn ATS.']
  skill_source varchar [note: 'Provenance indicator describing how this skill was attributed to the candidate. Self_reported: candidate entered directly; resume_parsed: extracted via ATS resume parsing in Bullhorn; assessment_verified: confirmed through a formal skills assessment; recruiter_tagged: added by a recruiter; certification_derived: inferred from a verified credential or certification record.']
  skill_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the skill record. Active: skill is current and searchable in talent pool; inactive: skill has been deactivated by recruiter or candidate; pending_verification: skill awaiting assessment or credential confirmation; archived: historical record retained for compliance and audit purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this skill record originated or was last synchronized. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Values: bullhorn (Bullhorn ATS/CRM), successfactors (SAP SuccessFactors), beeline (Beeline VMS), linkedin (LinkedIn integration), manual (direct data entry), resume_parser (automated resume parsing engine).']
  source_system_skill_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier for this skill record in the originating operational system (e.g., Bullhorn skill record ID, SuccessFactors competency ID). Enables bidirectional reconciliation between the Silver Layer lakehouse and source systems for data quality and lineage purposes.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the skill_category (e.g., within technical: programming_language, database, cloud_platform; within domain_expertise: healthcare_staffing, finance, manufacturing). Supports fine-grained talent pool segmentation and job order matching.']
  taxonomy_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code from a controlled skill taxonomy (e.g., O*NET SOC skill codes, ESCO skill URIs, or internal Staffing Hr taxonomy identifiers) used to normalize skill names across ATS, VMS, and job board integrations. Enables cross-system skill-to-requirement mapping in Beeline VMS and Bullhorn.']
  taxonomy_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the controlled vocabulary or taxonomy system from which the taxonomy_code is drawn. Supports multi-taxonomy normalization across ATS and VMS platforms. Values: onet (U.S. DOL O*NET), esco (EU ESCO framework), internal (Staffing Hr proprietary taxonomy), linkedin (LinkedIn Skills taxonomy), custom (client-specific taxonomy).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this skill record in the system of record. Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person, system, or organization that verified this skill (e.g., recruiter name, assessment platform name, certification body). Provides audit trail for skill verification provenance.']
  verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the skill was formally verified or validated. Populated when is_verified = True. Used to assess verification currency and determine if re-verification is required for compliance-sensitive placements.']
  years_of_experience decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of years the candidate has actively used or applied this skill across all work history entries. Expressed as a decimal to support fractional years (e.g., 2.5 years). Used in talent pool search filters and QoS scoring workflows.']

  Note: 'Canonical skill inventory record for a candidate, representing a single skill or competency attributed to the individual. Captures skill name (normalized to a controlled taxonomy where available), skill category (technical, soft skill, domain expertise, language, tool/platform), proficiency level (beginner, intermediate, advanced, expert), years of experience with the skill, last used date, skill source (self-reported, resume parsed, assessment verified, recruiter tagged), endorsement count, and is_primary flag indicating the candidates top marketable skills. Supports talent pool search, job order matching via skill-to-requirement mapping, and QoS (Quality of Submission) scoring workflows in Bullhorn. Skills may be linked to specific work_history entries or assessment results for provenance.'
}

Table candidate.availability {
  availability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the candidate availability and preference record. Primary key for this data product.']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Available-candidate-by-job-category matching is a core daily staffing operation. Recruiters run \'show me available candidates for job category X\' reports. Linking availability to job_category enables ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose availability and placement preferences this record describes. Links to the candidate master profile in the Candidate domain.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current placement availability state of the candidate. available indicates actively seeking work; on_assignment indicates currently placed; bench indicates between assignments but still engaged; unavailable indicates not seeking work; do_not_contact indicates candidate has opted out of outreach. Critical for bench tracking and redeployment workflows in Bullhorn ATS.']
  available_end_date date [note: 'The date through which the candidate has indicated availability, if bounded. Null indicates open-ended availability. Useful for temporary or project-based candidates with known commitment windows.']
  available_start_date date [note: 'The earliest date on which the candidate is available to begin a new assignment or placement. Used by recruiters for job order matching and dispatch scheduling. Sourced from Bullhorn ATS candidate record.']
  confirmed_date date [note: 'Date on which the candidates current availability status was last confirmed by a recruiter or by the candidate directly. Distinct from preference_last_updated_date; specifically tracks confirmation of the availability_status field.']
  desired_bill_rate_aware boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has been informed of or is aware of the client bill rate associated with their placement. True = candidate is aware of bill rate; False = candidate has not been informed. Relevant for co-employment risk management and transparency compliance.']
  desired_pay_rate_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum hourly or annual pay rate the candidate is targeting, expressed in USD. Represents the upper bound of the candidates compensation expectation. Used alongside desired_pay_rate_min for range-based matching.']
  desired_pay_rate_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum hourly or annual pay rate the candidate is willing to accept, expressed in USD. Used by recruiters to qualify candidates against job order pay rate budgets and to calculate spread and gross margin. Confidential compensation data.']
  do_not_place_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has been flagged as ineligible for placement (e.g., due to performance issues, compliance violations, or client restrictions). True = do not place; False = eligible for placement. Critical compliance and risk management control.']
  do_not_place_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the candidate has been flagged as do-not-place. Populated only when do_not_place_flag is True. Examples: client_restriction, compliance_violation, performance_issue, voluntary_withdrawal.']
  geo_city varchar [note: 'City within which the candidate is willing to accept placements. Used for geographic matching in job order dispatch and sourcing outreach workflows.']
  geo_radius_miles varchar [note: 'Maximum commute radius in miles from the candidates home location that they are willing to travel for an assignment. Used in proximity-based job order matching and sourcing outreach.']
  geo_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state (two-letter USPS abbreviation) within which the candidate is willing to accept placements. Supports state-level labor law compliance checks (e.g., SUTA, state wage-hour laws).']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date on which a recruiter last made contact with the candidate to confirm or update their availability status. Used to identify stale availability records and trigger re-engagement outreach. Supports candidate engagement analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text recruiter notes regarding the candidates availability, special circumstances, or placement preferences not captured in structured fields (e.g., Candidate available after current project ends mid-month; prefers no weekends).']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of notice the candidate requires before starting a new assignment or must give to their current employer. Used to calculate realistic Time to Start (TTS) for job order fulfillment planning.']
  open_to_per_diem boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is willing to accept per diem compensation arrangements (daily allowances for travel, lodging, meals). True = willing to accept per diem; False = not interested. Relevant for travel nursing, field services, and project-based placements.']
  open_to_temp_to_perm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is open to a Temp-to-Perm (contract-to-hire) arrangement where a temporary assignment may convert to a permanent position. True = open to conversion; False = not interested in conversion. Drives conversion fee eligibility tracking.']
  pay_rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the candidates desired pay rate is expressed as an hourly rate, annual salary, or daily per diem rate. Required to normalize compensation comparisons across job orders with different pay structures.']
  preference_last_updated_date date [note: 'Date on which the candidate last updated their placement preferences (job titles, industries, pay rate, work arrangement, etc.). Used to assess preference data freshness and trigger re-engagement when preferences are stale.']
  preferred_company_size varchar [note: 'Candidates stated preference for the size of the client organization they wish to work with. Supports matching candidates to client accounts by headcount tier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: startup|small|mid_market|enterprise — promote to reference product if additional tiers are needed]']
  preferred_employment_type varchar [note: 'Candidates stated preference for the type of engagement. temp = temporary/contract; contract_to_hire = Contract-to-Hire (CTH); direct_placement = Direct Hire/Permanent; fte = Full-Time Equivalent salaried role; per_diem = per diem/daily rate engagement. Drives job order matching logic in Bullhorn ATS.']
  preferred_hours_per_week_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours per week the candidate is willing to work. Prevents over-scheduling and supports overtime (OT) management. Relevant for FLSA compliance and ACA hour-tracking thresholds.']
  preferred_hours_per_week_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours per week the candidate is willing to work. Used to qualify candidates for part-time versus full-time job orders. Supports FLSA classification analysis.']
  preferred_industries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of industries the candidate prefers to work in (e.g., Healthcare, Technology, Finance). Used to match candidates to client accounts by industry vertical. Aligns with SIC/NAICS industry classification.']
  preferred_job_titles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of job titles the candidate is actively seeking. Used for keyword-based job order matching and proactive sourcing outreach in Bullhorn ATS. Examples: Software Engineer, Senior Developer, Tech Lead.']
  preferred_locations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific cities, metro areas, or regions the candidate prefers for placement (e.g., Austin TX, Dallas TX, Remote). More granular than geo_city/geo_state for multi-location preferences.']
  preferred_shift varchar [note: 'Candidates preferred work shift. Used in workforce scheduling and dispatch workflows, particularly for light industrial, healthcare, and manufacturing placements managed through Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  preferred_work_arrangement varchar [note: 'Candidates stated preference for physical work location arrangement: fully on-site, fully remote, or hybrid. Used to filter and match job orders by work model in ATS and VMS platforms.']
  preferred_work_culture varchar [note: 'Free-text or comma-separated description of the candidates preferred workplace culture attributes (e.g., collaborative, fast-paced, diverse, mission-driven). Used in qualitative matching for direct hire and FTE placements.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this availability record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR/CCPA data retention compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this availability record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for identifying recently updated availability records for recruiter workflows.']
  redeployment_candidate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this candidate has been flagged for proactive redeployment outreach before their current assignment ends. True = flagged for redeployment; False = not flagged. Supports bench management and redeployment workflows to reduce fall-off rate.']
  redeployment_target_date date [note: 'Target date by which the recruiter aims to redeploy the candidate into a new assignment. Typically set relative to the current assignment end date. Drives redeployment pipeline reporting in Power BI.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this availability record originated. Supports data lineage and master data management in the Silver Layer. Primary source is Bullhorn ATS/CRM.']
  source_system_record_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this availability record in the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn candidate ID or availability record ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  sourcing_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the candidates current availability record was captured or updated (e.g., recruiter_call, job_board, referral, linkedin, career_site, vms_portal). Supports sourcing channel attribution analytics in Power BI.']
  travel_percentage_max varchar [note: 'Maximum percentage of work time the candidate is willing to spend traveling (0–100). Used to qualify candidates for roles with travel requirements. Null if willing_to_travel is False.']
  willing_to_relocate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is open to relocating for a permanent or long-term placement opportunity. True = willing to relocate; False = not willing. Relevant for direct hire and FTE placement matching.']
  willing_to_travel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate is willing to travel for work assignments. True = willing to travel; False = not willing. Used in conjunction with travel_percentage_max for travel-intensive roles.']
  worker_classification_preference varchar [note: 'Candidates preferred worker classification for engagement: W-2 (employee of staffing firm), 1099 (independent contractor), EOR (Employer of Record), or C2C (Corp-to-Corp). Critical for IRS worker classification compliance and co-employment risk management.']

  Note: 'Tracks a candidates current and future availability for placement along with stated job search and placement preferences. Availability attributes: status (available, on assignment, bench, unavailable), available start date, preferred shift, preferred hours per week (min/max), preferred work arrangement (on-site, remote, hybrid), geographic availability (city, state, radius), willing to relocate flag, willing to travel flag, travel percentage max. Preference attributes: preferred job titles, preferred industries, preferred employment type (temp, contract-to-hire, direct placement, FTE), desired pay rate (min/max), desired bill rate awareness flag, preferred locations, preferred company size, preferred work culture attributes, open to temp-to-perm flag, open to per diem flag, preference last updated date. Critical for bench tracking, redeployment, dispatch workflows, and proactive sourcing outreach.'
}

Table candidate.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each candidate status transition record in the immutable audit log.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement record associated with this status transition, applicable for on-assignment, fall-off, redeployment, and bench transitions.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account associated with this status transition, applicable when the transition is client-driven (e.g., client rejection, client-specific DNP, placement). Null for transitions not tied to a specific client.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Interview and client feedback stages require tracking which client contact conducted interview or provided feedback. Standard ATS practice for relationship management, audit trail, and candidate exper']
  corrected_record_status_history_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original status_history record that this correction supersedes. Null when is_correction is false. Maintains immutable audit chain integrity.']
  credential_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Candidate status transitions to \'compliance hold\' are triggered by a specific unmet credential requirement. status_history already has compliance_hold and compliance_hold_reason columns; linking to cr']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Status transitions often occur at specific client locations (on-site interview, location-specific offer, site assignment). Critical for compliance reporting, workers comp classification, and candidat']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP program pipeline dashboards track candidate status transitions (submitted, interviewed, placed, fell off) at the program level for supplier scorecards and SLA measurement. status_history has clien']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) associated with this status transition, if applicable. Null for status changes not tied to a specific req (e.g., global DNP, bench, inactive).']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Compliance holds recorded in status_history (compliance_hold=true, compliance_hold_reason) are directly caused by placement_compliance_check failures. Recruiters and compliance teams must trace a cand']
  position_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.position_requirement. Business justification: Multi-position orders have multiple position_requirements under one order_header. Candidate status transitions (submitted, interviewed, rejected) must be tracked at the position_requirement level to s']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate whose ATS lifecycle status changed. Core linkage to the candidate master record in Bullhorn.']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate submittal record associated with this transition, applicable for submitted, interviewed, and placed status changes. Links to the Quality of Submission (QoS) tracking record.']
  ats_workflow_step varchar [note: 'The specific ATS workflow step or stage name within Bullhorn that corresponds to this status transition (e.g., Initial Screen, Client Submittal, Offer Extended). Provides granular workflow context beyond the high-level status.']
  bench_start_date date [note: 'The date the candidate entered bench status (unassigned but available for redeployment). Populated when new_status is bench. Null for non-bench transitions.']
  compliance_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition was triggered by or resulted in a compliance hold (e.g., pending I-9 verification, background check failure, credential expiration). Drives compliance workflow routing.']
  compliance_hold_reason varchar [note: 'The specific compliance issue causing the hold when compliance_hold is true: I-9 pending, background check (BGC) failed, drug screen pending, credential expired, E-Verify pending, or other. Null when compliance_hold is false.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status history record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Distinct from transition_timestamp (business event time). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  days_in_prior_status varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the candidate spent in the prior status before this transition. Captured at transition time for pipeline velocity and Time-to-Fill (TTF) / Time-to-Start (TTS) analytics.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'The business division or practice area code (e.g., IT, Healthcare, Finance, Light Industrial) under which this status transition is tracked. Enables division-level pipeline velocity and fill rate analytics.']
  dnp_expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the DNP restriction expires and the candidate becomes eligible for placement consideration again. Null for permanent DNP designations or non-DNP transitions.']
  dnp_initiator varchar [note: 'The role or party that initiated the DNP designation: recruiter, branch_manager, compliance_officer, client, system (automated rule), or legal. Null for non-DNP transitions. Supports compliance audit and accountability tracking.']
  dnp_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code for the DNP designation (e.g., CONDUCT_VIOLATION, SAFETY_INCIDENT, CLIENT_REQUEST, BACKGROUND_FAILURE, POLICY_VIOLATION, PERFORMANCE_ISSUE). Null for non-DNP transitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product — values exceed 6]']
  dnp_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DNP designation requires a formal compliance or legal review before the restriction can be lifted or modified. Drives DNP enforcement workflow routing. Null for non-DNP transitions.']
  dnp_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of the Do-Not-Place restriction: client_specific (restricted from a single client account), program_specific (restricted from a VMS/MSP program), or global (restricted from all placements across the enterprise). Null for non-DNP transitions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The business-effective date of the status transition, which may differ from the transition_timestamp (e.g., a backdated status change or a future-dated transition). Used for accurate pipeline and assignment reporting.']
  fall_off_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the candidate was on assignment before the fall-off occurred. Calculated at the time of the fall-off transition and stored for reporting. Null for non-fall-off transitions.']
  interview_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the interview when the transition involves an interview stage. Null for non-interview transitions. Drives pipeline conversion analytics.']
  interview_type varchar [note: 'Type of interview conducted when the transition is to the interviewed status. Null for non-interview transitions.']
  is_correction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a correction to a previously recorded status transition (e.g., a data entry error was corrected). When true, corrected_record_id should reference the original erroneous record.']
  is_dnp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition represents a Do-Not-Place (DNP) designation. When true, the DNP-specific fields (dnp_scope, dnp_reason_code, dnp_initiator, dnp_expiration_date, dnp_review_required) are populated.']
  is_fall_off boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition represents a fall-off event — a placement that ended prematurely before the agreed assignment end date. Used for fall-off rate KPI tracking and recruiter performance analytics.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The candidates ATS lifecycle status after this transition. Represents the current state entered as a result of this event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_lead|in_progress|submitted|interviewed|placed|on_assignment|bench|redeployed|inactive|do_not_place|fall_off — promote to reference product]']
  pipeline_stage_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number of this status transition within the candidates overall ATS pipeline for a given job order. Enables pipeline velocity analysis and stage-by-stage conversion funnel reporting.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The type of placement associated with this status transition: temp (temporary), temp_to_perm (contract-to-hire), direct_hire (permanent placement), contract, or SOW (Statement of Work). Null for pre-placement transitions.']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'The candidates ATS lifecycle status immediately before this transition. Enables full before/after audit trail and pipeline velocity analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_lead|in_progress|submitted|interviewed|placed|on_assignment|bench|redeployed|inactive|do_not_place|fall_off — promote to reference product]']
  redeployment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate was flagged as eligible for redeployment at the time of this status transition (e.g., when transitioning to bench or end-of-assignment). Supports bench management and redeployment pipeline workflows.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason for candidate rejection when the transition results from a client or internal rejection (e.g., OVERQUALIFIED, SALARY_MISMATCH, SKILLS_GAP, CLIENT_PREFERENCE). Null for non-rejection transitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product — values exceed 6]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this status transition event (e.g., Bullhorn ATS, Beeline VMS, SAP SuccessFactors, manual entry, API integration). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_record_ref varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Bullhorn candidate history record ID, Beeline event ID). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation.']
  transition_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the recruiter or system describing the context, rationale, or details of the status transition. Supports compliance audit trails and recruiter activity documentation.']
  transition_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the status transition occurred (e.g., CANDIDATE_WITHDREW, CLIENT_REJECTED, ASSIGNMENT_ENDED, SCREENING_FAILED, OFFER_ACCEPTED). Drives fall-off rate analysis and pipeline quality reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product — values exceed 6]']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the candidate status transition occurred. This is the authoritative business event time used for pipeline velocity, TTF, and TTS calculations.']
  transition_trigger varchar [note: 'Identifies the actor or mechanism that initiated the status transition: system (automated ATS rule), recruiter (manual recruiter action), candidate (candidate self-service or withdrawal), or client (client feedback or rejection).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this status history record was last modified in the Silver layer. Since this is an immutable audit log, updates should be rare and limited to corrections. Supports data quality monitoring.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition number from the Vendor Management System (VMS) such as Beeline, associated with this status transition for contingent workforce program candidates. Null for non-VMS placements.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'The employment classification of the candidate at the time of this status transition: W-2 (employee), 1099 (independent contractor), EOR (Employer of Record), or C2C (Corp-to-Corp). Relevant for compliance and payroll routing.']

  Note: 'Immutable audit log of all candidate lifecycle status transitions within the ATS workflow. Captures prior status, new status, transition reason code, transition trigger (system, recruiter, candidate, client), recruiter ID who made the change, associated job order ID (if applicable), transition timestamp, and notes. Status values include: new lead, in-progress, submitted, interviewed, placed, on assignment, bench, redeployed, inactive, do-not-place, fall-off. For do-not-place (DNP) transitions, additionally captures DNP scope (client-specific, program-specific, global), DNP reason code, DNP initiator, DNP expiration date, and review-required flag. Supports fall-off rate tracking, pipeline velocity, compliance audit trails, and DNP enforcement workflows.'
}

Table candidate.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each candidate interaction event record in the Bullhorn CRM activity log.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Optional reference to an active or historical placement associated with this interaction, used for assignment management communications, extension discussions, or offboarding events.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: Candidate interactions (calls, interviews, outreach) are frequently client-specific — recruiters contact candidates about specific client opportunities. Client-level interaction reporting (\'all candid']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Client-side interactions (hiring manager calls candidate, candidate calls client HR) require tracking which client contact participated.
