SSOT for onboarding status across the staffing lifecycle. One record per worker per placement. Central hub entity — all onboarding activities (tasks, documents, screenings, I-9, orientation, training, equipment, communications) link back to this record.'
}

Table recruitment.task_assignment {
  task_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the task assignment record. Primary key.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement record this task supports. Links task to the specific job assignment the worker is being onboarded or offboarded from.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: Onboarding tasks track specific BGC orders for completion monitoring, SLA management, and blocking task resolution. Essential for coordinating background check completion with onboarding workflow prog']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the credential instance being verified or collected as part of this task. Links to credentialing domain for licenses, certifications, background checks.']
  dependency_task_task_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to another task assignment that must be completed before this task can begin. Supports sequential task workflows and conditional logic.']
  direct_deposit_setup_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.direct_deposit_setup. Business justification: Task assignments can track direct deposit setup completion. This links the task to the direct deposit setup record and enables tracking banking setup as a task completion criterion.']
  document_collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.document_collection. Business justification: Task assignments can track collection of specific documents. This links the task to the document collection record and enables tracking document submission as a task completion criterion. The existing']
  drug_screen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.drug_screen. Business justification: Onboarding tasks track specific drug screen orders for completion verification and blocking task resolution. Critical for DOT-regulated and client-required drug screening compliance before assignment ']
  first_shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.shift. Business justification: Onboarding task completion verification requires confirming the worker completed their first scheduled shift. This is a standard gate for orientation effectiveness measurement, early-tenure quality ch']
  i9_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.i9_verification. Business justification: I-9 completion is a specific onboarding task tracked in task_assignment. The task_assignment already links to BGC, drug screen, and license verification records for similar compliance tasks. Linking t']
  license_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.license_verification. Business justification: Onboarding tasks track license verification completion as blocking requirement for healthcare and professional staffing placements. Essential for state licensure compliance and client-required verific']
  offboarding_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent offboarding record this task belongs to. Links task to the workers offboarding journey. Null if task is onboarding-related.']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent onboarding record this task belongs to. Links task to the workers onboarding journey.']
  orientation_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to candidate.orientation_session. Business justification: Task assignments can include attending specific orientation sessions. This links the task to the scheduled session and enables tracking session attendance as a task completion criterion.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (candidate) to whom this task is assigned. The individual responsible for completing the task or subject of the task.']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: Onboarding tasks are mandated by specific compliance requirements (state-required safety training, OSHA certifications, mandatory harassment training). Compliance officers configure required tasks bas']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.schedule. Business justification: Schedule setup is a distinct onboarding task in staffing — confirming the worker\'s recurring shift schedule is configured before first day. Distinct from first_shift_id (single shift occurrence); sche']
  skills_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.skills_assessment. Business justification: Onboarding tasks track skills assessment completion for role qualification verification. Critical for client-required competency validation and placement eligibility determination before assignment st']
  task_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the task checklist template from which this task assignment was instantiated. Enables traceability to standard task definitions.']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.training_record. Business justification: Onboarding tasks track training completion as placement prerequisite. Essential for mandatory safety training, client-specific orientation, and regulatory compliance training completion before assignm']
  access_granted_date date [note: 'The date when system access was successfully provisioned. Null if access has not yet been granted or task is not access-related.']
  access_revoked_date date [note: 'The date when system access was revoked during offboarding. Null if access has not been revoked or task is not access-related.']
  completed_date date [note: 'The date when the task was marked as completed. Null if task is not yet completed.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the task was marked as completed. Provides audit trail for compliance and SLA reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this task assignment record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which this task must be completed. Used for SLA tracking and escalation workflows.']
  equipment_item_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific equipment item being provisioned or returned as part of this task. Examples: laptop, phone, badge, uniform, tools.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'The current escalation level for overdue or blocked tasks. 0 = no escalation, 1 = first reminder, 2 = manager escalation, 3 = executive escalation.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The estimated time in minutes required to complete this task. Used for workload planning and worker communication.']
  external_task_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this task in the source system. Enables cross-system reconciliation and integration.']
  is_blocking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task must be completed before the worker can start their assignment. True for critical compliance tasks like I-9, background checks, drug screens.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this task is required for all workers in this onboarding/offboarding process. False for optional or conditional tasks.']
  last_reminder_sent_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent reminder notification was sent for this task. Used to control reminder frequency and escalation timing.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this task assignment record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the task assignment. Used for additional context, issues encountered, or special instructions.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The role or party responsible for completing or facilitating this task. Identifies who must take action to move the task forward. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: worker|recruiter|onboarding_coordinator|client|hr_admin|it_admin|facilities|payroll — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The date when the task becomes active and available for completion. Supports phased onboarding workflows where tasks unlock sequentially.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record where this task assignment originated. Examples: SAP SuccessFactors, Bullhorn, Manual Entry, API Integration.']
  system_access_type varchar [note: 'The type of system access being provisioned or revoked. Examples: ATS, VMS, ERP, Email, VPN, Client Portal, Timekeeping System.']
  task_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the task type. Categorizes tasks into functional groups for reporting and workflow management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: document_collection|orientation|training|equipment|compliance|credentialing|system_access|benefits_enrollment|exit_interview|knowledge_transfer — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  task_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the task requirements, instructions, and expected deliverables. Provides context and guidance for task completion.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'Short descriptive name of the task assignment. Examples: Complete I-9 Form, Submit Direct Deposit Form, Complete Safety Orientation, Return Equipment.']
  task_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level of the task assignment. Critical tasks block assignment start; high priority tasks are time-sensitive; medium and low priority tasks are standard workflow items.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the task assignment. Tracks progression from assignment through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|pending_review|completed|waived|cancelled|overdue — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  task_subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the task category. Examples: Tax Forms, Safety Training, Background Check, Laptop Return.']
  training_completion_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The score or percentage achieved by the worker on the training assessment. Null if training does not include assessment or not yet completed.']
  waived_date date [note: 'The date when this task was waived. Null if task was not waived.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why this task was waived or marked as not applicable. Null if task was not waived.']

  Note: 'Individual onboarding or offboarding task assigned to a specific worker within their onboarding_record or offboarding_record. Tracks task category (document collection, orientation, training, equipment, compliance, credentialing), responsible party (worker, recruiter, coordinator, client), due date, completion status, and blocking dependencies. Instantiated from task_checklist templates.'
}

Table recruitment.status_event {
  status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for status_event']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the placement or assignment this onboarding event is associated with. Nullable for general onboarding events not tied to a specific placement.']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: BGC order status changes (ordered, completed, adverse action initiated) generate status events in the recruitment workflow. Compliance and recruiter dashboards correlate status_event records with spec']
  candidate_screening_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.candidate_screening. Business justification: Candidate screenings have status transitions (scheduled, in_progress, completed, passed, failed, waived) that should be captured in the status_event log. Adding candidate_screening_id to status_event ']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Status events track credential instance expirations, renewals, or verifications that trigger onboarding holds or re-verification workflows. Critical for real-time onboarding status management based on']
  document_collection_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the document (I-9, tax form, direct deposit form, etc.) that this event references. Nullable for events not related to document collection.']
  drug_screen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.drug_screen. Business justification: Status events track drug screen completion, failure, or recollection requirements that change onboarding status. Essential for real-time workflow management when drug screen results arrive and impact ']
  interview_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.interview. Business justification: Interviews generate multiple status events: scheduled, confirmed by candidate, confirmed by client, rescheduled, completed, cancelled. The status_event table is the immutable event log for all such tr']
  license_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.license_verification. Business justification: License verification status changes (verified, expired, failed reverification) trigger status events affecting placement eligibility. Staffing coordinators in healthcare and professional services vert']
  offboarding_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.offboarding_record. Business justification: Status events track milestones and transitions in both onboarding and offboarding workflows. This links status events to offboarding records and enables audit trail and timeline tracking for offboardi']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent onboarding or offboarding case that this event belongs to. Groups all events for a single onboarding lifecycle.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the worker (candidate or employee) undergoing onboarding or offboarding. Links to the worker profile in the candidate or placement domain.']
  rtr_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.rtr_agreement. Business justification: RTR agreements have a defined status lifecycle (requested, acknowledged, granted, expired, revoked, conflict_flagged) that generates status events. Adding rtr_agreement_id to status_event enables comp']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: status_event explicitly carries sla_met and sla_target_timestamp — direct SLA compliance tracking fields. Linking to the governing SLA record enables SLA breach root-cause analysis, identifying which ']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.submittal. Business justification: The status_event is an immutable event log capturing every status transition and milestone event. Submittals have rich status lifecycles (submitted, viewed, interview_requested, offer_extended, accept']
  work_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: Work authorization expiration and I-9 reverification due dates trigger compliance status events. Staffing compliance teams track these events to prevent unauthorized placements. Direct FK enables even']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which communication was sent or received for this event. Set to none for status transitions that do not involve direct communication.']
  communication_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the communication was sent to the worker (outbound), received from the worker (inbound), or not applicable for non-communication events.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this event record was first inserted into the data platform. Represents the system record creation time, distinct from the business event timestamp.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'The delivery and engagement status for outbound communications. Tracks whether emails or SMS messages were successfully delivered and opened by the recipient. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sent|delivered|opened|clicked|bounced|failed|not_applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the coordinator or system about this event. Provides additional context for manual overrides, exceptions, or worker-specific details.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when this status event or communication occurred in the onboarding lifecycle. Represents the real-world business event time, not the system record creation time.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The type of event being recorded in the onboarding timeline. Discriminates between status transitions, outbound communications, inbound worker responses, system notifications, and milestone events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: status_change|outbound_email|outbound_sms|inbound_response|portal_notification|task_reminder|document_request|welcome_message|orientation_invite|bgc_status_update|offer_letter_delivery|phone_call — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  external_event_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this event in the source system (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors event ID, Bullhorn activity ID). Used for reconciliation and troubleshooting.']
  is_milestone_event boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this event represents a major milestone in the onboarding lifecycle (e.g., orientation complete, ready for assignment, assignment start). Used for high-level timeline visualization.']
  message_body_preview varchar [note: 'A preview or excerpt of the message body content for outbound or inbound communications. Truncated for storage efficiency; full content stored in document management system.']
  message_subject varchar [note: 'The subject line or title of the communication sent or received. Applicable for email and portal notifications.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The onboarding or offboarding status after this event occurred. Represents the current state following the transition or milestone. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|documents_pending|bgc_in_progress|orientation_scheduled|training_assigned|equipment_pending|ready_for_assignment|assignment_active|offboarding_initiated|offboarding_complete — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'The onboarding or offboarding status immediately before this event occurred. Nullable for the first event in a case lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|documents_pending|bgc_in_progress|orientation_scheduled|training_assigned|equipment_pending|ready_for_assignment|assignment_active|offboarding_initiated|offboarding_complete — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'The email address to which an outbound communication was sent. Applicable for email-based events.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number to which an outbound SMS or phone call was directed. Applicable for SMS and phone-based events.']
  sla_met boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the SLA target was met for this event. True if the event occurred on or before the SLA target timestamp.']
  sla_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The target date and time by which this onboarding milestone or task should be completed according to the Service Level Agreement (SLA). Used for Time to Fill (TTF) and Time to Start (TTS) measurement.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'The source system or application that generated or recorded this event. Identifies the operational system of record for traceability and integration auditing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bullhorn|sap_successfactors|sterling_bgc|docusign|kronos|internal_portal|api — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  trigger_source varchar [note: 'The specific action, system event, or integration that caused this status event to be recorded. Provides traceability for automated workflows and manual interventions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: task_completion|document_signing|bgc_clearance|lms_completion|manual_override|scheduled_reminder|api_integration|portal_submission — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  triggering_actor_type varchar [note: 'The type of actor (person or system) that triggered or initiated this event. Distinguishes between automated system actions and human-initiated actions.']
  worker_response_received boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the worker provided a response or acknowledgment for this event. True for inbound responses or completed actions.']

  Note: 'Immutable event log capturing every status transition, communication, and milestone event in the onboarding or offboarding lifecycle for a worker. Records event type (status_change, outbound_email, outbound_sms, inbound_response, portal_notification, task_reminder, document_request, welcome_message, orientation_invite, bgc_status_update, offer_letter_delivery, phone_call), prior status, new status, transition timestamp, triggering actor (system, recruiter, coordinator, worker), trigger source (task completion, document signing, BGC clearance, LMS completion, manual override), communication channel (email, SMS, portal, phone), delivery status, worker response flag, linked task or document reference, and notes. Consolidates all onboarding timeline events including outbound/inbound communications into a single unified event stream. Provides the single onboarding timeline for audit trail, SLA measurement (TTF, TTS), coordinator engagement tracking, and communication history.'
}

Table recruitment.sourcing_campaign {
  sourcing_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the sourcing campaign record. Primary key for the sourcing_campaign data product.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom this sourcing campaign is being executed. Enables client-level campaign spend and effectiveness reporting.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: The hiring manager or program contact who initiated or approved the sourcing campaign is a key operational data point for campaign management, follow-up, and NPS attribution. sourcing_campaign has cli']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns must target bill/pay rates that comply with contracted rate schedules for the client account. Real business process: rate compliance validation during campaign planning and budget a']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns for specific verticals (healthcare, security clearance) are built around a credential_package defining candidate eligibility requirements. Recruiters filter candidate pools by packa']
  headcount_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.headcount_plan. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns are initiated to fulfill approved headcount plans. MSP workforce planning links sourcing spend and activity to headcount plan budgets for variance reporting and plan utilization tra']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns are frequently geo-targeted to a specific client site (plant, office, distribution center). Site-level campaign spend tracking and headcount planning against location capacity (max_']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: In MSP/RPO engagements, sourcing campaigns are explicitly scoped to a managed program. Program managers track campaign spend and headcount against program budgets and SLA targets. This is a core MSP o']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) this sourcing campaign is designed to fill. Links campaign activity to the originating staffing demand.']
  program_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.program_supplier. Business justification: In managed programs, sourcing campaigns are executed by specific program-authorized suppliers. The program_supplier record governs the supplier\'s authorization tier, fill rate SLAs, and markup caps th']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns have target_fill_date, target_submittal_count, and target_headcount — all SLA-governed metrics. Linking to the governing SLA enables automated campaign SLA compliance reporting, ale']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: RPO and MSP sourcing campaigns are explicitly scoped under a Statement of Work defining deliverables, headcount targets, and pricing model. Program managers require this link to track campaign actual_']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Multi-state sourcing campaigns must comply with jurisdiction-specific rules (pay transparency posting laws, ban-the-box, salary history bans). Staffing firms configure campaigns per applicable state r']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns in MSP/VMS programs partner with supplier networks to source candidates. Essential for tracking supplier-driven sourcing initiatives, budget allocation, and campaign performance att']
  talent_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to candidate.talent_pool. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns are routinely executed against specific talent pools (redeployment pools, bench pools, client-specific pools). Staffing operations teams track pool depletion rates, sourcing ROI, an']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns executed through a supplier are governed by the vendor agreement defining fee structures, diversity requirements, and channel restrictions. Procurement and finance teams require thi']
  vendor_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Sourcing campaigns set pay_rate_min/max and bill_rate_target directly from the supplier\'s negotiated rate card. MSP/VMS program managers reference the vendor rate card when budgeting campaigns. No exi']
  vms_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vms_enrollment. Business justification: VMS sourcing campaigns are restricted to suppliers with active VMS enrollment; enrollment status (approved job categories, approved work locations) directly constrains campaign eligibility. sourcing_c']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the VMS program under which this campaign is being executed when operating in an MSP/VMS delivery model. Links campaign to Beeline VMS program configuration and rate management.']
  actual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount actually spent on this sourcing campaign to date in USD. Compared against budget_amount to compute budget utilization and variance for financial reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or director who authorized the launch of this sourcing campaign and its associated budget. Supports governance and audit trail requirements.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the sourcing campaign and its budget were formally approved by the designated authority. Required for financial governance and audit trail.']
  ats_campaign_ref varchar [note: 'The native campaign identifier or reference number from the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system of record. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail between the lakehouse and the operational ATS.']
  bill_rate_target decimal(10,2) [note: 'The target bill rate per hour to be charged to the client for placements resulting from this campaign. Used for gross margin and spread analysis (Bill Rate minus Pay Rate).']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved monetary budget allocated to this sourcing campaign in USD. Covers job board postings, advertising spend, referral bonuses, and agency fees. Used for cost-per-hire and ROI analysis.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the campaign across systems (e.g., SRC-2024-IT-CHI-001). Used for cross-system reconciliation and billing attribution.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the campaign objectives, sourcing strategy, target candidate profile, and any special instructions for recruiters executing the campaign.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name assigned to the sourcing campaign (e.g., Q3 2024 IT Contract Sourcing — Chicago). Used for identification in ATS dashboards and reporting.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sourcing campaign by channel or delivery model. Drives channel effectiveness analysis and cost-per-hire attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|social_media|referral|rpo|msp|direct_outreach|internal_database — promote to reference product]']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of sourcing channels activated in this campaign (e.g., LinkedIn, Indeed, ZipRecruiter, Internal ATS, Employee Referral). Supports channel effectiveness and cost-per-source analysis.']
  clearance_level_required varchar [note: 'The minimum government security clearance level required for candidates sourced through this campaign. Only applicable when is_security_clearance_required is true.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this sourcing campaign record was first created in the system. Audit trail field conforming to SOC 2 Type II record creation logging requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this campaign record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign is executed under a standard staffing model, Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO), Managed Service Provider (MSP), or a hybrid arrangement. Affects billing, SLA, and reporting structures.']
  diversity_sourcing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this campaign includes targeted diversity sourcing initiatives (e.g., HBCU outreach, veteran hiring, disability inclusion). Supports EEOC and OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'The type of employment arrangement being sourced: contract (temporary W-2 or 1099), temp-to-perm (contract-to-hire), direct hire (permanent placement), per diem, or Statement of Work (SOW). Determines compliance, billing, and onboarding workflows.']
  end_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date on which the sourcing campaign closes. Nullable for open-ended campaigns. Used for campaign duration reporting and budget burn-rate analysis.']
  experience_level varchar [note: 'The seniority or experience level of candidates targeted by this campaign. Used to calibrate sourcing channels, compensation ranges, and screening criteria.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic area targeted by this campaign (e.g., Chicago Metro, Remote — USA, Northeast Region). Used for sourcing strategy planning and labor market analysis.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The industry sector or vertical being targeted by this campaign (e.g., Healthcare, Information Technology, Finance, Manufacturing). Supports vertical-specific sourcing strategy and market analytics.']
  is_remote_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the positions being sourced in this campaign are eligible for fully remote work arrangements. Expands the candidate pool beyond the defined geographic scope when true.']
  is_security_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether candidates sourced through this campaign must hold an active government security clearance. Restricts the eligible candidate pool and triggers specialized credentialing workflows.']
  pay_rate_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum pay rate (per hour or annual salary) offered to candidates sourced through this campaign. Defines the upper bound for offer negotiations and budget planning.']
  pay_rate_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum pay rate (per hour or annual salary) offered to candidates sourced through this campaign. Used for candidate targeting, offer management, and spread/margin analysis.']
  pay_rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the pay rate range is expressed as an hourly, annual (salary), or daily rate. Required for accurate compensation benchmarking and FLSA classification.']
  primary_channel varchar [note: 'The single dominant sourcing channel expected to generate the highest candidate volume for this campaign. Used for channel ROI benchmarking and budget allocation decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linkedin|indeed|ziprecruiter|internal_ats|referral|dice|monster|careerbuilder — promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this sourcing campaign, reflecting urgency relative to client SLAs and fill deadlines. Drives recruiter task prioritization and resource allocation.']
  requisition_count varchar [note: 'The number of open requisitions (reqs) this campaign is designed to address. A single campaign may target multiple related job orders, particularly in high-volume or RPO engagements.']
  sourcing_campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sourcing campaign. Drives operational workflows: draft (being configured), active (live sourcing), paused (temporarily halted), completed (target met or end date reached), cancelled (terminated before completion).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the sourcing campaign officially launches and begins generating candidate activity. Used for TTF (Time to Fill) and campaign duration calculations.']
  target_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary country where candidates are being sourced (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Drives compliance requirements including I-9, E-Verify, and GDPR applicability.']
  target_fill_date date [note: 'The business-committed date by which the associated job order(s) must be filled. Drives urgency scoring and SLA compliance tracking for TTF (Time to Fill) KPI measurement.']
  target_headcount varchar [note: 'The number of positions (headcount) this sourcing campaign is designed to fill. Drives submittal volume targets and sourcing capacity planning.']
  target_job_title varchar [note: 'The primary job title or role designation being sourced in this campaign (e.g., Senior Java Developer, Registered Nurse). Used for job board posting titles and candidate matching.']
  target_skill_set varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list or free-text description of the primary skills, certifications, or competencies this campaign is sourcing for (e.g., Java, AWS, Agile, PMP). Drives Boolean search strings and job board targeting.']
  target_state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state or Canadian province code for the primary sourcing geography (e.g., IL, NY, CA). Used for SUTA, workers comp, and state-specific labor law compliance.']
  target_submittal_count varchar [note: 'The planned number of qualified candidate submittals this campaign aims to generate. Used to set QoS (Quality of Submission) benchmarks and recruiter productivity targets.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this sourcing campaign record was most recently modified. Used for change detection, incremental data loads, and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for talent sourcing campaigns initiated to fill job orders. Captures campaign name, type (job board, social media, referral, RPO, MSP), target skill sets, geographic scope, budget, start/end dates, channel mix, campaign owner, associated requisition targets, and campaign status. Supports sourcing strategy planning and channel effectiveness analysis.'
}

Table recruitment.job_posting {
  job_posting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the job posting record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom this job is being posted. Supports client-level reporting on posting activity and fill rates.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Job postings advertise pay/bill rates that must match contracted rate schedules for compliance. Real business process: posting approval workflow validates rate compliance before publication to prevent']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Job postings communicate the full credential package required for the role to candidates and sourcing channels. ATS and VMS systems use the credential_package to auto-screen applicants. Staffing exper']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Job postings are classified by job category for EEOC reporting, job board taxonomy, and market rate benchmarking. job_posting has eeoc_job_category as a denormalized text field. Normalizing via job_ca']
  labor_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.labor_category. Business justification: Job postings define the labor category governing bill rate ranges, FLSA classification, and workers\' comp codes for the role. Staffing rate governance requires this link to validate posted rates again']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Job postings require specific client site details (parking instructions, badge requirements, dress code, site contact) for candidate preparation and onboarding. Location-specific attributes are critic']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: In MSP programs, job postings are issued under a specific managed program governing BGC requirements, drug screen panels, markup caps, and supplier routing rules. Program spend tracking and SLA compli']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent job order requisition (Req) from which this posting was derived. Links the published advertisement back to the originating client requisition.']
  order_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_location. Business justification: Job postings advertise work at a specific order work site (order_location). job_posting carries denormalized city/state_province/postal_code/country_code from order_location. Normalizing via FK enable']
  position_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.position_requirement. Business justification: Job postings are the external advertisement of a specific position_requirement slot. Recruiters and MSP managers trace postings back to position_requirements for fill-rate reporting, max-submittal enf']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: OFCCP federal contractor posting requirements and state pay transparency mandates require specific job posting content. Staffing firms must track which compliance requirement each posting satisfies fo']
  sourcing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.sourcing_campaign. Business justification: A job posting is derived from and driven by a sourcing campaign. In staffing, a sourcing_campaign is initiated to fill job orders and generates one or more job postings across channels. Adding sourcin']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Job postings must comply with state-specific rules: pay transparency salary range disclosure, ban-the-box language, and EEO statement requirements vary by jurisdiction. Staffing firms posting across s']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Job postings distributed to supplier networks in VMS programs for candidate submissions. Core MSP workflow enabling supplier allocation enforcement, submission tracking, and first-look period manageme']
  vendor_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Job postings in VMS/MSP programs populate bill_rate_min/max and pay_rate_min/max from the supplier\'s vendor rate card. Rate compliance validation at posting creation requires this direct reference. Di']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: In VMS-driven programs, job postings are issued through the VMS platform under a specific program governing markup caps, submission limits, and routing rules. VMS fee reconciliation (vms_fee_pct) and ']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Job postings must comply with FLSA exempt/non-exempt classification, prevailing wage requirements (Davis-Bacon, SCA), and state minimum wage laws. This link ensures posted pay rates align with wage-ho']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Anticipated end date of the temporary or contract assignment being advertised. Null for permanent direct placement roles. Used for workforce planning, redeployment scheduling, and bench management.']
  bgc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Background Check (BGC) is a mandatory credentialing requirement for candidates applying to this posting. Triggers BGC order workflow in Sterling Background Check upon candidate selection.']
  bill_rate_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum bill rate range for this posting, representing the upper bound of the rate charged to the client. Used for internal pricing analysis, Spread calculation, and Gross Margin forecasting. Not disclosed to candidates.']
  bill_rate_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum bill rate range for this posting, representing the lower bound of the rate charged to the client. Used for internal pricing analysis, Spread calculation, and Gross Margin forecasting. Not disclosed to candidates.']
  bullhorn_posting_ref varchar [note: 'External reference identifier assigned by Bullhorn ATS/CRM to this job posting. Used for cross-system reconciliation and deep-link navigation into the source system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this job posting record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail anchor for the posting lifecycle and supports SLA measurement from requisition receipt to posting publication.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary rate fields on this posting (pay_rate_min, pay_rate_max, bill_rate_min, bill_rate_max). Defaults to USD for domestic U.S. operations.']
  drug_screen_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-placement drug screening is required for candidates applying to this posting. Triggers drug screen order workflow in Sterling Background Check upon candidate selection.']
  education_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum educational attainment level required for the role. Used for candidate screening and EEOC-compliant job requirement documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|professional_certification — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the engagement model being advertised. Drives candidate targeting, pay/bill rate structures, and compliance requirements. Includes Temp, Contract, Contract-to-Hire (CTH), Direct Placement, Temp-to-Perm, Per Diem, and Statement of Work (SOW). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temporary|contract|contract_to_hire|direct_placement|temp_to_perm|per_diem|statement_of_work — promote to reference product]']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the job posting is scheduled to expire and be automatically removed from active distribution channels. Null indicates an open-ended posting with no scheduled expiry.']
  filled_count varchar [note: 'Number of openings that have been successfully filled with a placed candidate as of the current record state. Compared against openings_count to determine remaining unfilled positions.']
  filled_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the job posting was marked as filled following a successful candidate placement. Used to calculate Time-to-Fill (TTF) as the difference between posting_date and filled_date.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the role is classified as FLSA-exempt (salaried, not eligible for overtime) or non-exempt (eligible for overtime pay). Critical for payroll compliance, Overtime (OT) and Double Time (DT) calculations, and DOL audit readiness.']
  job_description varchar [note: 'Full externally-published job description text including responsibilities, qualifications, and role overview. This is the candidate-facing content distributed across all posting channels.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this job posting record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  min_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for the role. Used as a candidate screening threshold in the Applicant Tracking System (ATS).']
  msp_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job posting is managed through a Managed Service Provider (MSP) contingent workforce program. Affects supplier distribution, VMS workflow, and program-level SLA compliance.']
  openings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of positions available under this job posting. A single posting may represent multiple headcount openings for the same role. Used for Headcount planning and fill-rate tracking.']
  pay_rate_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum pay rate offered to the candidate for this role, expressed in the local currency per hour (for hourly roles) or annual salary (for direct placement). Represents the upper bound of the Pay Rate range disclosed in the posting.']
  pay_rate_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum pay rate offered to the candidate for this role, expressed in the local currency per hour (for hourly roles) or annual salary (for direct placement). Represents the lower bound of the Pay Rate range disclosed in the posting.']
  pay_rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the pay frequency basis for the advertised pay rate (e.g., hourly for temporary/contract workers, annual for direct placement salaried roles). Determines how pay_rate_min and pay_rate_max values are interpreted.']
  posting_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel through which this job posting is published. Supports multi-channel job distribution analytics and sourcing effectiveness measurement. Includes job boards (Indeed, LinkedIn), career site, Vendor Management System (VMS), social media, internal postings, referral programs, and email campaigns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|career_site|vms|social_media|internal|referral|email_campaign — promote to reference product]']
  posting_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the job posting was first published and made visible to candidates. Used as the start anchor for Time-to-Fill (TTF) and Time-to-Start (TTS) KPI calculations.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the job posting. Controls visibility and candidate application eligibility. filled indicates a successful placement; paused indicates temporarily suspended distribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|active|paused|closed|filled|expired|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  posting_title varchar [note: 'The externally-visible job title displayed on job boards, career sites, and VMS portals. May differ from the internal job order title to optimize for candidate search discoverability.']
  preferred_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited or free-text list of desired but non-mandatory skills, technologies, or competencies. Used to differentiate candidates during submittal ranking and Quality of Submission (QoS) evaluation.']
  required_skills varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited or free-text list of mandatory skills, technologies, certifications, or competencies required for the role. Used for candidate matching, ATS keyword search, and Quality of Submission (QoS) scoring.']
  rpo_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job posting is being fulfilled under a Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) delivery model. Drives program-specific SLA tracking, reporting, and billing rules.']
  security_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role requires the candidate to hold an active government security clearance. Used for candidate eligibility filtering and OFCCP/federal contractor compliance.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'The work shift schedule associated with the posted role. Used for candidate matching, scheduling, and Kronos Workforce Ready integration for shift-based workforce dispatch. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: day|evening|night|rotating|weekend|flexible|on_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_start_date date [note: 'The clients desired date for the placed candidate to begin the assignment or employment. Used to calculate Time-to-Start (TTS) KPI and to prioritize urgent requisitions in the sourcing queue.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition number assigned by the clients Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this job posting. Required for MSP/VMS-managed programs to reconcile postings with client-side requisition tracking.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the role requires the worker to be physically present at a client site (onsite), work fully remotely, or follow a hybrid schedule. Critical for candidate targeting and geographic sourcing strategy.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates the tax and employment classification of the worker to be placed: W-2 employee (payrolled through staffing firm), 1099 Independent Contractor (IC), or Corp-to-Corp (C2C). Drives payroll tax treatment, co-employment risk assessment, and IRS compliance.']

  Note: 'Represents a published job advertisement derived from a job order requisition. Tracks posting title, description, required skills, location, employment type (temp, contract-to-hire, direct placement), pay range, bill rate range, posting channel (job board, career site, VMS, social), posting date, expiration date, posting status (active, paused, closed, filled), and ATS reference from Bullhorn. Supports multi-channel job distribution and posting lifecycle management.'
}

Business justification: Submittals must be delivered within SLA commitment windows (submittal_sla_hours, submission_window_close_date). Direct FK enables real-time SLA compliance tracking per submittal, penalty breach calcul']
  sow_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.sow_agreement. Business justification: SOW-type submittals are governed by a specific vendor SOW agreement defining deliverables, pricing, and worker classification. Submittal has sow_id→contract.sow but not the vendor-side sow_agreement. ']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Submittals fill specific SOW-defined positions with defined scope, rates, headcount, and acceptance criteria. Real business process: SOW fulfillment tracking, budget consumption monitoring, and delive']
  submittal_order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) for which the candidate is being submitted. Links this submittal to the job order that originated the talent need.']
  submittal_req_order_header_id varchar [note: 'The client-assigned requisition number associated with the job order for this submittal. Used in VMS and client procurement systems to cross-reference the staffing firms job order with the clients internal requisition tracking.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Submittals originate from suppliers in VMS/MSP programs. Critical for supplier performance scorecards (QOS, time-to-submit), tier management, allocation compliance, and invoice reconciliation. Core VM']

Tracking which vendor contact submitted a candidate is essential for supplier relationship management, SLA accountability, and ']

  bullhorn_submittal_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by Bullhorn ATS/CRM to this submittal record. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational ATS source system.']
  client_feedback_date date [note: 'The date on which the client provided formal feedback (shortlist, rejection, interview request) on this submittal. Used to measure client response time SLA compliance and pipeline velocity.']
  client_feedback_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing the clients qualitative feedback on the submitted candidate. May include reasons for rejection, interest level, or specific concerns. Used for recruiter coaching and future submittal quality improvement.']
  client_viewed_date date [note: 'The date on which the client contact first viewed or opened the candidate submittal. Tracked via VMS or ATS portal activity. Used to measure client responsiveness and calculate submittal-to-feedback cycle time.']
  converted_to_placement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submittal resulted in an active placement or assignment. True when the candidate was hired and a placement record was created. Key metric for submittal-to-hire conversion ratio reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this submittal record was first created in the system. Distinct from submittal_date which captures the business event time. Used for audit trail and data lineage. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'The recruitment delivery model under which this submittal was made. Indicates whether the submittal was made through direct staffing, Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO), Managed Service Provider (MSP), or Vendor Management System (VMS) channel. Affects billing, compliance, and SLA requirements.']
  eeoc_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EEOC-required equal opportunity disclosures were provided to the candidate prior to submittal. Supports compliance with EEOC regulations and OFCCP requirements for federal contractor clients.']
  interview_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the client interview for this submittal. Indicates whether the candidate passed, failed, is pending feedback, did not show, or needs rescheduling. Drives next-step actions in the recruitment workflow.']
  interview_requested_date date [note: 'The date on which the client formally requested an interview with the submitted candidate. Marks the transition from submittal review to active interview stage in the recruitment pipeline.']
  interview_scheduled_date date [note: 'The date on which the interview between the client and the submitted candidate is scheduled to take place. Used for pipeline management, recruiter follow-up scheduling, and TTF milestone tracking.']
  interview_type varchar [note: 'The format or modality of the interview requested by the client for this submittal (e.g., phone screen, video, onsite, panel, technical). Used for scheduling coordination and interview outcome tracking.']
  is_backfill boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submittal is for a backfill position — replacing a worker who has left or been removed from an assignment. Backfill submittals are tracked separately for workforce planning and client relationship analytics.']
  is_redeployment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submitted candidate is being redeployed from a previous or ending assignment within the same client or another client. Redeployment tracking is a key metric for bench utilization and workforce retention.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the recruiter at the time of submittal. May include candidate highlights, match rationale, availability details, or special considerations communicated to the client contact.']
  offer_accepted_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate formally accepted the offer for this submittal. Marks the conversion of a submittal to a placement. Used for submittal-to-hire ratio and TTS calculations.']
  offer_extended_date date [note: 'The date on which a formal employment or engagement offer was extended to the candidate following a successful interview. Used for offer-to-acceptance cycle time measurement and TTF milestone tracking.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intended engagement type for this submittal. Distinguishes between temporary staffing, contract-to-hire (CTH), direct placement, temp-to-perm, and Statement of Work (SOW) engagements. Drives billing, compliance, and conversion fee logic.']
  proposed_bill_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The hourly or periodic bill rate proposed to the client at the time of submittal. Represents the rate charged to the client for the candidates services. Used in spread and gross margin calculations. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  proposed_pay_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The hourly or periodic pay rate proposed for the candidate at the time of submittal. Represents the compensation offered to the worker. Used in spread and gross margin calculations. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  qos_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes accompanying the QoS rating, providing qualitative context on why the candidate was rated at that quality level. May include match strengths, gaps, or client-specific considerations.']
  qos_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numeric quality score assigned to this submittal reflecting how well the candidate profile matches the job order requirements. Scored on a defined scale (e.g., 1.0–5.0). Used as a KPI for recruiter performance, submittal quality benchmarking, and client satisfaction analysis.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to the proposed pay rate and bill rate on this submittal (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the pay and bill rate period basis for this submittal (e.g., hourly, daily, weekly, annual, per diem). Determines how proposed_pay_rate and proposed_bill_rate are interpreted for compensation and billing calculations.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason a submittal was rejected by the client or withdrawn by the candidate. Used for pipeline analytics, fall-off rate analysis, and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: overqualified|underqualified|rate_mismatch|availability|culture_fit|position_filled|no_response|candidate_withdrew — promote to reference product]']
  rtr_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate granted Right to Represent (RTR) authorization to the staffing firm for this submittal. Used for compliance tracking and dispute resolution in cases of duplicate representation claims.']
  rtr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the candidate has granted the staffing firm the Right to Represent (RTR) them for this specific job order and client. A True value confirms the candidate has authorized the submittal, preventing duplicate representation conflicts.']
  rtr_method varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the candidate provided Right to Represent (RTR) consent (e.g., email, phone, text, verbal, self-service portal). Supports compliance documentation and audit trail for RTR disputes.']
  sla_response_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the client is contractually required to respond to this submittal per the Master Service Agreement (MSA) or VMS program SLA. Used to flag overdue submittals and measure client SLA compliance.']
  submittal_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time the candidate profile was formally submitted to the client contact for the specified job order. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp. Used as the start point for Time-to-Fill (TTF) and Time-to-Start (TTS) KPI calculations. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  submittal_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-visible business identifier for this submittal (e.g., SUB-2024-00123). Used in client communications, ATS references, and operational reporting. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  submittal_source varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the candidate submittal was delivered to the client (e.g., ATS portal, VMS portal, email, phone, direct). Used for channel effectiveness analysis and process standardization.']
  submittal_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the candidate submittal in the recruitment pipeline. Tracks progression from initial submission through client review to hiring decision. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|viewed|shortlisted|interview_requested|rejected|offer_extended|hired|withdrawn — promote to reference product if additional states are needed]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this submittal record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any field in the submittal record for audit and change management purposes. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category per TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  vms_submittal_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the clients Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, to this submittal. Required for MSP/VMS-managed programs to reconcile submittal records between the staffing firms ATS and the clients VMS platform.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record representing a candidate submittal (submission) to a client for a specific job order. Captures submittal date, submitting recruiter, candidate profile reference, job order reference, client contact submitted to, submittal status (submitted, viewed, shortlisted, rejected, interview requested), right-to-represent (RTR) flag, RTR date, QoS (Quality of Submission) rating, pay rate proposed, bill rate proposed, and Bullhorn ATS submittal ID. Central entity for tracking recruiter activity and submittal-to-hire conversion ratios.'
}

Table recruitment.rtr_agreement {
  rtr_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the Right-to-Represent (RTR) agreement record. Primary key for this entity.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom the candidate is being represented. Enables RTR conflict detection across multiple job orders at the same client.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: RTR agreements reference parent MSA terms for non-solicitation periods, representation scope, and conflict resolution. Real business process: RTR conflict checking against MSA restrictions and client-']
  credential_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: RTR agreements define the scope of candidate representation including credential compliance obligations. The credential_requirement specifies what the candidate must satisfy under the RTR. Staffing co']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: In managed programs, RTR agreements are governed by program-level supplier tier rules, exclusivity policies, and submission limits. Program-level RTR compliance auditing and supplier performance track']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific job order (requisition) for which the RTR agreement is granted. Ties the representation right to a specific client opening.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate for whom the Right-to-Represent (RTR) agreement is being established. Links to the candidate master record.']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: RTR agreements in specific states carry distinct legal requirements — California AB5, Massachusetts ABC test, and Illinois staffing regulations impose specific representation obligations. Staffing fir']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Right-to-represent agreements track which supplier has exclusive representation rights for a candidate on a requisition. Essential for conflict resolution, duplicate submittal prevention, and supplier']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: The RTR agreement grants a supplier the right to represent a candidate under the terms of their vendor agreement, which defines exclusivity scope, non-solicitation clauses, and representation rights. ']
  vendor_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_contact. Business justification: RTR agreements are negotiated with and acknowledged by a specific vendor contact; tracking the vendor contact enables dispute resolution when multiple agencies claim representation rights. rtr_agreeme']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: In VMS programs, RTR agreements are submitted through the VMS platform and governed by program-specific supplier authorization rules and exclusivity policies. VMS program RTR compliance tracking and s']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: RTR agreements must specify the worker classification under which the candidate is represented. IC vs W2 classification materially affects RTR terms, exclusivity obligations, and legal exposure. Staff']
  acknowledgment_method varchar [note: 'The channel or method by which the candidate acknowledged and granted the RTR. Supports audit and legal defensibility. Values: email = email reply confirmation; portal = candidate self-service portal; verbal = phone/in-person verbal consent (requires recruiter attestation); sms = text message confirmation; signed_document = formal signed RTR form.']
  acknowledgment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the candidate formally acknowledged and granted the RTR agreement. Distinct from the request timestamp; captures the moment consent was received.']
  bullhorn_rtr_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier for this RTR agreement as stored in Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Used for system-of-record cross-referencing, data lineage, and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational ATS.']
  candidate_consent_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the stored consent document (e.g., signed RTR form, email thread ID, DocuSign envelope ID) that evidences the candidates acknowledgment. Supports FCRA and GDPR audit requirements.']
  conflict_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the competing staffing agency that has also claimed representation rights for the same candidate-client pairing, triggering a co-representation conflict. Populated only when conflict_flag is True.']
  conflict_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a co-representation conflict has been detected for this RTR agreement — i.e., another agency has also claimed the right to represent the same candidate to the same client or job order.']
  conflict_resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the co-representation conflict was formally resolved. Populated only when conflict_resolution_status is not unresolved.']
  conflict_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the co-representation conflict resolution process. unresolved = conflict is open; resolved_in_favor = this firms RTR was upheld; resolved_against = competing agencys RTR was upheld; withdrawn = one party withdrew the claim.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RTR agreement record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  delivery_model varchar [note: 'The recruitment delivery model under which this RTR agreement was established. direct = standard staffing firm direct placement; rpo = Recruitment Process Outsourcing (RPO) engagement; msp = Managed Service Provider (MSP) program; vms = Vendor Management System (VMS) submitted requisition.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the RTR agreement expires if no placement or extension has occurred. After this date, the staffing firm loses exclusive or non-exclusive representation rights for this candidate-client pairing.']
  extension_count varchar [note: 'The number of times this RTR agreement has been extended beyond its original expiration date. Tracks repeated extensions that may indicate slow client decision-making or candidate availability issues.']
  grant_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the RTR agreement was formally granted and the staffing firms right to represent the candidate to the specified client or job order became effective.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RTR agreement grants exclusive representation rights to this staffing firm for the specified candidate-client pairing. True = exclusive (candidate cannot be submitted by another agency); False = non-exclusive.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The job title or position title associated with the job order for which the RTR agreement is established. Captured at the time of RTR creation to preserve historical context even if the job order title changes.']
  last_extension_date date [note: 'The most recent date on which the RTR agreement expiration was extended. Null if no extensions have been granted.']
  nda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client requires the candidate to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) as a condition of representation or submission. True = NDA required before submittal.']
  non_compete_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non-compete agreement review is required for this candidate before representation can proceed. True = recruiter must verify no active non-compete conflicts exist with the target client.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recruiter or account manager notes related to the RTR agreement, such as special client instructions, candidate preferences, or conflict resolution details.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The type of placement this RTR agreement is intended to support. contract = temporary/contingent assignment; temp_to_perm = temporary with conversion option; direct_hire = permanent direct placement; contract_to_hire = contract with defined conversion path.']
  representation_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of the representation right. job_order = limited to a single requisition; client = covers all open roles at the client account; client_division = covers all roles within a specific client division or business unit.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the RTR request was initiated by the recruiter and sent to the candidate for acknowledgment. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for this agreement.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the RTR agreement was formally revoked. Populated only when rtr_status is revoked.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for revoking the RTR agreement. Populated only when rtr_status is revoked. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: candidate_request|firm_decision|conflict_lost|placement_made|job_order_closed|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  rtr_duration_days varchar [note: 'The agreed-upon duration of the RTR agreement in calendar days, measured from grant_date to expiration_date. Standard industry practice ranges from 30 to 90 days. Used for SLA monitoring and TTF (Time-to-Fill) analytics.']
  rtr_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the RTR agreement, used in recruiter communications, ATS tracking, and dispute resolution. Format: RTR-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  rtr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the RTR agreement. pending = awaiting candidate acknowledgment; granted = candidate has confirmed representation rights; expired = past expiration date without placement; revoked = candidate or firm withdrew consent; disputed = co-representation conflict under review.']
  sourcing_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the candidate was originally sourced before the RTR was established. Used for sourcing effectiveness analytics and QoS (Quality of Submission) reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RTR agreement record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance.']
  vms_req_number varchar [note: 'The requisition or job order number as assigned by the clients Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline. Used to cross-reference the RTR agreement with the VMS-managed program when delivery_model is vms or msp.']

  Note: 'Tracks Right-to-Represent (RTR) agreements between a staffing firm and a candidate for a specific client or job order. Records RTR grant date, expiration date, RTR status (pending, granted, expired, revoked), candidate acknowledgment method (email, portal, verbal), exclusivity flag, and any RTR conflicts with competing agencies. Ensures legal protection of candidate representation rights and prevents co-representation disputes.'
}

Table recruitment.interview {
  interview_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for interview']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: Interviews at certain stages trigger background check orders. Links interview record to the BGC it initiated, supporting workflow automation and tracking which interview round required screening. Comm']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Interviews for licensed or cleared roles are only scheduled after credential verification is confirmed. The interview record must reference the credential_instance that cleared the candidate for inter']
  license_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.license_verification. Business justification: For licensed professional roles (RN, PE, CPA), interviews are scheduled only after license verification is confirmed. The interview record references the specific license_verification that cleared the']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Interviews at client sites require location-specific logistics: site address, parking instructions, badge procedures, site contact details, and security requirements. Essential for candidate coordinat']
  nda_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.nda. Business justification: Interviews for sensitive client roles require executed NDAs before candidate disclosure of confidential information. Real business process: compliance gate in interview scheduling workflow to prevent ']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) for which this interview is being conducted. Supports Time-to-Fill (TTF) and Time-to-Start (TTS) KPI calculations and links interview activity to open requisitions.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate profile record for the individual being interviewed. Core party reference enabling candidate-level interview history, fall-off rate tracking, and redeployment analytics.']
  req_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.req_pipeline. Business justification: An interview event is a stage within the req_pipeline progression. Linking interview to req_pipeline_id allows direct association between the interview event and the pipeline record tracking the candi']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Interview processes must comply with state-specific rules prohibiting salary history questions, restricting criminal history inquiries, and requiring specific disclosures. Staffing firms conducting in']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate submittal record that triggered this interview. Links the interview back to the specific submission of a candidate for a job order, enabling submittal-to-hire ratio analytics and Quality of Submission (QoS) scoring.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Interview scheduling tracks which supplier submitted the candidate. Required for supplier performance metrics (submittal-to-interview ratio), scorecard calculation, and coordinating interview logistic']
  vendor_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_contact. Business justification: In VMS/MSP programs, the vendor contact coordinates interview scheduling, candidate confirmation, and feedback collection. interview has supplier_id but no vendor_contact_id. Staffing coordinators nee']
  work_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: Work authorization must be confirmed before scheduling interviews for visa-restricted or security-clearance roles. The interview record references the work_authorization confirming eligibility. Compli']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the interview commenced. Compared against scheduled_start_timestamp to measure punctuality and scheduling adherence. Null if the interview was cancelled or the candidate was a no-show.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for interview cancellation. Populated when interview_status is cancelled. Supports fall-off rate analysis and process improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: candidate_withdrew|client_cancelled|position_filled|budget_freeze|no_show|duplicate|other — promote to reference product]']
  candidate_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether the candidate has confirmed attendance for the scheduled interview. Critical for no-show risk management, coordinator follow-up workflows, and fall-off rate analysis.']
  candidate_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the candidate confirmed their attendance for the interview. Used to measure confirmation lead time and coordinator responsiveness metrics.']
  client_confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Tracks whether the client-side interviewer(s) have confirmed availability for the scheduled interview. Supports SLA measurement and client engagement analytics.']
  communication_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Numeric rating for the candidates communication skills as assessed during the interview, typically on a scale of 1.0 to 5.0. Key competency dimension for client satisfaction and placement quality analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interview record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data governance, compliance, and Silver layer lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
