  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or virtual work location where the SOW engagement is performed.']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: SOW/independent contractor engagements require worker classification determination to assess co-employment risk and ensure proper W2 vs 1099 classification. Critical for IRS/DOL compliance and misclas']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date the SOW engagement concluded, which may differ from the contractual engagement_end_date due to early termination, extension, or scope completion. Null if engagement is still active.']
  bgc_status varchar [note: 'Status of the background check (BGC) requirement for the worker on this SOW engagement. Sourced from Sterling Background Check system. Used for compliance credentialing and onboarding verification.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly or unit bill rate charged to the client for the worker under this SOW engagement, where applicable (primarily for time-and-materials SOW types). Used for spread and gross margin calculations.']
  co_employment_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed co-employment risk level for this SOW engagement based on the degree of client control over the worker. Used by compliance teams to monitor joint employment exposure. low = minimal client direction; medium = some client oversight; high = significant client control; critical = requires immediate legal review.']
  co_employment_risk_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from the compliance team documenting the basis for the co-employment risk assessment, specific risk factors identified, and any mitigation actions taken for this SOW engagement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SOW engagement record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this transaction. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this SOW engagement record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Completes the MONETARY_TRIPLET alongside total_sow_value and invoiced_amount.']
  deliverable_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the primary deliverables, outcomes, or scope of work defined in the SOW. Captures the key work products the worker or vendor is contracted to produce. Sourced from the executed SOW document in DocuSign CLM.']
  department varchar [note: 'The client department or business unit that the SOW engagement serves. Used for client-side cost allocation, workforce planning analytics, and headcount reporting.']
  engagement_end_date date [note: 'The contractually agreed date on which the SOW engagement is scheduled to conclude. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this placement record. Nullable for open-ended engagements. Used for redeployment planning and assignment lifecycle management.']
  engagement_start_date date [note: 'The contractually agreed date on which the SOW engagement commences. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_FROM and BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this placement record. Used for assignment tracking, billing period calculation, and compliance reporting.']
  engagement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SOW engagement. Tracks the operational state from initial drafting through completion or termination. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this transaction. draft = not yet executed; active = currently in progress; completed = deliverables fulfilled; terminated = ended before completion; on_hold = temporarily paused.']
  engagement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SOW engagement by billing and delivery structure. fixed_price = total price agreed upfront for defined deliverables; time_and_materials = billed on actual hours and costs; milestone_based = payments tied to deliverable milestones; retainer = recurring fixed fee for ongoing availability.']
  extension_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this SOW engagement has been extended beyond its original engagement_end_date. Used for assignment lifecycle analytics, client relationship management, and redeployment planning.']
  fall_off_date date [note: 'The date on which the fall-off event occurred for this SOW engagement. Populated only when fall_off_flag is True. Used for fall-off rate calculation and placement quality reporting.']
  fall_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOW engagement experienced a fall-off event — where the worker departed or the engagement was terminated prematurely before the contracted end date. Used for fall-off rate KPI monitoring and recruiter performance analytics.']
  fall_off_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for the fall-off event on this SOW engagement. Used for root cause analysis and placement quality improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: candidate_withdrew|client_terminated|performance|scope_change|budget_cut|other — promote to reference product if additional values needed]']
  i9_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workers I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification has been completed and verified for this SOW engagement. Mandatory for all U.S.-based workers under the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA).']
  ic_classification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker engaged under this SOW is classified as an Independent Contractor (IC/1099) rather than a W-2 employee. Critical for IRS worker classification compliance, co-employment risk assessment, and FLSA determination.']
  invoiced_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount invoiced to the client against this SOW engagement to date. Represents the adjustment/progress component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Used for billing reconciliation and revenue tracking in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The job title or role designation of the worker engaged under this SOW. Used for workforce analytics, EEOC reporting, and assignment tracking. May differ from the job order title if the SOW specifies a distinct deliverable-based role.']
  milestone_billing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOW engagement uses milestone-based billing tied to deliverable completion events rather than time-and-materials or fixed periodic billing. When True, billing events are triggered by milestone approval in Beeline VMS or Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the worker onboarding process for this SOW engagement. Tracks completion of required onboarding steps including I-9 verification, background check, and client-specific requirements. Sourced from SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding module.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly or unit pay rate paid to the worker under this SOW engagement, where applicable. Used with bill_rate to calculate spread (bill rate minus pay rate) and gross margin analytics.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Worker engagement classification under the SOW. sow_ic = Independent Contractor (1099) engaged directly under SOW; sow_w2 = W-2 worker placed under SOW through staffing firm; sow_vendor = third-party vendor supplying resources under SOW.']
  redeployment_candidate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker on this SOW engagement has been identified as a candidate for redeployment to another assignment upon engagement completion or early termination. Supports bench management and redeployment pipeline analytics.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this SOW engagement record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. docusign_clm = DocuSign CLM; beeline_vms = Beeline VMS SOW module; bullhorn = Bullhorn ATS/CRM; manual = manually entered.']
  sow_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the Statement of Work document. Sourced from DocuSign CLM or Beeline VMS SOW module. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction. Used for cross-system reconciliation and client-facing communication.']
  sow_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the Statement of Work engagement as defined in the executed SOW document. Used for identification and reporting purposes (e.g., Q3 Data Migration Project, Network Infrastructure Upgrade Phase 2).']
  total_hours_budgeted decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of hours budgeted for the SOW engagement as defined in the contract. Used for time-and-materials SOW types to track utilization against budget and for workforce planning analytics.']
  total_hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual hours worked by the worker under this SOW engagement to date. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready time and attendance records. Used for budget utilization tracking and billing reconciliation.']
  total_sow_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total contracted monetary value of the SOW engagement as agreed in the executed SOW document. Represents the gross base amount for the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Used for revenue recognition, billing reconciliation, and financial reporting in Oracle NetSuite ERP and Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SOW engagement record was last modified. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED for this transaction. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  work_arrangement varchar [note: 'The physical work arrangement for the SOW engagement. onsite = worker performs work at client facility; remote = worker performs work from their own location; hybrid = combination of onsite and remote.']

  Note: 'Master record for Statement of Work (SOW) based placements where workers are engaged under a defined deliverable-based contract rather than a time-and-materials assignment. Tracks SOW reference number, SOW title, engagement start date, engagement end date, total SOW value, milestone-based billing flag, deliverable descriptions, SOW status (draft, active, completed, terminated), IC classification flag, co-employment risk assessment, and vendor reference for MSP-managed SOW programs. Distinct from the contract domains SOW template — this product tracks the operational placement-level engagement instance, not the commercial agreement terms. Sourced from DocuSign CLM and Beeline VMS SOW module.'
}

Table placement.offer {
  offer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the job offer record. Primary key for the placement.offer data product.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.assignment. Business justification: When a temporary/contract offer is accepted, it results in an assignment record. Linking offer to the resulting assignment enables tracking of offer-to-start conversion, offer acceptance to actual sta']
  bgc_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.bgc_order. Business justification: Offers are contingent on background check clearance. Links offer to BGC order for offer-to-hire workflow tracking, adverse action processing, and decline-reason analysis (BGC fail vs candidate decline']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which the placement offer is being made.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Pre-placement rate compliance validation requires verifying that the offer\'s proposed bill_rate, pay_rate, and markup_percentage comply with the contracted rate schedule. Offer approval workflows in M']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: At offer stage, the staffing firm communicates which credential package the candidate must complete before start date. This link enables offer-to-credential-package traceability for pre-employment onb']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: When a direct hire offer is accepted, it results in a direct_hire placement record. Linking offer to the resulting direct_hire enables tracking of offer-to-placement conversion for permanent roles, of']
  hiring_decision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.hiring_decision. Business justification: An offer is the direct downstream artifact of a hiring decision. Staffing ops tracks offer-to-decision traceability for QoS reporting, fall-off root-cause analysis, and compliance audits. A recruiter ']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: Offers to independent contractors require executed IC agreements to establish worker classification, scope, payment terms, and IP assignment before work begins. Compliance requirement to mitigate misc']
  interview_feedback_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.interview_feedback. Business justification: Offers are extended based on final-round interview feedback scores. Linking offer to interview_feedback enables offer-acceptance-rate analysis by feedback score and interviewer, a standard staffing KP']
  msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Offers are made under the terms of the client MSA governing fee schedules, placement terms, and compliance requirements. Pre-offer compliance checks and fee validation require linking the offer to the']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order (requisition) for which this offer was generated.']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Offer activation in staffing is gated by compliance clearance (BGC, drug screen, I-9). Recruiters and compliance teams must confirm placement_compliance_check status before extending or activating an ']
  position_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.position_requirement. Business justification: Offers are extended against a specific position_requirement slot. Offer-to-fill tracking by position_requirement is standard in ATS/VMS reporting — enables offer acceptance rate, time-to-offer, and su']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate to whom the job offer was extended.']
  req_pipeline_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.req_pipeline. Business justification: Offers are extended at a specific pipeline stage. Linking offer to req_pipeline enables offer-acceptance-rate-by-pipeline-stage reporting and pipeline-to-offer conversion metrics, standard recruiter p']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: offer is a transactional record capturing formal job offers for ALL placement types including SOW engagements. offer already has assignment_id and direct_hire_id FKs covering temp and perm placements ']
  submittal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate submittal record that preceded and triggered this offer, linking the recruitment pipeline to the offer stage.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Offers track which supplier submitted the candidate for supplier performance metrics (offer acceptance rate, time-to-offer). Used in VMS scorecards, supplier tier reviews, and fee attribution. Essenti']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Offer rate compliance validation requires checking proposed bill_rate and markup_percentage against the vendor agreement\'s markup cap. Pre-placement compliance workflows verify the offer terms comply ']
  vendor_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Offer rate compliance validation in MSP programs requires confirming that bill_rate, pay_rate, and markup_percentage on the offer comply with the supplier\'s approved vendor rate card for the job categ']
  vms_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.vms_order. Business justification: VMS-managed offers must be reconciled against the originating vms_order for submittal-to-offer ratio reporting, rtr_required_flag enforcement, and submission_window compliance audits — standard MSP pr']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: Offers submitted through VMS platforms require VMS program linkage for submission limit enforcement, VMS fee calculation, and VMS-specific offer workflow routing. offer.vms_req_number is a denormalize']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Offers must undergo wage-hour compliance validation before extension: FLSA exempt/non-exempt classification, minimum wage compliance, salary threshold verification, prevailing wage requirements. Pre-o']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific client work location where the offered position will be performed. Used for geographic workforce analytics and compliance (state-specific labor law, workers comp).']
  accepted_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate formally accepted the offer. Null if the offer has not been accepted. Used to calculate offer-to-acceptance cycle time KPI.']
  annual_salary decimal(18,2) [note: 'The annual base salary offered to the candidate for permanent or direct hire placements. Applicable for perm offer types. Used in compensation benchmarking and conversion fee calculations.']
  bgc_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a background check (BGC) is required as a condition of this offer. Drives Sterling Background Check initiation workflow and offer contingency tracking.']
  bill_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The hourly or unit bill rate offered to the client for the temporary or contract worker. Applicable for temp, CTH, and SOW offer types. Used in spread and gross margin calculations.']
  bullhorn_offer_reference varchar [note: 'Native offer record identifier from the Bullhorn ATS/CRM system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability back to the source system of record.']
  counter_offer_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The compensation amount proposed by the candidate as a counter-offer to the original offer. Applicable when offer_status is counter_offered. Used in negotiation tracking and compensation benchmarking.']
  counter_offer_rate_type varchar [note: 'The rate basis (hourly, annual, etc.) for the candidates counter-offer amount. Required to correctly interpret counter_offer_amount in negotiation analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the offer record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary amounts on this offer record (bill rate, pay rate, salary, sign-on bonus). Defaults to USD for domestic U.S. operations.']
  decline_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the candidate declined the offer (e.g., COMP_LOW, ACCEPTED_COMPETITOR, LOCATION, ROLE_MISMATCH, PERSONAL). Null if offer was not declined. Critical for competitive intelligence and offer strategy improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comp_low|accepted_competitor|location|role_mismatch|personal|counter_accepted — promote to reference product]']
  declined_date date [note: 'The date on which the candidate formally declined the offer. Null if not declined. Used in fall-off rate monitoring and competitive compensation benchmarking.']
  department varchar [note: 'The clients organizational department or cost center to which the offered position belongs. Used for client billing allocation and workforce planning analytics.']
  drug_screen_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-employment drug screen is required as a condition of this offer. Drives Sterling drug screening workflow and offer contingency tracking.']
  exempt_status varchar [note: 'Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) classification of the offered position as exempt (salaried, not eligible for overtime) or non-exempt (eligible for overtime pay). Critical for payroll compliance and OT/DT calculations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date by which the candidate must accept or decline the offer before it automatically expires. Used to enforce offer validity windows and trigger follow-up workflows.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The job title communicated to the candidate as part of the offer. May differ from the internal job order title. Used in candidate communications, onboarding, and EEOC job category reporting.']
  markup_percentage decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage markup applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate, representing the staffing firms gross margin component. Calculated as ((bill_rate - pay_rate) / pay_rate) * 100. Stored for offer-level financial benchmarking.']
  msp_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this offer is part of a Managed Service Provider (MSP)-managed contingent workforce program. Drives VMS submission workflows, rate card compliance checks, and MSP fee calculations.']
  negotiation_round_count varchar [note: 'The total number of negotiation rounds completed for this offer, including the initial offer and any counter-offers. Used to measure negotiation complexity and recruiter efficiency.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recruiter or account manager notes related to the offer, including negotiation context, special conditions, client-specific requirements, or candidate feedback not captured in structured fields.']
  offer_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the formal job offer was extended to the candidate. Principal business event date used in Time-to-Fill (TTF) and offer cycle time analytics.']
  offer_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-visible business identifier for the offer (e.g., OFR-2024-00123). Used in candidate communications, recruiter workflows, and reporting.']
  offer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the offer. Drives offer acceptance rate KPI and time-to-fill analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|accepted|declined|rescinded|expired|counter_offered — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  offer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the offer by engagement model: temp (temporary assignment), contract_to_hire (Contract-to-Hire / CTH), perm (permanent/direct placement), or sow (Statement of Work / SOW-based engagement).']
  pay_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The hourly or unit pay rate offered to the candidate for the temporary or contract assignment. Applicable for temp, CTH, and SOW offer types. Used in spread and markup percentage calculations.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The staffing engagement model for this offer, aligned with the downstream placement record type. Distinguishes temporary, temp-to-perm, contract-to-hire (CTH), direct hire, and SOW engagements for routing to the correct placement workflow.']
  proposed_start_date date [note: 'The anticipated assignment or employment start date communicated to the candidate as part of the offer. Used in Time-to-Start (TTS) analytics and workforce scheduling.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the unit basis for the offered pay rate and bill rate (e.g., hourly for temp workers, annual for direct hire, fixed for SOW engagements). Drives payroll and billing calculations.']
  rescind_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the offer was rescinded by the firm or client (e.g., BGC_FAILED, DRUG_SCREEN_FAILED, CLIENT_CANCELLED, HEADCOUNT_FREEZE, CANDIDATE_MISREPRESENTATION). Null if not rescinded. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bgc_failed|drug_screen_failed|client_cancelled|headcount_freeze|candidate_misrepresentation|other — promote to reference product]']
  rescinded_date date [note: 'The date on which the staffing firm or client rescinded (withdrew) the offer after it had been extended. Null if not rescinded. Tracked for compliance and candidate experience reporting.']
  sign_on_bonus decimal(18,2) [note: 'One-time sign-on bonus amount offered to the candidate as part of the compensation package. Null if no sign-on bonus is included. Relevant for compensation benchmarking and candidate attraction analytics.']
  sourcing_channel varchar [note: 'The recruitment sourcing channel through which the candidate was originally identified and submitted (e.g., job_board, referral, internal_database, social_media, campus, direct_sourcing). Used in sourcing effectiveness and cost-per-hire analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: job_board|referral|internal_database|social_media|campus|direct_sourcing|other — promote to reference product]']
  to_acceptance_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed between the offer_date and the accepted_date. Populated upon acceptance. Key KPI for Time-to-Fill (TTF) analytics and recruiter performance measurement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the offer record was last modified. Audit trail field used for change data capture (CDC), Silver layer incremental processing, and compliance audit trails.']
  work_arrangement varchar [note: 'The physical work arrangement offered to the candidate: onsite (at client location), remote (fully remote), or hybrid (combination). Relevant for candidate matching, client SLA compliance, and workforce planning.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Tax and employment classification of the worker under this offer: W-2 (employee), 1099 (independent contractor / IC), corp-to-corp (C2C), or EOR (Employer of Record). Drives payroll tax treatment, co-employment risk assessment, and IRS compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal job offer extended to a candidate for a temporary assignment, contract-to-hire, or permanent placement. Tracks offer date, offer type (temp, CTH, perm, SOW), offered bill rate, offered pay rate, offered salary, sign-on bonus, offer expiration date, offer status (pending, accepted, declined, rescinded, expired, counter-offered), decline reason code, counter-offer details, negotiation round count, and offer-to-acceptance cycle time. Serves as the bridge between the recruitment submittal process and the active assignment or direct hire record. Critical for offer acceptance rate KPI, time-to-fill analytics, and compensation benchmarking. Sourced from Bullhorn offer management workflow.'
}

Table placement.assignment_compliance {
  assignment_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each assignment-compliance requirement fulfillment record']
  assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to candidate.assessment. Business justification: Regulatory compliance tracking: assignment compliance records must reference the specific assessment (drug screen, background check, skills test) that satisfied each compliance requirement. Auditors a']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the worker assignment record that must satisfy this compliance requirement']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: assignment_compliance tracks per-requirement fulfillment status; the fulfillment artifact is a credential_instance. This FK enables compliance officers to trace exactly which credential instance satis']
  primary_assignment_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the specific compliance requirement that must be fulfilled for this assignment']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to compliance.compliance_requirement.compliance_requirement_id']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Individual assignment compliance requirements are often mandated by specific state rules (state BGC timing laws, state drug testing statutes, state-mandated training). Compliance teams need to trace e']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which this compliance requirement was successfully fulfilled for this assignment. Used to calculate re-verification due dates and track compliance history.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this assignment-compliance requirement record was created, typically at assignment activation when requirements are evaluated and assigned']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this completed requirement expires for this assignment and must be re-verified. Calculated from completion_date plus the requirements re_verification_interval_days. Null if requirement does not expire.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this assignment-compliance fulfillment record, tracking status changes, completions, and re-verifications']
  re_verification_due_date date [note: 'The date by which this requirement must be re-verified for this assignment to maintain compliance. Typically set before expiration_date to allow processing time. Drives operational workflows and alerts.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this compliance requirement for this specific assignment: pending (not yet started), in_progress (documentation being collected), completed (requirement satisfied), waived (exception granted), expired (completed but now expired and needs re-verification), failed (requirement not met), not_applicable (requirement determined not to apply to this assignment)']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'The method used to verify fulfillment of this requirement for this assignment: document_review (manual review of uploaded documents), background_check (third-party BGC vendor), drug_test (lab results), training_completion (LMS integration), credential_verification (license/certification check), client_attestation (client confirmation), system_integration (automated verification via API)']
  verification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured by the verifying user regarding the fulfillment of this requirement for this assignment. May include document names, vendor confirmation numbers, or special circumstances.']
  waiver_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a waiver or exception was granted for this requirement on this assignment, allowing the assignment to proceed despite non-completion']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for granting a waiver for this requirement on this assignment. Required when waiver_flag is true. Captures approval authority and business rationale.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance fulfillment relationship between a worker assignment and a regulatory or client-specific compliance requirement. It captures the operational tracking of which requirements must be satisfied for each assignment, the completion status, verification methods, and re-verification schedules. Each record links one assignment to one compliance requirement with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific assignment-requirement pairing, enabling the business to enforce compliance gates, track waivers, and manage re-verification workflows.. Existence Justification: In staffing operations, each worker assignment must satisfy multiple compliance requirements (I-9 employment eligibility, background checks, drug screens, client-specific credentials, industry certifications, training mandates). Simultaneously, each compliance requirement applies to many assignments across different workers, clients, and time periods. The business actively manages the fulfillment status of each assignment-requirement pairing, tracking completion dates, verification methods, waivers, expiration dates, and re-verification schedules as operational data that belongs to neither the assignment nor the requirement alone.'
}

Table timesheet.approved_hours_summary {
  approved_hours_summary_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approved_hours_summary']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment under which the worker performed the hours captured in this summary. Ties the hours to a specific job order and client engagement.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client (end-customer) for whom the worker performed services during this pay period. Used to route billable amounts to the correct client invoice.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Approved hours summaries aggregate billing using rate schedules; invoicing systems pull contracted rates for billing calculations and client invoice generation. Real business process: invoice generati']
  direct_deposit_setup_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.direct_deposit_setup. Business justification: The approved_hours_summary drives worker payment, and the direct_deposit_setup contains the banking instructions for that payment. Linking approved_hours_summary to direct_deposit_setup establishes wh']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Approved hours summaries are the authoritative billable-hours records that drive invoice generation. AR teams reconcile invoices against approved_hours_summary records. A staffing billing analyst expe']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition (Req) that originated the workers assignment. Enables traceability from approved hours back to the originating staffing request.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the pay period calendar record that defines the start and end dates of the period covered by this approved hours summary.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker whose approved hours are summarized in this record. Links to the worker master record in the Candidate/Worker domain.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Approved hours summaries for SOW workers drive milestone billing and SOW budget consumption reporting. Finance teams require approved_hours_summary→SOW engagement linkage to generate SOW invoices and ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Approved hours summaries drive vendor billing and diversity spend reporting. Attributing each summary to the supplying vendor is required for MSP program spend analytics, vendor scorecards, and client']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source timesheet record from which this approved hours summary was derived. Provides traceability back to the raw time entry data in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Approved hours summaries are the primary payroll and billing output — they must be validated against the governing wage/hour determination (SCA, Davis-Bacon, state prevailing wage) to confirm pay rate']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any post-approval amendment to this hours summary. Required when is_amended is True. Supports audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the approved hours summary record within the timesheet approval workflow. approved indicates hours are cleared for billing and payroll; disputed indicates a client or worker challenge is open; amended indicates a post-approval correction was applied.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the timesheet hours were formally approved by the authorized approver. Represents the principal business event timestamp that triggers downstream billing and payroll processing.']
  bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for the workers regular hours during this pay period, as defined in the client MSA or VMS rate card. Expressed in USD per hour. Used to calculate total billable amount.']
  billing_transmission_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current state of this approved hours summarys transmission to the client billing system (Salesforce Revenue Cloud). invoiced means a client invoice has been generated from this record.']
  burden_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The total employer burden rate applied to this workers payable wages for the pay period, expressed as a decimal percentage. Encompasses FICA, FUTA, SUTA, Workers Compensation (Workers Comp), and benefits costs. Used to calculate fully-loaded labor cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approved hours summary record was first created in the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements.']
  dt_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for double-time (DT) hours during this pay period. Typically 2x the regular bill rate per the client MSA or applicable state labor law.']
  dt_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved double-time (DT) hours worked during the pay period, applicable in jurisdictions or client agreements requiring 2x pay for hours beyond a daily or weekly threshold (e.g., California daily OT rules).']
  flsa_exemption_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA) for this assignment. Non-exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay at 1.5x for hours exceeding 40 per week.']
  gross_margin_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'The gross margin (Spread) for this pay period, calculated as total_billable_amount minus total_payable_amount minus burden costs. Represents the staffing agencys gross profit contribution from this worker-assignment combination before SG&A.']
  holiday_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved hours worked on designated company or client holidays during the pay period. Holiday hours may carry a premium bill rate and pay rate per the client MSA or worker agreement.']
  is_amended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this approved hours summary record has been amended after initial approval (e.g., due to a timesheet correction, missed punch adjustment, or retroactive rate change). True triggers a reconciliation process in payroll and billing.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'Boolean flag confirming that all hours in this summary have been formally approved by an authorized approver. True indicates the record is cleared for payroll and billing handoff. False indicates approval is still pending or was revoked.']
  ot_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate charged to the client for overtime (OT) hours during this pay period. Typically 1.5x the regular bill rate, but may be negotiated differently in the client MSA.']
  ot_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved overtime (OT) hours worked during the pay period, typically hours exceeding 40 per week for non-exempt workers under FLSA. Billed and paid at the OT multiplier rate (commonly 1.5x).']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the pay period covered by this approved hours summary. Defines the inclusive end boundary for all hours aggregated in this record.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the pay period covered by this approved hours summary. Defines the inclusive start boundary for all hours aggregated in this record.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The hourly rate paid to the worker for regular hours during this pay period, as defined in the workers placement agreement. Expressed in USD per hour. Used to calculate total payable amount and gross margin (Spread).']
  payroll_transmission_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current state of this approved hours summarys transmission to the payroll processing system (TempWorks Payroll). confirmed means payroll has acknowledged receipt and the record is locked for the pay run.']
  per_diem_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The daily per diem allowance rate paid to the worker per approved per diem unit. Must not exceed IRS-published per diem limits to maintain tax-free treatment. Expressed in USD per day.']
  per_diem_units decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of per diem units (typically days) approved for the worker during the pay period. Per diem is a daily allowance paid to workers for meals, lodging, or incidentals when working away from their tax home. Not subject to payroll taxes when within IRS limits.']
  pto_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved paid time off (PTO) hours for the worker during the pay period. PTO hours are payable to the worker but are typically not billable to the client unless specified in the MSA.']
  regular_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total approved regular (straight-time) hours worked by the worker during the pay period, excluding overtime and double-time. Used as the base quantity for both client billing and worker payroll at the standard bill rate and pay rate respectively.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this approved hours summary was sourced or triggered. Kronos Workforce Ready is the primary source; Beeline VMS may be the source for MSP-managed programs; Manual indicates a staffing coordinator override entry.']
  summary_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this approved hours summary record, formatted as AHS-{YYYY}-{WW}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in billing and payroll correspondence to reference a specific approved period.']
  total_billable_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total gross amount to be invoiced to the client for this pay period, calculated as the sum of (regular_hours × bill_rate) + (ot_hours × ot_bill_rate) + (dt_hours × dt_bill_rate) + (holiday_hours × applicable holiday bill rate). Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice generation.']
  total_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Sum of all approved hour types (regular + OT + DT + holiday) for the worker during the pay period. Represents the total time-based quantity used for workforce analytics and FTE calculations. Excludes PTO hours.']
  total_payable_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total gross wages payable to the worker for this pay period, calculated as the sum of (regular_hours × pay_rate) + (ot_hours × ot_pay_rate) + (dt_hours × dt_pay_rate) + (holiday_hours × holiday pay rate) + (per_diem_units × per_diem_rate). Feeds TempWorks Payroll processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this approved hours summary record was last modified. Tracks amendments such as retroactive adjustments or correction reprocessing.']
  vms_requisition_number varchar [note: 'The requisition or purchase order number assigned by the clients Vendor Management System (VMS), such as Beeline, for this workers engagement. Required on client invoices for MSP-managed programs.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the worker performed services on-site at the client location, fully remote, or in a hybrid arrangement during this pay period. Affects per diem eligibility and certain state tax obligations.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workers tax and employment relationship for this assignment. W-2 indicates a staffing agency employee; 1099 indicates an independent contractor (IC); C2C indicates a corp-to-corp engagement. Drives payroll tax treatment and burden rate application.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'The National Council on Compensation Insurance (NCCI) workers compensation class code assigned to the worker for this assignment. Determines the workers comp insurance premium rate applied within the burden rate calculation.']

  Note: 'Unified aggregated summary of approved hours for a worker-assignment-client combination within a pay period, broken down by pay type (regular, OT, DT, per diem, holiday). Serves as the single authoritative handoff record for both client invoice generation (billing domain) and payroll processing (payroll domain). Captures total regular hours, OT hours, DT hours, per diem units, bill rate applied, pay rate applied, total billable amount, total payable amount, burden rate components, approval confirmation flag, and payroll transmission status. Replaces separate billable and payable hour summaries to ensure a single reconciled SSOT. Distinct from raw time_entry records — this is the approved, billing-and-payroll-ready output of the timesheet approval process.'
}

Linking adjustment directly to the causative billing_dispute enables dispute-driven adjustment tracking — a named staffing billing o']
  earnings_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.earnings_line. Business justification: Payroll adjustments correct specific earnings lines. Linking adjustment.earnings_line_id → payroll.earnings_line enables auditors to trace which earnings line was corrected by a retroactive adjustment']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client invoice or credit memo generated as a result of this billing adjustment. Links the timesheet correction to the corresponding accounts receivable transaction in Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: Post-submission timesheet adjustments are tied to a specific pay period for payroll and billing correction purposes. The adjustment table currently stores pay_period_start_date and pay_period_end_date']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll run in which this adjustment was processed. Enables reconciliation between timesheet adjustments and payroll output records. Null until the adjustment is applied to payroll.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contingent worker whose timesheet is being adjusted. Enables worker-level payroll reconciliation, compliance reporting, and dispute resolution. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE canonical category.']
  rate_override_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.rate_override. Business justification: Retroactive billing and payroll adjustments are frequently triggered by approved rate overrides. Staffing billing teams must link each retroactive adjustment to the specific rate_override that authori']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: Timesheet adjustments may be triggered by wage-hour violations discovered in DOL/state audits (unpaid OT, misclassified hours). Real business process: remediation tracking linking adjustments to viola']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Billing and pay adjustments for SOW workers must reference the SOW engagement for correct cost allocation and invoice correction. Finance teams require adjustment→SOW engagement linkage to reconcile S']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Billing and payroll adjustments in MSP programs must be attributed to the supplying vendor for vendor dispute resolution, chargeback processing, and supplier billing reconciliation. Without this FK, a']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original approved timesheet record that this adjustment corrects or amends. Core linkage enabling full audit trail from original submission to corrected values. Maps to the PARTY_REFERENCE canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable business identifier for the adjustment (e.g., ADJ-2024-000123). Used in dispute resolution, payroll reconciliation, and client billing communications. Maps to the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the adjustment record. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval and application to payroll and billing. Maps to the LIFECYCLE_STATUS canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  adjustment_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the nature of the adjustment. Drives downstream payroll and billing processing logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hours_correction|rate_correction|pay_type_reclassification|retroactive_change|break_correction|absence_correction — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  affects_billing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timesheet adjustment impacts client billable hours or bill rates, triggering a credit memo or supplemental invoice. False for adjustments that only affect worker pay (e.g., internal pay type reclassifications with no billing impact).']
  affects_payroll boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this timesheet adjustment impacts worker payable hours or pay rates, requiring a payroll correction or supplemental pay run. False for adjustments that only affect billing (e.g., bill rate corrections with no pay rate change).']
  applied_to_billing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the approved adjustment was applied to client billing in Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Null until billing is updated. Confirms the client invoice or credit memo has been generated for the adjustment.']
  applied_to_payroll_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the approved adjustment was applied to the payroll run in TempWorks Payroll. Null until the adjustment is processed. Confirms the adjustment has been actioned in the payroll system of record.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the approval tier required for this adjustment based on the adjustment type, dollar impact, or client contract requirements. Single-level approvals are handled internally; dual or client-required approvals involve additional stakeholders.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the timesheet adjustment was approved by the designated approver. Null if not yet approved. Used for approval cycle time analytics and SLA compliance reporting.']
  bill_amount_adjusted decimal(12,2) [note: 'The corrected gross client billing amount (in USD) after the adjustment. Drives credit memo issuance or supplemental invoice generation in Salesforce Revenue Cloud. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.']
  bill_amount_original decimal(12,2) [note: 'The gross client billing amount (in USD) calculated from the original timesheet hours and bill rate before adjustment. Baseline for client invoice reconciliation and credit memo calculation.']
  bill_rate_adjusted decimal(10,4) [note: 'The corrected client bill rate (per hour) in USD after the adjustment. Used when a rate correction changes the amount invoiced to the client. Drives credit memo or supplemental invoice generation.']
  bill_rate_original decimal(10,4) [note: 'The client bill rate (per hour) recorded on the original timesheet, in USD. Preserved for billing reconciliation when rate corrections affect client invoices. Represents the rate charged to the client before adjustment.']
  client_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the client has formally approved the timesheet adjustment, required when the adjustment affects billable hours or bill rates under the clients Master Service Agreement (MSA). True when client sign-off is obtained.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this timesheet adjustment record was first created in the system. Maps to the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this adjustment record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for international staffing engagements. Part of MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.']
  hours_adjusted decimal(6,2) [note: 'The corrected number of hours after the adjustment is applied. Replaces the original hours value for payroll processing and client billing. The difference between hours_adjusted and hours_original represents the net hours change.']
  hours_original decimal(6,2) [note: 'The number of hours recorded on the original approved timesheet for the work date and pay type being corrected. Preserved as the pre-adjustment baseline for audit, dispute resolution, and reconciliation.']
  is_retroactive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adjustment applies to a prior pay period that has already been processed in payroll and/or billing. Retroactive adjustments require special handling in payroll tax recalculation (FICA, FUTA, SUTA) and may trigger amended invoices.']
  pay_amount_adjusted decimal(12,2) [note: 'The corrected gross worker pay amount (in USD) after the adjustment is applied. Represents the authoritative payable amount for the adjusted period. The difference from pay_amount_original drives payroll debit or credit entries. Maps to MONETARY_TRIPLET canonical category.']
  pay_amount_original decimal(12,2) [note: 'The gross worker pay amount (in USD) calculated from the original timesheet hours and pay rate before adjustment. Serves as the financial baseline for payroll reconciliation and adjustment impact analysis.']
  pay_rate_adjusted decimal(10,4) [note: 'The corrected worker pay rate (per hour) in USD after the adjustment. Used for rate correction and retroactive change adjustment types. Drives recalculation of worker gross pay for the affected period.']
  pay_rate_original decimal(10,4) [note: 'The worker pay rate (per hour) recorded on the original timesheet, in USD. Preserved for rate correction adjustments and retroactive pay rate changes. Sensitive compensation data.']
  pay_type_adjusted varchar [note: 'The corrected pay type code after adjustment. Used when a pay type reclassification adjustment is applied (e.g., regular hours reclassified as overtime (OT) or double-time (DT)). Drives recalculation of worker pay and client bill amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|overtime|double_time|per_diem|pto|holiday|on_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pay_type_original varchar [note: 'The pay type code recorded on the original timesheet before adjustment (e.g., regular, overtime (OT), double-time (DT), per diem, paid time off (PTO), holiday, on-call). Preserved for full audit trail and reclassification tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|overtime|double_time|per_diem|pto|holiday|on_call — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code categorizing why the timesheet adjustment was initiated. Supports root-cause analysis, compliance reporting, and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_entry_error|system_error|missed_punch|rate_change|ot_reclassification|client_dispute|worker_dispute|manager_correction — promote to reference product]']
  reason_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explanation provided by the requestor describing the specific circumstances requiring the timesheet adjustment. Supplements the reason code with contextual detail for audit and dispute resolution.']
  requestor_role varchar [note: 'The business role of the individual who submitted the adjustment request. Provides context for approval routing and compliance audit without requiring a join to the user table.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated or triggered the timesheet adjustment (e.g., Kronos Workforce Ready, Bullhorn, Beeline VMS, TempWorks, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation across integrated systems.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the timesheet adjustment request was formally submitted into the workflow system. Maps to the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for SLA tracking and audit trail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this timesheet adjustment record was last modified. Maps to the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks all changes for compliance and audit purposes.']
  work_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date on which the hours being adjusted were originally worked. Enables day-level audit trail and supports overtime calculation corrections at the daily level.']

  Note: 'Records post-submission corrections and amendments to approved timesheets, capturing the original timesheet reference, adjustment type (hours correction, rate correction, pay type reclassification, retroactive change), original values, adjusted values, adjustment reason, requestor, approver, and effective pay period. Maintains a complete audit trail of all timesheet modifications for compliance, dispute resolution, and payroll/billing reconciliation.'
}

Table timesheet.labor_category {
  labor_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the labor category record. Primary key for the labor_category reference entity. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — a reference/code table defining labor classifications; per-role minimums are exempt, but MASTER_RESOURCE categories are applied given the entity carries substantive business attributes beyond a simple enumeration.]']
  burden_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.burden_rate. Business justification: Labor categories define job classifications; burden_rate defines employer cost loads (FICA, SUTA, WC) by worker classification. Linking labor_category.burden_rate_id → payroll.burden_rate enables payr']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Labor categories map to rate schedule job categories; rate schedules define bill/pay rates per labor category for contract compliance. Real business process: rate card mapping and labor category prici']
  credential_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Staffing operations require mapping labor categories to credential requirements for compliance gating at assignment start. A \'Registered Nurse\' labor category must reference the credential requirement']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Staffing firms must reconcile timesheet labor categories against job order job categories for bill rate validation, markup calculation, and workforce cost reporting. job_category carries typical_marku']
  ot_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the overtime rule set applicable to this labor category. Determines how overtime hours are calculated (e.g., daily OT after 8 hours, weekly OT after 40 hours, California daily/weekly OT rules). Links to the ot_rule reference entity as specified in the product description.']
  skill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to candidate.skill. Business justification: Labor categories are defined by primary skill type and proficiency level — a staffing firm\'s rate card maps directly from verified skill to labor category tier. Linking labor_category to the canonical']
  vendor_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: Labor categories must map to vendor-specific rate cards for rate validation, margin analysis, and vendor bill rate verification. When evaluating vendor submissions or processing timesheets, staffing f']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: VMS programs define client-specific labor categories with rate caps and category codes. The vms_category_code on labor_category is a denormalized reference to vms_program. Staffing VMS operations requ']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Labor categories must have validated wage-hour determinations (FLSA classification, prevailing wage, exempt status). Rate cards cannot be published without determination. Real business process: compli']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business user or manager who approved this labor category definition and its associated rates. Required for governance and audit compliance in rate card management.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this labor category record was formally approved by the designated approver. Used for governance, audit trail, and effective dating validation.']
  billable_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether time entries coded to this labor category are billable to the client. When true, hours are included in client invoicing. When false, hours are internal or non-billable (e.g., orientation, training, bench time).']
  burden_rate_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The total employer burden rate percentage applied to the base pay rate for workers in this labor category. Includes employer-side FICA, FUTA, SUTA, workers comp, and benefits costs expressed as a percentage of base pay. Used for gross margin and spread analysis.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the labor category. Used in timesheets, billing systems, and VMS integrations to reference the category. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready and synchronized to Beeline VMS and Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the labor category, including scope of work, typical duties, and applicable skill requirements. Used for client-facing documentation, MSA exhibits, and SOW labor category definitions.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the labor category (e.g., Software Engineer – Senior, Light Industrial – Forklift Operator, Administrative – Data Entry). Displayed in timesheets, client invoices, and workforce analytics dashboards.']
  category_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification grouping the labor category into a workforce segment (e.g., professional, light industrial, clerical, technical, healthcare, executive, skilled trades). Drives workforce analytics segmentation and client billing tier assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: professional|light_industrial|clerical|technical|healthcare|executive|skilled_trades — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor category record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  default_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly bill rate (in USD) charged to the client for workers assigned to this labor category. Serves as the baseline rate before client-specific or contract-specific overrides are applied. Used by Salesforce Revenue Cloud for invoice generation.']
  default_dt_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly double-time (DT) bill rate charged to the client for double-time hours worked by workers in this labor category. Applicable in states or contracts requiring double-time pay (e.g., California labor law).']
  default_dt_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly double-time (DT) pay rate paid to workers in this labor category for double-time hours. Applicable under state-specific labor laws or union agreements requiring double-time compensation.']
  default_ot_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly overtime (OT) bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours worked by workers in this labor category. Typically 1.5x the standard bill rate unless a client-specific rate card overrides it.']
  default_ot_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly overtime (OT) pay rate paid to workers in this labor category for overtime hours. Must comply with FLSA overtime requirements (minimum 1.5x regular rate for non-exempt workers).']
  default_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Default hourly pay rate (in USD) paid to workers assigned to this labor category. Serves as the baseline rate before worker-specific or assignment-specific overrides are applied. Used by TempWorks Payroll for compensation calculation.']
  eeo_job_category varchar [note: 'EEOC EEO-1 job category assigned to this labor category for equal employment opportunity reporting. Required for federal contractors and employers with 100+ employees. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executives_senior_officials|first_mid_officials|professionals|technicians|sales_workers|administrative_support|craft_workers|operatives|laborers_helpers|service_workers — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this labor category definition and its associated rates become effective. Supports rate versioning and historical rate lookups for timesheet processing and billing reconciliation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this labor category definition and its associated rates expire. Null for open-ended categories. After this date, the category is no longer available for new timesheet entries unless renewed or superseded.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Indicates whether workers in this labor category are classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Non-exempt workers are entitled to overtime pay; exempt workers are not. Critical for overtime rule application and payroll compliance.']
  ic_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether workers in this labor category may be engaged as independent contractors (1099) rather than W-2 employees. Drives worker classification compliance checks per IRS worker classification rules and DOL economic reality test.']
  job_function_code varchar [note: 'Standardized job function code classifying the primary work function of this labor category (e.g., IT, Finance, Operations, Engineering, Healthcare). Aligns with O*NET or SOC occupational classification codes for workforce analytics and regulatory reporting.']
  kronos_labor_category_ref varchar [note: 'The native labor category identifier in Kronos Workforce Ready, the system of record for time and attendance. Used for system integration and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the Kronos source system.']
  labor_category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the labor category record. active indicates the category is available for use in timesheet entries and billing; inactive indicates it has been retired; pending indicates it is awaiting approval; deprecated indicates it has been superseded by a newer category.']
  markup_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'Default markup percentage applied over the pay rate to derive the bill rate for this labor category. Represents the spread as a percentage of pay rate. Used in pricing models and profitability analysis. Markup = (Bill Rate - Pay Rate) / Pay Rate × 100.']
  max_bill_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum allowable hourly bill rate for this labor category. Used as a ceiling in VMS rate card negotiations and client contract management. Bill rates above this ceiling require management approval.']
  min_pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum allowable hourly pay rate for workers assigned to this labor category. Enforces compliance with applicable federal, state, or local minimum wage laws and prevailing wage requirements. Pay rates below this floor are rejected during payroll processing.']
  per_diem_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether workers assigned to this labor category are eligible for per diem allowances (daily expense reimbursements for travel, lodging, or meals). Affects payroll processing and client billing for travel-based assignments.']
  prevailing_wage_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this labor category is subject to prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act or Service Contract Act (SCA) for government or federally funded contracts. When true, the prevailing_wage_rate field applies.']
  prevailing_wage_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The federally or state-mandated prevailing wage rate (in USD per hour) applicable to this labor category for government or public works contracts. Determined by DOL wage determinations under the Davis-Bacon Act or SCA. Null when prevailing_wage_indicator is false.']
  rate_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the default bill/pay rate tier associated with this labor category. Rate tiers define pricing bands used across client contracts and VMS programs. Links to rate tier configuration in Beeline VMS and Salesforce Revenue Cloud.']
  sca_labor_category_code varchar [note: 'The official Service Contract Act (SCA) labor category code or title as defined in the applicable DOL wage determination. Used to map internal labor categories to SCA-compliant job classifications for federal service contracts. Required for compliance reporting on government staffing contracts.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Proficiency or seniority tier associated with the labor category (e.g., entry, junior, mid, senior, lead, principal, executive). Used to differentiate bill/pay rate tiers and to support workforce planning analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry|junior|mid|senior|lead|principal|executive — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code mapped to this labor category. Used for EEOC reporting, DOL compliance, workforce analytics, and labor market benchmarking. Format: XX-XXXX (e.g., 15-1252 for Software Developers).']
  temp_to_perm_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether placements in this labor category are eligible for temp-to-perm conversion (contract-to-hire). When true, a conversion fee may apply upon permanent placement. Used in Bullhorn ATS/CRM for placement tracking and conversion fee calculation.']
  union_applicable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this labor category is subject to a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) or union work rules. When true, the union_code field identifies the applicable union. Affects pay rates, overtime rules, and benefit requirements.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union or collective bargaining agreement (CBA) applicable to this labor category. Null when union_applicable is false. Used to apply union-specific pay rules, benefit contributions, and work rules in payroll processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor category record was last modified. Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'NCCI (National Council on Compensation Insurance) workers compensation class code associated with this labor category. Determines the workers comp insurance rate applied to payroll for workers in this category. Used by TempWorks Payroll for burden rate calculation.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining labor categories and work classifications used to categorize time entries for cost allocation, billing rate determination, and workforce analytics. Captures category code, category name, skill level, FLSA classification (exempt/non-exempt), prevailing wage indicator, prevailing wage rate (for Davis-Bacon Act compliance on government staffing contracts), union code applicability, SCA (Service Contract Act) labor category mapping, and default bill/pay rate tier. Used by clients and staffing operations to segment labor costs by function, skill, or contract type. Links to ot_rule for determining applicable overtime calculation rules.'
}

Table timesheet.offboarding_record {
  offboarding_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the offboarding record. Primary key for the offboarding_record product.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement assignment that is ending. Links this offboarding event to the original placement record.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: Offboarding must track which client the worker is separating from for rehire eligibility, conversion tracking, client relationship management, and exit reason analysis. Essential for client retention ']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Offboarding involves client notification, exit interviews with client managers, equipment return coordination, and final approvals. Client contact link is essential for offboarding workflow completion']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: During worker offboarding, staffing firms must close out the credential package associated with the assignment — notifying credentialing bodies of employment end, deactivating access credentials, and ']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire placements require offboarding processing for guarantee period tracking, final payroll, and fall-off management. HR operations teams need offboarding_record→direct_hire linkage to manage r']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC worker offboarding must reference the IC agreement to enforce termination notice requirements, final payment terms, non-compete clauses, and IP assignment obligations at separation. Staffing firms ']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Offboarding processes are site-specific: equipment_return_required, system_access_revoked, and badge return all depend on the client location. Staffing coordinators need the specific site to coordinat']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Offboarding records must reference the originating job order to decrement headcount_remaining, trigger order status updates (e.g., backfill), and calculate time-to-fill metrics. Staffing operations te']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Offboarding workflows must verify that all placement compliance checks (background, drug screen, credential verification) are in a terminal status before separation is finalized. Staffing operations m']
  placement_fee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.placement_fee. Business justification: Offboarding records track temp-to-perm conversions (conversion_to_perm_flag, conversion_fee_amount). When a conversion occurs at offboarding, a placement fee is generated. Staffing billing teams link ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker being offboarded. Links to the candidate master record.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW engagement workers require offboarding processing for final billing reconciliation, system access revocation, and deliverable sign-off. Operations teams need offboarding_record→SOW engagement link']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: State final pay laws impose strict deadlines (e.g., California requires same-day final pay for involuntary terminations). Offboarding records must reference the governing state compliance rule to enfo']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Track supplier for offboarded workers to calculate supplier-specific fall-off rates, rehire eligibility tracking, and performance scorecard metrics. Critical for vendor management and preferred suppli']

This links the offboarding record to the checklist template that defines required offboarding tasks (equipment return, exit intervi']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.timesheet. Business justification: The offboarding_record tracks the final timesheet status (final_timesheet_status field) as part of the worker separation workflow.   worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Worker classification at separation determines final pay obligations, COBRA eligibility, unemployment insurance liability, and separation agreement requirements. HR managers processing offboarding mus']
  benefits_termination_date date [note: 'Date when the workers health, dental, vision, and other benefits coverage ends. Typically end of month following separation or last day worked.']
  client_feedback_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating provided by the client regarding the workers overall performance during the assignment. Scale typically 1-5 or 1-10.']
  client_feedback_received boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether feedback was received from the client regarding the workers performance and the reason for assignment end.']
  cobra_notification_date date [note: 'Date when COBRA continuation coverage notification was sent to the worker. Must occur within 14 days of separation per federal law.']
  cobra_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the worker is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage and must receive notification. Based on benefits enrollment and separation circumstances.']
  conversion_to_perm_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is being converted to a permanent employee of the client. Triggers conversion fee billing and positive outcome tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the offboarding record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  equipment_return_date date [note: 'Date when all required equipment was returned and verified. Null if equipment return is pending or not applicable.']
  equipment_return_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the worker has company or client equipment that must be returned as part of offboarding.']
  equipment_return_status varchar [note: 'Current status of equipment return process. Tracks whether all issued equipment has been returned and accounted for.']
  exit_interview_date date [note: 'Date when the exit interview was conducted. Null if exit interview was not completed.']
  exit_interview_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether an exit interview was conducted with the departing worker. Used for retention analytics and quality improvement.']
  exit_reason_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the reason for separation. Used for turnover analytics and retention strategy development. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compensation|career_growth|work_environment|personal|relocation|performance|client_relationship|commute|benefits|workload|culture — promote to reference product]']
  exit_reason_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes capturing the specific reasons for separation as provided by the worker or client. Confidential information used for internal analysis.']
  final_payroll_date date [note: 'Date when the workers final paycheck was processed. Used for payroll reconciliation and compliance reporting.']
  final_timesheet_status varchar [note: 'Status of the workers final timesheet submission and approval. Critical for ensuring complete and accurate final payroll processing.']
  last_day_worked date [note: 'The final date the worker performed work for the client. Used for payroll cutoff, benefits termination, and compliance reporting.']
