  published_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the schedule was published and made visible to the contingent worker. Used to measure schedule advance notice compliance and worker communication lead time.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the schedule record as assigned by Kronos Workforce Ready (e.g., SCH-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference and operational communication.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the planned schedule record. Draft indicates the schedule is being built; published indicates it has been released to the worker; confirmed indicates worker acknowledgement; modified indicates a post-publish change; cancelled indicates the shift was removed; completed indicates the shift period has passed.']
  scheduled_break_minutes varchar [note: 'Total planned unpaid break duration in minutes for this shift as defined in the schedule template. Used to calculate net scheduled working hours and to validate compliance with mandatory rest period regulations.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of planned working hours for this shift, excluding unpaid break time. Represents the expected billable and payable hours for the shift. Used as the baseline for actual vs. scheduled variance reporting and OT threshold monitoring.']
  shift_date date [note: 'The specific calendar date on which this scheduled shift is to occur. Distinct from shift_start_time in that it provides a day-level grain for schedule reporting, calendar views, and daily workforce planning without requiring timestamp precision.']
  shift_end_time timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which the contingent workers shift is scheduled to end, expressed in local worksite time with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used in conjunction with shift_start_time to derive scheduled shift duration for variance analysis.']
  shift_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time at which the contingent workers shift is scheduled to begin, expressed in local worksite time with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the baseline for actual time-entry variance analysis and OT calculation.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the scheduled shift by time-of-day or day-of-week category. Used for differential pay rate application, OT (Overtime) and DT (Double Time) rule triggering, and workforce analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: day|evening|night|weekend|holiday|on_call — promote to reference product if additional shift types are required]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this schedule record was sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across integrated systems in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  worker_confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the contingent worker confirmed the scheduled shift. Null if the worker has not yet confirmed. Used for advance notice compliance measurement and scheduling analytics.']

  Note: 'Defines the planned work schedule for a contingent worker on a specific assignment, including scheduled shift start/end times, days of week, shift type (day/evening/night/weekend), expected weekly hours, and effective date range. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling module. Serves as the baseline against which actual time entries are compared for variance analysis and OT calculation.'
}

Table timesheet.shift {
  shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a specific scheduled or worked shift instance for a contingent worker. Primary key for the shift data product in the Kronos Workforce Ready time and attendance system.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the active placement or assignment under which this shift is worked. Links the shift to the specific job order and client engagement governing the workers deployment.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the client organization at whose worksite this shift is performed. Used for client-level labor reporting, billing, and SLA compliance tracking.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Actual worked shifts must reference the rate schedule used for billing/payroll; variance analysis compares shift rates to contracted rates for margin analysis. Real business process: shift billing rec']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Shifts occur at specific client locations — shift.geofence_verified requires knowing the client site boundary. Location-based workforce reporting, site headcount tracking, and workers_comp_code valida']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the job order (requisition) associated with this shift. Connects the shift to the clients staffing request for labor analytics and billing reconciliation.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll pay period to which this shift belongs. Links the shift to the correct payroll processing cycle in TempWorks Payroll for wage calculation and tax filing.']
  pay_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_rate. Business justification: Each shift\'s payable hours must be priced against the authoritative pay_rate record for the assignment. Linking shift.pay_rate_id → payroll.pay_rate enables shift-level payroll cost validation and WC ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contingent worker assigned to this shift. Links to the worker/candidate master record in Bullhorn ATS/CRM and Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.schedule. Business justification: shift represents an actual worked shift instance, while schedule represents the planned schedule. A shift should link back to the schedule it was planned under to enable variance analysis (scheduled v']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: Shifts worked under a SOW engagement must reference the engagement for billing reconciliation and workers comp classification. Staffing operations requires this to correctly allocate shift costs to SO']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: State compliance rules govern daily hour limits, mandatory rest/meal breaks, and shift scheduling restrictions. Staffing compliance officers validate each shift against the applicable state rule (e.g.']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent timesheet record that aggregates this shift along with other shifts in the same submission period. Links shift-level data to the timesheet approval workflow.']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific physical worksite or location where the shift is performed. Supports multi-site client operations, workers compensation jurisdiction assignment, and OSHA compliance.']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Worker classification governs whether a shift is billable, what overtime rules apply, and whether IC behavioral-control restrictions are triggered. Scheduling and billing systems must reference classi']
  absence_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for a missed or partial shift absence (e.g., NCNS for no-call-no-show, SICK, FMLA, PTO, JURY). Used for attendance tracking, FMLA compliance, and workforce reliability analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCNS|SICK|FMLA|PTO|JURY|BEREAVEMENT|MILITARY — promote to reference product]']
  actual_end_time timestamp [note: 'The actual clock-out date and time recorded when the worker ended the shift. Sourced from Kronos punch data. Used for early departure tracking, schedule variance, and payable hours calculation.']
  actual_start_time timestamp [note: 'The actual clock-in date and time recorded when the worker began the shift. Sourced from Kronos punch data. Used for tardiness tracking, schedule variance, and payable hours calculation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the shift approval workflow. pending = awaiting supervisor review; approved = authorized for payroll and billing; rejected = returned for correction; disputed = worker or client contest; auto_approved = system-approved per configured rules.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift was approved by the authorized approver. Used for SLA tracking on approval turnaround time and payroll cutoff compliance.']
  billable_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours approved for client invoicing for this shift. May differ from worked_hours due to client-specific billing rules, approved adjustments, or non-billable time. Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud for invoice generation.']
  break_minutes varchar [note: 'Total scheduled or actual break time in minutes deducted from the shift duration. Includes meal breaks and rest periods. Used for net hours calculation and compliance with mandatory break laws (e.g., FLSA, state labor codes).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Client or internal cost center code to which this shifts labor cost is allocated. Supports client-side cost allocation reporting, internal P&L tracking, and Oracle NetSuite ERP general ledger posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift record was first created in the system, either by the scheduling engine or manual entry. Serves as the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Client department or business unit code associated with this shift. Used for granular labor cost reporting to clients and workforce analytics by department.']
  differential_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the pay differential applicable to this shift based on timing (e.g., evening, night, weekend, holiday). Drives premium pay rate calculations in TempWorks Payroll and corresponding bill rate adjustments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAY|EVE|NIGHT|WEEKEND|HOLIDAY|HAZARD|ONCALL — promote to reference product]']
  dt_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked beyond the double-time threshold (typically 12 hours/day or 7th consecutive day per California Labor Code and similar state laws) classified as double-time (DT) at 2x pay rate. Used for payroll and client billing at DT bill rate.']
  geofence_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workers clock-in/clock-out was verified within the approved geofence boundary of the worksite. Supports fraud prevention, buddy-punching detection, and client worksite compliance requirements.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift falls on a recognized company or federal holiday. Drives holiday pay premium calculations in TempWorks Payroll and holiday bill rate surcharges for client invoicing.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift falls on a Saturday or Sunday. Used to apply weekend shift differential pay codes and corresponding client bill rate adjustments per MSA terms.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Standardized job classification code for the role performed during this shift. Drives pay rate lookup, workers compensation classification, and EEOC/OFCCP labor category reporting.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest change for data lineage, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for supervisor or worker notes related to this shift, such as reasons for schedule deviation, special instructions, incident observations, or client-specific comments. Used for operational context and dispute resolution.']
  ot_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Hours worked beyond the standard threshold (typically 40 hours/week or 8 hours/day depending on state law) classified as overtime (OT) at 1.5x pay rate. Critical for FLSA compliance, payroll cost management, and client billing at OT bill rate.']
  payable_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours approved for worker compensation for this shift. May differ from worked_hours due to guaranteed minimum hours, rounding rules, or approved adjustments. Feeds TempWorks Payroll for worker pay calculation.']
  per_diem_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of per diem allowance payable to the worker for this shift. Applicable for travel assignments and remote deployments. Sourced from assignment-level per diem rate configuration. Feeds TempWorks Payroll.']
  per_diem_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is eligible to receive a per diem allowance for this shift (e.g., for travel assignments, remote worksite deployments). Drives per diem payment processing in TempWorks Payroll.']
  punch_source varchar [note: 'Method or system through which the shifts time punches were captured. time_clock = physical Kronos terminal; mobile_app = Kronos mobile; web_portal = browser entry; supervisor_entry = manager-entered; vms = Beeline VMS submission; manual_adjustment = back-office correction.']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Portion of worked hours classified as straight-time regular pay hours, not subject to overtime or double-time premiums. Used for payroll processing and client billing at the standard pay/bill rate.']
  schedule_variance_minutes varchar [note: 'Difference in minutes between actual worked hours and scheduled hours for this shift (actual minus scheduled). Positive values indicate overtime beyond schedule; negative values indicate early departure or missed time. Key metric for schedule adherence and labor analytics.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'The planned end date and time of the shift as defined in the workforce schedule. Used alongside scheduled_start_time to determine planned shift duration and schedule adherence.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total planned hours for the shift as defined in the workforce schedule, calculated from scheduled_start_time to scheduled_end_time minus planned break time. Used for headcount planning and FTE calculations.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'The planned start date and time of the shift as defined in the workforce schedule. Used for schedule adherence analysis, variance calculation, and dispatch confirmation.']
  shift_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the shift is scheduled or was worked. Serves as the principal business event date for the shift, used for daily labor reporting, payroll period alignment, and client billing.']
  shift_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this shift instance, typically generated by Kronos Workforce Ready. Used for cross-system reference, timesheet reconciliation, and worker/supervisor communication.']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shift. scheduled = future shift confirmed; in_progress = worker clocked in; completed = shift ended and hours recorded; missed = worker did not appear; cancelled = shift removed before start; no_call_no_show = worker absent without notification.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift by its operational nature. regular = standard scheduled shift; overtime = OT-designated shift; holiday = worked on a recognized holiday; on_call = standby/on-call shift; split = non-contiguous shift segments; per_diem = per diem eligible shift.']
  start_variance_minutes varchar [note: 'Difference in minutes between actual_start_time and scheduled_start_time (actual minus scheduled). Positive values indicate tardiness; negative values indicate early arrival. Used for punctuality tracking and SLA compliance.']
  worked_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total actual hours worked during the shift, calculated from actual_start_time to actual_end_time minus actual break time. Represents the gross worked duration before OT/DT classification. Feeds payroll and client billing.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'NCCI or state-specific workers compensation classification code applicable to the work performed during this shift. Determines workers comp insurance premium calculation and is required for accurate burden rate costing.']

  Note: 'Represents a specific scheduled or worked shift instance for a worker, capturing the shift date, planned start/end, actual start/end, shift duration, break time, net hours, shift differential code, and variance from schedule. Distinct from time_entry in that it represents the shift-level aggregation and schedule comparison, while time_entry captures individual punches. Supports scheduling dispatch and labor analytics.'
}

Table timesheet.ot_rule {
  ot_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the overtime and double-time calculation rule record. Primary key for the ot_rule entity. [ROLE: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this entity defines enumerated OT/DT calculation rules used as a reference by time entry and payroll processing; exempt from per-role minimums per canonical-attrs-enforced rule.]']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: OT rules implement state-specific compliance requirements (CA daily OT, NY spread-of-hours, etc.). Each OT rule must reference the governing state compliance rule. Real business process: jurisdiction-']
  superseded_by_rule_ot_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ot_rule_id of the newer rule that replaces this rule when rule_status is superseded. Enables rule version chain tracing for historical timesheet recalculation and compliance audits. Null if this rule has not been superseded.']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: OT rules (daily/weekly thresholds, multipliers) are derived from and must be validated against wage/hour determinations (FLSA, SCA, state law). Compliance officers auditing OT rule configurations need']
  applicable_law_reference varchar [note: 'Specific statutory or regulatory citation governing this OT rule (e.g., FLSA §207, California Labor Code §510, Nevada Revised Statutes §608.018, Davis-Bacon Act 40 U.S.C. §3141). Used for compliance documentation, legal audit trails, and regulatory reporting to DOL and OFCCP.']
  applies_to_billable_hours boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OT rule governs the classification of billable OT hours for client invoicing purposes (True), in addition to payable hours for worker compensation. When True, OT bill rate multipliers are applied in Salesforce Revenue Cloud for client billing. When False, the rule applies only to payroll (pay side) calculations.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the payroll manager, compliance officer, or HR administrator who approved this OT rule configuration for activation. Required for SOC 2 Type II change management controls and FLSA compliance audit trails. Populated when rule_status transitions to active.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this OT rule configuration was formally approved for activation by the authorized approver. Distinct from effective_date (when the rule starts being applied) and created_timestamp (when the record was created). Supports change management audit trails per SOC 2 Type II.']
  bill_rate_dt_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the standard bill rate to calculate the DT bill rate charged to the client for double-time hours (e.g., 2.00 means the client is billed at 2x the straight-time bill rate for DT hours). Null if the rule does not include a DT tier or if applies_to_billable_hours is False.']
  bill_rate_ot_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the standard bill rate to calculate the OT bill rate charged to the client for overtime hours (e.g., 1.50 means the client is billed at 1.5x the straight-time bill rate for OT hours). May differ from the pay-side OT multiplier based on client MSA terms. Null if applies_to_billable_hours is False.']
  blended_rate_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the regular rate of pay for OT purposes when a worker is on multiple concurrent assignments with different pay rates. weighted_average = FLSA §207(g)(1) weighted average of all rates; highest_rate = FLSA §207(g)(2) highest rate method; primary_assignment = rate of the primary assignment governs; not_applicable = worker is on a single assignment only.']
  break_deduction_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unpaid meal/rest break deductions are applied before OT threshold calculation under this rule. True = break time is deducted from hours worked before OT is computed; False = all clocked hours count toward OT threshold without break deduction. Affects OT calculation in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OT rule record was first created in the system (ISO 8601 format with timezone offset). Provides audit trail for rule configuration history and supports SOC 2 Type II compliance logging requirements.']
  daily_dt_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked in a single workday beyond which double-time (DT) pay is triggered (e.g., 12.00 hours per California Labor Code §510). Hours worked above this threshold are compensated at the DT multiplier rate. Null if the rule does not include a daily DT threshold.']
  daily_ot_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked in a single workday beyond which daily overtime pay is triggered (e.g., 8.00 hours per California Labor Code §510). Hours worked above this threshold and below the DT threshold are compensated at the OT multiplier. Null if the rule does not apply a daily OT threshold.']
  dt_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Pay rate multiplier applied to the workers regular rate of pay for hours classified as double-time (e.g., 2.00 for double-time per California Labor Code §510). Null if the rule does not include a DT tier. Used by TempWorks Payroll for DT wage calculation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this OT rule becomes effective and begins to be applied to time entry calculations (inclusive). Used for date-range-based rule selection to ensure the correct rule version is applied to historical and current timesheets. Aligns with FLSA and state law effective dates.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this OT rule ceases to be effective (inclusive). Null for open-ended rules with no planned expiration. Used in conjunction with effective_date for date-range-based rule version management. When a rule is superseded, this date is set to the day before the new rules effective_date.']
  flsa_exemption_category varchar [note: 'Specific FLSA exemption category when flsa_exemption_status is exempt or partial_exempt (e.g., executive, administrative, professional, computer_employee, highly_compensated, 207k_public_safety). Null for non-exempt workers. Required for FLSA compliance documentation and OFCCP audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive|administrative|professional|computer_employee|highly_compensated|207k_public_safety|outside_sales|seasonal — promote to reference product]']
  flsa_exemption_status varchar [note: 'FLSA overtime exemption classification applicable under this rule. non_exempt = worker is entitled to OT pay under FLSA; exempt = worker meets FLSA white-collar or other exemption criteria and is not entitled to OT; partial_exempt = specific exemption applies to some but not all OT provisions (e.g., §207(k) for law enforcement/fire). Drives OT eligibility in TempWorks Payroll.']
  holiday_hours_ot_treatment varchar [note: 'Defines how holiday hours are treated for OT threshold calculation. count_as_worked = holiday pay hours count toward weekly OT threshold; exclude = holiday hours are excluded from OT threshold calculation; premium_separate = holiday premium is calculated separately and does not affect OT threshold. Configured per client MSA or state law.']
  job_category_scope varchar [note: 'Job category or occupational classification to which this OT rule is restricted (e.g., light_industrial, healthcare_rn, it_professional, clerical, all). Null or all indicates the rule applies across all job categories. Supports differentiated OT rules for prevailing wage or union-covered job categories.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Level of legal jurisdiction governing this OT rule. federal = applies nationwide under FLSA; state = applies within a specific U.S. state; local = applies within a specific municipality or county; contract = applies per client MSA or SOW terms regardless of geography.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this OT rule record was most recently updated (ISO 8601 format with timezone offset). Used to detect configuration changes, trigger downstream recalculation of affected timesheets, and support SOC 2 Type II audit logging.']
  ot_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Pay rate multiplier applied to the workers regular rate of pay for hours classified as overtime (e.g., 1.50 for time-and-a-half per FLSA §207). Used by TempWorks Payroll to calculate OT wages and by Salesforce Revenue Cloud to calculate OT bill amounts.']
  prevailing_wage_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OT rule incorporates prevailing wage requirements under the Davis-Bacon Act (for federal construction contracts) or the Service Contract Act (SCA) for government service contracts. True = prevailing wage OT rates apply; False = standard commercial OT rates apply. Critical for government staffing contract compliance.']
  prevailing_wage_determination_number varchar [note: 'DOL wage determination number associated with the prevailing wage schedule governing this OT rule (e.g., WD 2023-0001). Populated only when prevailing_wage_applies is True. Required for Davis-Bacon Act and SCA compliance documentation on government staffing contracts.']
  pto_counts_toward_ot boolean [note: 'Indicates whether paid time off (PTO) hours are included in the weekly hours count for OT threshold purposes. True = PTO hours count toward the OT threshold (e.g., 32 hours worked + 8 PTO = 40 hours, no OT triggered); False = only actual hours worked count toward OT threshold per FLSA standard. Configurable per client MSA.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this OT/DT rule for reference in payroll configurations, VMS integrations, and client billing systems (e.g., FLSA_FED_STD, CA_510_DAILY, DAVIS_BACON_GOV). Used as the business key in Kronos Workforce Ready and TempWorks Payroll rule tables.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the overtime rule, including the legal basis, applicable thresholds, and any special conditions (e.g., seventh-consecutive-day provisions, blended rate requirements, prevailing wage applicability). Supports compliance documentation and auditor review.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the overtime rule (e.g., Federal FLSA Standard 40-Hour Weekly OT, California Daily OT 8/12 Rule, Davis-Bacon Prevailing Wage OT). Used in payroll configuration UIs, compliance reports, and worker assignment documentation.']
  rule_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank used for conflict resolution when multiple OT rules are applicable to the same time entry (e.g., a worker in California on a government contract may have federal FLSA, California state, and Davis-Bacon rules all applicable). Lower integer = higher priority. Ensures deterministic OT rule selection in Kronos Workforce Ready and TempWorks Payroll.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the OT rule record. active = currently applied to time entry calculations; inactive = disabled and not applied; draft = under review before activation; superseded = replaced by a newer rule version but retained for historical reference.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the overtime rule by its governing authority or applicability scope. federal = FLSA-based federal standard; state = state-specific labor law (e.g., California, Nevada); client_specific = client-negotiated OT terms in MSA; prevailing_wage = Davis-Bacon Act or SCA government contract rules; blended = multi-assignment blended rate OT calculation.']
  seventh_day_dt_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'On the seventh consecutive workday, the number of hours beyond which double-time pay applies (e.g., 8.00 hours per California Labor Code §510 — first 8 hours at OT, beyond 8 at DT). Null if seventh_day_ot_applies is False or if no DT tier applies on the seventh day.']
  seventh_day_ot_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rule includes the seventh-consecutive-day OT provision, where all hours worked on the seventh consecutive day of a workweek are paid at OT rate (True) per California Labor Code §510. False for rules that do not include this provision.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this OT rule configuration was sourced or is mastered. kronos = Kronos Workforce Ready time and attendance; tempworks = TempWorks Payroll rule configuration; beeline = Beeline VMS rate and rule management; manual = manually entered by payroll or compliance team; bullhorn = Bullhorn ATS/CRM placement configuration.']
  source_system_rule_ref varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this OT rule in the originating operational system (e.g., Kronos Workforce Ready pay rule ID, TempWorks Payroll OT rule code). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system for audit and troubleshooting purposes.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state abbreviation (ISO 3166-2:US) identifying the state whose labor law governs this OT rule (e.g., CA for California Labor Code §510, NV for Nevada daily OT). Null for federal-level rules applicable nationwide. Used for multi-state OT rule resolution in Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  union_agreement_applies boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this OT rule is governed by or must comply with a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) or union contract. True = union OT provisions apply and may override standard FLSA or state rules; False = no union agreement governs this rule. Relevant for unionized light industrial and healthcare staffing placements.']
  union_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the collective bargaining agreement (CBA) that governs this OT rule when union_agreement_applies is True (e.g., SEIU Local 1199 CBA 2023-2026, Teamsters Local 25 CBA). Null when union_agreement_applies is False. Used for compliance documentation and payroll audit.']
  weekly_dt_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked in a workweek beyond which double-time pay is triggered (e.g., some state or client rules apply DT after 60 hours/week). Null if the rule does not apply a weekly DT threshold. Distinct from the daily DT threshold.']
  weekly_ot_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked in a workweek beyond which overtime pay is triggered (e.g., 40.00 hours per FLSA §207 federal standard). Hours worked above this threshold are compensated at the OT multiplier rate. Null if the rule does not apply a weekly OT threshold.']
  worker_classification_scope varchar [note: 'Worker tax classification to which this OT rule applies. w2_employee = applies only to W-2 employees on payroll; 1099_ic = applies to independent contractors (IC) per contract terms; all = applies regardless of worker classification. Aligns with IRS worker classification rules and FLSA co-employment considerations.']
  workweek_start_day varchar [note: 'The designated first day of the workweek for purposes of weekly OT threshold calculation (e.g., sunday for a Sunday–Saturday workweek per FLSA §207). Workweek definition is fixed and regularly recurring per FLSA. Configured in Kronos Workforce Ready per client or assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sunday|monday|tuesday|wednesday|thursday|friday|saturday — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining overtime and double-time calculation rules applicable to specific clients, states, job categories, or worker classifications. Captures the weekly OT threshold (e.g., 40 hrs/week federal FLSA §207), daily OT threshold (e.g., 8 hrs/day California Labor Code §510), DT threshold (e.g., 12 hrs/day California), seventh-consecutive-day rules, applicable FLSA exemption status, state-specific OT law references, effective date range, and rule priority for conflict resolution. Supports blended OT rate calculation for workers on multiple assignments. Includes prevailing wage OT rules for Davis-Bacon Act compliance on government staffing contracts. Ensures compliant OT/DT classification across all time entries in multi-state staffing operations.'
}

Table timesheet.pay_period {
  pay_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each pay period record in the staffing operation. Primary key for the timesheet_pay_period reference entity. [_canonical_skip_reason: REFERENCE_LOOKUP — this entity is a calendar/reference table defining pay period cycles; per-role minimums do not apply, but comprehensive coverage is still provided for operational completeness.]']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: State compliance rules mandate pay frequency (weekly in some states, bi-weekly in others) and pay period structure. Pay periods must reference the governing state rule for payroll compliance audits. a']
  approval_deadline timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp by which client supervisors or staffing coordinators must approve submitted timesheets. Drives SLA enforcement for approval workflows and escalation triggers in Kronos Workforce Ready. Supports on-time payroll processing and client billing cycle alignment.']
  billing_cycle_date date [note: 'The date on which client invoices are generated for billable hours worked during this pay period. May differ from check_date in cases where billing and payroll cycles are offset. Feeds Salesforce Revenue Cloud invoice generation and Oracle NetSuite Accounts Receivable.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The standard calendar year (4-digit integer, e.g., 2024) to which this pay period belongs. Distinct from fiscal_year when the business operates on a non-calendar fiscal year. Used for W-2 and 1099 tax year alignment in TempWorks Payroll.']
  check_date date [note: 'The date on which worker paychecks or direct deposit payments are issued for this pay period. Represents the actual pay date visible on W-2 wage statements and worker pay stubs. Used for tax reporting, FICA, FUTA, and SUTA liability calculations in TempWorks Payroll.']
  client_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment or client grouping for which this pay period definition is applicable (e.g., Healthcare, Light Industrial, IT Staffing, All). Supports multi-client staffing operations where different client verticals may operate on different pay cycles per MSA (Master Service Agreement) terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay period record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance. Populated automatically at record insertion and never updated.']
  dt_threshold_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked beyond which double-time (DT) pay rates apply for this pay period. Applicable in states such as California where DT is mandated after 12 hours in a workday or on the 7th consecutive workday. Supports multi-state payroll compliance in TempWorks Payroll.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this pay period definition record became effective in the system. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking for pay period calendar changes (e.g., a client switching from weekly to bi-weekly pay cycles). Enables historical payroll reprocessing and audit compliance.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this pay period definition record ceases to be effective. Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned end date. Supports SCD Type 2 history tracking for pay period calendar changes and enables point-in-time payroll audit queries.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the pay period. All timesheet hours submitted with a work date on or before this date are grouped into this pay period. Together with start_date, defines the authoritative time boundary for worker compensation and client billing.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'The fiscal month (1–12) within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs. Supports monthly financial close processes in Oracle NetSuite ERP and monthly workforce analytics reporting in Power BI Analytics Platform.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1–4) within the fiscal year to which this pay period belongs. Used for quarterly payroll tax filings (941, SUTA), financial reporting, and KPI dashboards in Power BI Analytics Platform.']
  fiscal_week_number varchar [note: 'The sequential fiscal week number (1–53) within the fiscal year. Used for weekly labor analytics, overtime threshold tracking, and workforce scheduling reports in Kronos Workforce Ready and Power BI Analytics Platform. Particularly important for weekly pay period types.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year (4-digit integer, e.g., 2024) to which this pay period belongs. Used for financial reporting, budget allocation, and year-end tax filings in Oracle NetSuite ERP. Supports multi-year analytics in Power BI Analytics Platform.']
  holiday_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period contains one or more observed holidays that affect standard workday counts, holiday pay eligibility, or payroll processing schedules. When True, payroll coordinators must review holiday pay rules and adjusted cutoff dates. Sourced from Kronos Workforce Ready holiday calendar.']
  holiday_count varchar [note: 'Number of observed holidays falling within this pay period. Used to calculate adjusted workday counts, holiday pay liability, and per diem adjustments. Supports holiday pay eligibility rules for temporary workers under client MSA (Master Service Agreement) terms.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period record is currently active and available for timesheet assignment and payroll processing. False records represent historical, future-dated, or deactivated periods. Used to filter active periods in Kronos Workforce Ready scheduling and TempWorks Payroll run selection.']
  is_current_period boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the currently active pay period (i.e., todays date falls within start_date and end_date). Only one record per period_type should have this flag set to True at any given time. Used as a quick filter in operational dashboards and payroll processing queues.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for payroll coordinators to document special instructions, exceptions, or contextual information for this pay period (e.g., Adjusted cutoff due to Thanksgiving holiday, Early check date for Christmas week). Supports operational communication and audit documentation.']
  ot_threshold_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of hours worked beyond which overtime (OT) pay rates apply for this pay period. Typically 40.00 hours for weekly FLSA overtime, but may vary by state law (e.g., California daily OT at 8 hours). Critical for payroll compliance and accurate OT cost calculations in TempWorks Payroll.']
  payroll_cutoff_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all timesheets must be submitted and approved for inclusion in this pay periods payroll run. Timesheets received after this date are held for the next pay period or processed as off-cycle adjustments. Operationally critical for TempWorks Payroll batch processing.']
  payroll_provider_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the payroll processing provider or entity responsible for executing payroll for this pay period (e.g., TEMPWORKS, EOR_PARTNER_01). Relevant for EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements where a third-party provider processes payroll on behalf of the staffing firm.']
  payroll_run_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of payroll run associated with this pay period. regular = standard scheduled payroll; off_cycle = unscheduled run for missed payments or terminations; supplemental = bonus or commission payments; correction = retroactive adjustment for prior period errors. Drives payroll processing logic in TempWorks Payroll.']
  per_diem_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether per diem allowances are applicable and tracked within this pay period. When True, per diem expense records are expected to be submitted alongside timesheets. Relevant for travel-intensive placements (e.g., healthcare travel nurses, field technicians) where IRS per diem rates apply.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this pay period across systems (e.g., PP-2024-01-001). Used for cross-system reconciliation between Kronos Workforce Ready, TempWorks Payroll, and Salesforce Revenue Cloud billing cycles.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the pay period (e.g., Week 1 Jan 2024, Bi-Weekly Period 3 - Q1 2024). Used in payroll reports, worker pay stubs, and client billing statements for clear period identification.']
  period_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number of this pay period within its fiscal year and period type (e.g., 1st bi-weekly period = 1, 2nd = 2). Used for period-over-period comparison analytics, payroll run ordering, and audit trail sequencing.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pay period. open = timesheets can be submitted; processing = payroll run in progress; locked = no further timesheet changes permitted; closed = payroll finalized and disbursed; cancelled = period voided (rare, e.g., system error). Drives workflow gating in TempWorks Payroll and Kronos Workforce Ready.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pay period cycle frequency. Weekly = 52 periods/year; bi_weekly = 26 periods/year; semi_monthly = 24 periods/year; monthly = 12 periods/year. Critical for multi-client operations where different clients may operate on different pay cycles. Drives payroll run scheduling in TempWorks Payroll.']
  pto_accrual_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PTO (Paid Time Off) accruals are calculated and posted during this pay period. Some staffing arrangements accrue PTO on a per-pay-period basis per state law (e.g., California AB 1522, New York Paid Sick Leave). Drives PTO accrual batch processing in TempWorks Payroll and SAP SuccessFactors.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this pay period definition originated. Kronos Workforce Ready is the primary source for time and attendance pay period calendars; TempWorks Payroll for payroll-specific period definitions; Beeline VMS for contingent workforce program pay cycles. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_period_ref varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this pay period record in the originating source system (e.g., Kronos pay period ID, TempWorks period key). Used for cross-system reconciliation, data lineage tracing, and integration audit trails between the lakehouse silver layer and operational systems.']
  standard_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expected number of standard straight-time hours for a full-time worker during this pay period (e.g., 40.00 for a weekly period, 80.00 for bi-weekly). Used as the baseline for overtime (OT) threshold calculations, FTE normalization, and payroll burden rate computations.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the pay period. All timesheet hours submitted with a work date on or after this date are grouped into this pay period. Authoritative boundary for timesheet aggregation and payroll processing.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The IRS tax year (4-digit integer) to which payroll taxes for this period are reported. Typically matches calendar_year but may differ for periods spanning year-end (e.g., a bi-weekly period starting Dec 28 and ending Jan 10). Critical for W-2, 1099, FICA, FUTA, and SUTA tax filing accuracy.']
  timesheet_submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp by which contingent workers must submit their timesheets for the pay period. Includes time-of-day precision to support automated reminder workflows in Kronos Workforce Ready and Bullhorn ATS/CRM. Distinct from payroll_cutoff_date which governs the payroll processing batch.']
  total_calendar_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in this pay period (end_date minus start_date plus 1). Used for pro-ration calculations for partial-period placements, per diem allowance calculations, and FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) normalization in workforce analytics.']
  total_workdays varchar [note: 'Number of standard business workdays (Monday–Friday, excluding holidays) within this pay period. Used for expected hours calculations, attendance rate reporting, and workforce scheduling in Kronos Workforce Ready. Supports SLA (Service Level Agreement) headcount compliance reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this pay period record in the data platform. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance. Updated automatically on every record modification.']
  vms_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period is associated with a VMS (Vendor Management System) managed contingent workforce program (e.g., Beeline VMS). VMS programs may have distinct pay period calendars, approval workflows, and billing cycle alignment requirements separate from direct-client placements.']
  year_end_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay period spans or falls at the calendar year-end boundary (e.g., a bi-weekly period crossing December 31 into January). When True, special year-end payroll processing rules apply including W-2 cutoff handling, FICA wage base reset, and FUTA/SUTA annual limit resets in TempWorks Payroll.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining pay period cycles used across the staffing operation. Captures pay period type (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly), period start date, period end date, payroll processing cutoff date, check date, fiscal week number, holiday adjustment flag, and active status. Serves as the authoritative calendar for timesheet grouping, payroll runs, and billing cycle alignment. Critical for multi-state operations where different clients may operate on different pay cycles.'
}

Table payroll.pay_run {
  pay_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each payroll processing cycle (pay run) executed through TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the primary key and authoritative anchor for all payroll transactions within a given cycle.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: pay_run has pay_period_start_date/end_date as plain columns but no FK to the authoritative pay_period record. Linking pay_run.pay_period_id → timesheet.pay_period is fundamental to payroll period reco']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll run was formally approved by the authorized approver. Captured for audit trail, SLA compliance, and payroll processing timeline analytics.']
  branch_code varchar [note: 'Staffing branch or cost center code associated with this payroll run. Used for branch-level payroll cost allocation, P&L reporting, and operational analytics in Power BI.']
  check_date date [note: 'Date printed on physical payroll checks or used as the effective date for direct deposit transactions. May differ from pay_date when banking processing windows shift the settlement date.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the legal employing entity or EOR entity under which this payroll run is processed. Supports multi-entity payroll operations and co-employment models where Staffing Hr acts as the employer of record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pay run record was first created in TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the audit record creation timestamp for data lineage and SOC 2 Type II compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll run. Typically USD for domestic operations; supports multi-currency for international EOR engagements.']
  disbursement_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to disburse net pay to workers in this run. Direct deposit (ACH) is standard; check and pay card are alternatives for workers without bank accounts; wire for special high-value payments.']
  gl_post_date date [note: 'Date on which this payroll runs journal entries were posted to the Oracle NetSuite ERP General Ledger. Marks the accounting period in which payroll costs are recognized.']
  ic_worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of 1099 independent contractors (ICs) included in this payroll run. Used for IRS 1099-NEC filing thresholds and worker classification compliance monitoring.']
  is_multi_state boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payroll run spans workers in more than one US state, triggering multi-state tax withholding, SUTA apportionment, and additional compliance review requirements.']
  pay_date date [note: 'Scheduled date on which worker net pay is disbursed via direct deposit, check, or pay card. Determines the tax deposit due date per IRS deposit schedule rules.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which workers are paid within this run. Drives pay period date calculations and tax withholding schedules per IRS Publication 15 annualization tables.']
  payroll_state_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of two-letter US state codes (e.g., CA,TX,NY) representing all states in which workers were paid in this run. Drives multi-state tax filing obligations and SUTA rate application.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run was finalized and posted to the General Ledger in Oracle NetSuite ERP, making it immutable for the accounting period.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when TempWorks Payroll completed the payroll calculation and tax computation processing for this run. Used for payroll operations SLA monitoring and processing time analytics.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the pay run, used in reconciliation, audit trails, and payroll operations communications. Format: PR-YYYY-MM-NNNN.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payroll run within TempWorks Payroll workflow. Progresses from open (data entry) through processing, approved, and posted (finalized to GL). Voided indicates the run was reversed.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payroll run indicating its operational purpose. Regular runs cover standard pay periods; off-cycle handles out-of-band payments; adjustment corrects prior-period errors; void-and-reissue replaces a voided run; supplemental covers bonuses or special payments.']
  tax_quarter varchar [note: 'Calendar quarter (1–4) to which this payroll run belongs, used for IRS Form 941 quarterly payroll tax filing and FUTA/SUTA quarterly reporting.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year to which this payroll runs earnings and withholdings are attributed for IRS W-2 and 1099 tax reporting purposes. Critical for year-end tax form generation.']
  total_deductions decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all voluntary and involuntary employee deductions (benefits, garnishments, 401k, health insurance premiums, etc.) withheld from worker gross pay in this run. Reconciles gross-to-net pay calculations.']
  total_dt_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of double time (DT) earnings for all workers in this run, applicable in states such as California where DT is mandated for hours exceeding 12 in a workday or 8 on the seventh consecutive day.']
  total_employer_burden decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all employer-side payroll costs beyond gross wages in this run, including employer FICA, FUTA, SUTA, workers comp premiums, and employer-paid benefits. Used to calculate the burden rate and total cost of workforce deployment.']
  total_fica_employee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of employee-side Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) withholdings (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) across all workers in this run. Reported on IRS Form 941.']
  total_fica_employer decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of employer-side Federal Insurance Contributions Act (FICA) matching contributions (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) for all W-2 workers in this run. Included in total employer burden calculation.']
  total_futa_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) employer tax accrued in this payroll run, calculated at 6.0% on the first $7,000 of each employees wages (net of SUTA credit). Filed annually on IRS Form 940.']
  total_gross_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all gross earnings for all workers in this payroll run before any deductions or tax withholdings. Includes regular pay, overtime (OT), double time (DT), per diem, and other earnings components.']
  total_net_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of net pay disbursed to all workers in this run after all tax withholdings and deductions. Represents the total cash outflow for worker compensation and is the primary reconciliation figure against bank ACH/check totals.']
  total_ot_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of overtime (OT) premium earnings for all workers in this run, calculated at 1.5x the regular rate for hours exceeding 40 per workweek per FLSA. Supports OT cost monitoring and client billing reconciliation.']
  total_per_diem_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of per diem allowances paid to workers in this run for travel, lodging, and meal reimbursements. Per diem amounts within IRS GSA rates are non-taxable; amounts above are taxable wages.']
  total_pto_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of paid time off (PTO) earnings disbursed in this payroll run, including vacation, sick, and holiday pay. Supports PTO accrual reconciliation and state-mandated paid leave compliance.']
  total_regular_pay decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of straight-time (regular rate) earnings for all workers in this run, excluding overtime, double time, and premium pay components. Used for pay rate analytics and FLSA regular rate of pay calculations.']
  total_suta_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) employer tax accrued across all applicable states in this payroll run. Rates and wage bases vary by state; critical for multi-state payroll compliance.']
  total_tax_withholding decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all employee-side tax withholdings across all workers in this run, including federal income tax, state income tax, local taxes, and employee FICA (Social Security and Medicare). Used for IRS Form 941 and state tax deposit reconciliation.']
  total_workers_comp_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of workers compensation insurance premiums accrued for all workers in this payroll run, calculated based on payroll amounts and applicable workers comp class codes and rates. Supports insurance carrier reporting and burden rate analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this pay run record was last modified in TempWorks Payroll or the Silver Layer. Supports change data capture, data lineage, and audit trail requirements.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason code for voiding this payroll run. Required when run_status = voided to support audit compliance and payroll error tracking.']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payroll run was voided, if applicable. Populated only for runs with run_status = voided. Supports payroll reversal audit trail and GL correction tracking.']
  w2_worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of W-2 (Wage and Tax Statement) employees included in this payroll run. Distinguishes co-employed temporary workers from independent contractors for tax and compliance reporting.']
  worker_count varchar [note: 'Total number of workers (W-2 employees and 1099 independent contractors) included in this payroll run. Used for headcount reporting, payroll audit reconciliation, and workforce analytics.']

  Note: 'Master record for each payroll processing cycle executed through TempWorks Payroll. Captures the payroll run header including pay period start and end dates, pay date, pay frequency, run type (regular, off-cycle, adjustment, void-and-reissue), run status (open, processing, approved, posted, voided), total gross pay, total net pay, total tax withholdings, total deductions, total employer burden, worker count, processing timestamps, and approver. Serves as the authoritative anchor for all payroll transactions within a given cycle and the primary reconciliation point for payroll operations.'
}

Table payroll.pay_stub {
  pay_stub_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each individual pay stub record. Primary key for the pay_stub data product. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — serves as the anchor for all gross-to-net pay disbursement details for a single worker in a single pay run.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the placement assignment for which this pay stub was generated. Ties earnings to the specific client engagement, job order, and work location for labor cost allocation and billing reconciliation.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Pay stubs must reference governing MSA for audit trail, co-employment determination, and compliance verification. Required for client audits, dispute resolution, and demonstrating adherence to contrac']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Payroll processing validates credential status before issuing payment; compliance holds block pay_stub generation when mandatory credentials expire. Staffing firms routinely hold paychecks until BGC/l']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Pay stubs must be validated against the credential package governing the assignment — staffing payroll audits run \'Pay Stubs by Credential Package\' to verify workers paid at premium rates hold the req']
  direct_deposit_setup_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.direct_deposit_setup. Business justification: pay_stub records ACH disbursement but has no FK to the direct_deposit_setup that governed it. Linking pay_stub.direct_deposit_setup_id → timesheet.direct_deposit_setup enables ACH return resolution an']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire employees receive pay stubs when staffing firm handles payroll during transition or under PEO arrangements. Links pay records to permanent placement for payroll continuity and compliance.']
  everify_case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.everify_case. Business justification: E-Verify case status must be resolved before continuing payroll for new hires at federal contractor clients. TNC (Tentative Non-Confirmation) cases require payroll suspension until resolution - federa']
  i9_verification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.i9_verification. Business justification: Every pay stub requires valid I-9 work authorization verification before payment issuance. Staffing firms cannot pay workers without documented work eligibility - core compliance gate for payroll proc']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC contractor payment processing requires referencing the IC agreement to validate payment_structure, payment_terms_net_days, and form_1099_reporting_flag when generating pay stubs. Staffing payroll t']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Payroll validation requires verifying worker completed onboarding before issuing first paycheck. Links pay stub to the onboarding engagement that authorized payment. Critical for compliance audits and']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Pay stubs must link to job orders for client billing reconciliation, revenue recognition by order, and job-level margin analysis. Essential for staffing P&L reporting and invoice validation against co']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: pay_stub has pay_period_start_date/end_date as plain columns with no FK to the authoritative pay_period record. Linking pay_stub.pay_period_id → timesheet.pay_period normalizes period reference, enabl']
  pay_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_rate. Business justification: A pay stub is generated based on a specific pay rate master record. Adding pay_rate_id to pay_stub creates a traceable link from the transactional pay statement back to the authoritative rate definiti']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll run batch that produced this pay stub. Enables grouping of all stubs processed in the same payroll cycle and supports reconciliation against the payroll run header.']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Staffing payroll gates pay stub issuance on compliance clearance (I-9, BGC, drug screen). Payroll auditors reference the placement_compliance_check record to confirm a worker was legally authorized to']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (candidate/employee) for whom this pay stub was generated. Links to the candidate or worker master record in the staffing system. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: W-2 workers on SOW engagements receive pay stubs. Links payroll record to SOW engagement for project cost tracking, client billing validation, and worker payment documentation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: When workers are vendor-supplied (subcontractor model), pay stubs must track the originating vendor for cost allocation, vendor invoice validation, and dispute resolution. Accounts payable reconciliat']
  timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.timesheet. Business justification: Pay stubs are generated directly from approved timesheets in staffing operations. This link enables traceability from time worked to payment issued, critical for payroll audit, dispute resolution, and']
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.wage_hour_determination. Business justification: Pay stub generation requires validated wage/hour determinations to ensure compliant overtime calculations, salary basis tests, and prevailing wage compliance. Staffing payroll systems validate each pa']
  wc_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.wc_policy. Business justification: A pay stub captures workers_comp_premium (the actual WC premium charged on this pay cycle) and workers_comp_code (the NCCI class code). Adding wc_policy_id links the pay stub to the governing WC polic']
  work_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: Work authorization validity at each pay date is a direct payroll compliance requirement — staffing firms must confirm active work authorization before disbursing pay. \'Payroll Compliance: Work Authori']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: Per diem eligibility and multi-state tax withholding on pay stubs depend on the specific work location. pay_stub carries work_state_code as a denormalized field; a direct FK to work_location enables p']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Pay stub processing depends on correct worker classification (W-2 vs 1099) to determine tax withholding obligations, FICA applicability, and reporting requirements. Classification drives payroll tax t']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Type of bank account designated for direct deposit disbursement. checking or savings as required by NACHA ACH transaction codes (PPD). Determines the ACH SEC code used in the payment file. Null for check and pay card disbursements.']
  ach_trace_number varchar [note: 'Unique 15-digit NACHA ACH trace number assigned to the direct deposit transaction for this pay stub. Null for check and pay card disbursements. Used for ACH payment tracing, return item resolution, and bank reconciliation.']
  check_number varchar [note: 'Sequential check number printed on the physical paycheck when pay_delivery_method is check. Null for direct deposit and pay card disbursements. Used for check reconciliation, stop-payment requests, and positive pay bank file matching.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay stub record was first created in the payroll system. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Used for audit trails and payroll processing SLA measurement.']
  deposit_allocation_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount or percentage value of net pay allocated to this account in a split-deposit arrangement. Populated when deposit_allocation_type is fixed_amount or percentage. Null when deposit_allocation_type is full_net.']
  deposit_allocation_type varchar [note: 'Specifies how net pay is allocated to this bank account in a split-deposit configuration. full_net = entire net pay to one account; fixed_amount = a specific dollar amount; percentage = a defined percentage of net pay. Supports multi-account direct deposit configurations.']
  deposit_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence (1 = highest priority) determining the order in which net pay is allocated across multiple bank accounts in a split-deposit configuration. Ensures correct disbursement sequencing when a worker has designated multiple accounts.']
  dt_earnings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of earnings for double-time hours (dt_hours × pay_rate × 2.0). Applicable in states with double-time mandates. Stored as a discrete earnings component for gross pay audit and state compliance reporting.']
  dt_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total double-time hours worked during the pay period, subject to the 2x pay multiplier. Applicable in states such as California with daily double-time thresholds (e.g., hours beyond 12 in a workday). Required for accurate gross pay calculation in multi-state payroll.']
  federal_income_tax_withheld decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of federal income tax withheld from the workers wages for this pay period based on W-4 elections and IRS withholding tables. Required for W-2 reporting (Box 2) and federal tax deposit obligations.']
  futa_taxable_wages decimal(10,2) [note: 'Portion of the workers gross wages subject to Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) employer tax for this pay period, up to the $7,000 annual wage base. Required for IRS Form 940 filing and employer burden rate reporting.']
  gross_pay decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total pre-deduction, pre-tax earnings for the pay period. Sum of regular_earnings, ot_earnings, dt_earnings, per_diem_amount, and any other pay components. Represents the gross-base amount in the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this transaction. Used as the basis for all tax withholding calculations.']
  medicare_tax_withheld decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employee share of Medicare (HI) tax withheld under FICA at the applicable rate (1.45%, plus 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax for high earners). Required for W-2 reporting (Box 6) and FICA deposit compliance.']
  net_pay decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final take-home amount disbursed to the worker after all deductions and tax withholdings (gross_pay − total_deductions − total_tax_withheld). Represents the net total in the MONETARY_TRIPLET. The amount actually paid via direct deposit, check, or pay card.']
  ot_earnings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of earnings for overtime hours (ot_hours × pay_rate × 1.5). Stored separately from regular earnings to support FLSA compliance reporting, overtime cost analytics, and client billing reconciliation.']
  ot_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked during the pay period, subject to the 1.5x pay multiplier under FLSA. Includes hours exceeding the standard weekly threshold or daily OT thresholds per applicable state law. Critical for gross pay calculation and FLSA compliance reporting.']
  pay_date date [note: 'The actual date on which payment was or is scheduled to be disbursed to the worker. Represents the principal business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). Used for cash flow reporting, tax period assignment, and worker payment scheduling.']
  pay_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which net pay is disbursed to the worker. direct_deposit = ACH electronic transfer to bank account; check = physical paper check; pay_card = prepaid debit card for unbanked workers. Determines downstream disbursement routing logic.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the worker is paid, determining the pay period cadence for this stub. Drives payroll scheduling, tax withholding annualization, and benefit deduction timing. Most staffing workers are paid weekly or biweekly.']
  pay_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'The workers base hourly or unit pay rate applied during this pay period, expressed in USD. Represents the agreed compensation rate from the placement rate card. Used to calculate regular earnings and validate gross pay. Classified confidential as worker compensation data.']
  pay_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the pay rate basis for this stub. hourly = standard time-based; salary = fixed periodic amount; per_diem = daily allowance-based; piece_rate = unit-of-output based. Determines gross pay calculation method and FLSA overtime applicability.']
  per_diem_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Non-taxable per diem allowance paid to the worker for travel, meals, or lodging during the pay period. Sourced from approved per diem claims. Tracked separately from taxable wages for IRS accountable plan compliance and gross pay composition.']
  prenote_status varchar [note: 'Status of the NACHA prenote (zero-dollar test transaction) process for this bank account. pending = prenote initiated; sent = prenote transmitted to bank; verified = bank confirmed account valid; failed = account validation failed. Governs whether live ACH disbursement is permitted.']
  pto_accrual_balance decimal(8,2) [note: 'Workers remaining PTO accrual balance as of this pay stubs pay period end date, expressed in hours. Snapshot value captured at time of pay run for worker statement display and leave liability reporting.']
  pto_hours_used decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of PTO hours applied to this pay stub for the pay period. Represents paid leave hours that contribute to gross pay but are not worked hours. Required for PTO accrual balance management and FLSA leave tracking.']
  regular_earnings decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of earnings for regular straight-time hours (regular_hours × pay_rate). Core component of gross pay. Stored as a raw earnings component to support gross-to-net audit trail and payroll reconciliation.']
  regular_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total straight-time hours worked by the worker during the pay period, up to the overtime threshold (typically 40 hours per week under FLSA). Sourced from approved timesheet records in Kronos Workforce Ready. Used to calculate regular earnings.']
  social_security_tax_withheld decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employee share of Social Security (OASDI) tax withheld under FICA at the applicable rate (6.2% up to the annual wage base). Required for W-2 reporting (Box 4) and FICA tax deposit compliance.']
  state_income_tax_withheld decimal(10,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of state income tax withheld for the primary work state during this pay period. Supports multi-state payroll compliance and state W-2 / equivalent reporting. May be zero for states with no income tax.']
  stub_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this pay stub as printed on the workers pay statement (e.g., PS-2024-00045231). Used for worker inquiries, audit trails, and payroll reconciliation. Satisfies BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  stub_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the pay stub within the payroll processing workflow. draft = in calculation; calculated = gross-to-net complete pending approval; approved = authorized for disbursement; issued = payment released to worker; voided = cancelled before disbursement; reversed = post-issuance correction applied. Satisfies LIFECYCLE_STATUS requirement.']
  suta_taxable_wages decimal(10,2) [note: 'Portion of the workers gross wages subject to State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) employer tax for this pay period, up to the applicable state wage base. Required for state unemployment tax filing and workforce cost burden rate calculation.']
  tax_filing_status varchar [note: 'Workers federal income tax filing status as declared on IRS Form W-4, used to determine the correct withholding amount for this pay stub. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|married_filing_jointly|married_filing_separately|head_of_household|exempt|qualifying_surviving_spouse — promote to reference product]']
  total_deductions decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount of all pre-tax and post-tax deductions applied to this pay stub, including benefits premiums, garnishments, voluntary deductions, and advances. Represents the adjustment component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Supports gross-to-net reconciliation.']
  total_tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount of all federal, state, and local taxes withheld from the workers gross pay for this pay period. Includes FICA (Social Security and Medicare), federal income tax, and applicable state/local income taxes. Key component of gross-to-net calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this pay stub record. Satisfies RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Tracks corrections, reversals, and status transitions for audit compliance.']
  w2_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this pay stub is associated with a W-2 employee (True) versus a 1099 independent contractor (False). Drives tax withholding applicability, FICA obligations, and year-end tax form generation routing in TempWorks Payroll.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'NCCI or state-specific workers compensation classification code assigned to the worker for this pay period based on job duties and work location. Drives workers comp premium calculation and is required for policy compliance and audit.']
  workers_comp_premium decimal(10,2) [note: 'Employer-paid workers compensation insurance premium allocated to this pay stub for the pay period, calculated as (workers_comp_rate × payroll_basis). Represents a key component of the employer burden rate and total labor cost.']
  ytd_gross_pay decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cumulative gross pay earned by the worker from the start of the calendar year through this pay stubs pay period end date. Displayed on the workers pay statement and used for W-2 wage box population, FICA wage base tracking, and annual compensation reporting.']
  ytd_net_pay decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cumulative net pay disbursed to the worker from the start of the calendar year through this pay stubs pay period end date. Displayed on the workers pay statement for annual earnings visibility and financial planning.']
  ytd_tax_withheld decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cumulative total of all taxes withheld from the workers pay from the start of the calendar year through this pay stubs pay period end date. Displayed on the pay statement and used for W-2 Box 2/4/6 population and tax deposit reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing the individual worker pay statement generated for each pay run, including full pay disbursement details. Captures worker identifier, pay run reference, gross pay, regular hours, overtime hours, double-time hours, per diem amounts, gross-to-net calculation details, total deductions, total tax withholdings, net pay, and stub status. Disbursement attributes: pay delivery method (direct deposit, check, pay card), check number or ACH trace number, bank routing number, account number (masked), account type (checking, savings), deposit allocation type (full net, fixed amount, percentage split), allocation amounts for split-deposit configurations, deposit priority for multiple accounts, prenote status, account verification status, and pay card routing for unbanked workers. SSOT for individual worker earnings per pay period, associated payment disbursement instructions, and banking configuration — consolidates all direct deposit and ACH details formerly tracked separately.'
}

Table payroll.pay_rate {
  pay_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the pay rate record. Primary key for the pay_rate data product in the payroll domain.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker assignment this pay rate applies to. Supports multi-rate scenarios where a worker may have different rates across concurrent assignments.']
  burden_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.burden_rate. Business justification: Pay rate structures should reference the master burden rate definition for accurate cost modeling and margin calculation. pay_rate currently has burden_rate_pct: DECIMAL(7,4) which is a denormalized v']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for which the worker is placed. Used to validate rate alignment with client rate cards and MSA terms.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Pay rates are governed by MSA-negotiated rate schedules; critical for billing compliance, margin calculation, and audit trail. Staffing operations require tracing worker pay rates to governing client ']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Pay rates must align with contracted rate cards to ensure margin integrity and prevent billing disputes. Staffing operations require direct linkage for rate validation, markup verification, and client']
  credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: Premium pay rates for licensed/certified workers (RN, CDL, PE) are validated against the specific active credential instance. \'Rate Validation by Active Credential Instance\' is a real staffing payroll']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Rate cards are tied to credential packages; different pay rates for workers with advanced certifications or security clearances. Healthcare staffing commonly pays premium rates for specialty certifica']
