  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Workers comp policies are MSA-level insurance obligations; MSAs specify minimum coverage requirements. Staffing firms must link WC policies to MSAs for compliance verification, certificate of insuran']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Workers\' comp policies are assigned by NCCI class code which maps directly to job category. Staffing firms assign WC policies per job category for premium calculation and audit compliance. wc_class_co']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Workers comp policies are jurisdiction-specific and tied to specific work locations. Required for accurate premium calculation, state compliance, and tracking which client locations are covered under']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: In EOR and co-employment MSP programs (co_employment_flag, eor_flag on wc_policy), workers\' comp policies are program-level constructs. Program-level WC insurance compliance reporting and premium allo']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.pay_run. Business justification: Workers compensation premium calculations are tied to specific payroll runs. wc_policy currently has pay_run_reference: STRING which is a denormalized reference. Adding structured FK pay_run_id → pay_']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Workers\' comp policies are governed by state-specific rules covering mandatory coverage thresholds, rate schedules, and exemptions. Staffing firms link WC policies to the governing state compliance ru']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Workers comp policies for vendor-supplied workers are often held by the vendor, not the staffing agency. Certificate of insurance (COI) tracking, premium audit, and liability determination require li']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor agreement specifies workers_comp_minimum_usd as a contractual requirement for supplier workers. WC policy compliance validation for supplier-provided workers requires referencing the vendor agr']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: Workers compensation policies are issued per work location and jurisdiction. Each site has specific WC class codes and rates. Links policy coverage to physical work location.']
  audit_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The dollar amount of premium adjustment resulting from the most recent carrier audit, representing the difference between estimated premiums paid and actual earned premium based on audited payroll. Positive values indicate additional premium owed; negative values indicate a return premium credit.']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the insurance carrier conducts a premium audit to reconcile estimated premiums against actual payroll. Staffing firms with high payroll volume often negotiate more frequent audits to reduce year-end true-up exposure.']
  base_rate_per_100 decimal(10,4) [note: 'The manual (base) WC premium rate expressed as a dollar amount per $100 of covered payroll for the associated class code and state jurisdiction, before application of the experience modification factor (EMR). Sourced from the applicable state rating bureau or NCCI.']
  burden_rate_wc_component decimal(8,6) [note: 'The WC premium cost expressed as a decimal rate applied to gross payroll, representing the WC component of the total employer burden rate. Used in bill rate calculations, spread analysis, and gross margin reporting. Derived from the modified rate and historical payroll mix for this class code.']
  carrier_naic_code varchar [note: 'The five-digit NAIC company code assigned to the insurance carrier by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners. Used for regulatory identification and carrier verification in multi-state WC compliance.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the insurance carrier or underwriter providing the workers compensation coverage under this policy. Used for carrier reporting, premium remittance, and audit correspondence.']
  client_cost_allocation_code varchar [note: 'The internal cost allocation code used to attribute WC premium costs to specific client accounts or job orders. Enables client-level WC cost reporting, profitability analysis, and accurate bill rate construction inclusive of WC burden.']
  co_employment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this WC policy record involves a co-employment arrangement where both the staffing firm and the client share employer responsibilities. Affects WC coverage determination and premium allocation between parties.']
  computed_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated WC premium dollar amount for the premium period, computed as (payroll_subject_to_wc / 100) multiplied by the modified_rate_per_100. Represents the accrued WC cost for the period and is used in burden rate application and client cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this workers compensation policy record was first created in the payroll system. Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  deductible_amount_per_claim decimal(18,2) [note: 'The per-claim deductible amount the employer is responsible for funding before the carrier pays WC benefits, applicable to large deductible programs. Zero for guaranteed cost policies. Impacts cash flow planning and loss reserve requirements.']
  deposit_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The initial deposit premium paid to the carrier at policy inception, typically a percentage of the estimated annual premium. Represents the upfront cash outlay and is reconciled against actual earned premium at audit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the workers compensation insurance policy becomes active and coverage begins. Used to determine coverage applicability for worker assignments and premium period calculations.']
  employer_liability_limit_disease_policy decimal(18,2) [note: 'The aggregate policy limit of employers liability coverage for bodily injury by disease. Distinct from the per-accident limit and the per-employee disease limit. Used for risk management reporting and MSA compliance verification.']
  employer_liability_limit_each_accident decimal(18,2) [note: 'The per-occurrence limit of employers liability coverage (Part B of the WC policy) for bodily injury by accident. Standard limits are $100,000, $500,000, or $1,000,000. Used for risk management and client contract compliance.']
  eor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this WC policy record applies to workers placed under an Employer of Record (EOR) arrangement, where the staffing firm acts as the legal employer for WC purposes. Drives co-employment risk management and premium allocation logic.']
  estimated_annual_premium decimal(18,2) [note: 'The estimated total annual WC premium for this policy as projected at policy inception, based on estimated payroll and applicable rates. Used for budgeting, cash flow planning, and comparison against actual accrued premiums during the audit cycle.']
  experience_mod_factor decimal(6,4) [note: 'The experience modification rate (EMR) assigned by NCCI or the applicable state rating bureau, reflecting the employers actual loss history relative to expected losses for the class. A factor below 1.0 indicates better-than-average loss experience; above 1.0 indicates worse. Applied to the base rate to compute the modified premium rate.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the workers compensation insurance policy expires and coverage ends. Triggers renewal workflow and is used to validate coverage during assignment management and payroll processing.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recently completed premium audit conducted by the insurance carrier. Used to track audit cycle compliance, identify overdue audits, and reconcile estimated versus actual premium balances.']
  loss_sensitive_program_type varchar [note: 'For loss-sensitive policies, identifies the specific program structure. Retrospective rated programs adjust final premium based on actual losses; large deductible programs require the employer to fund losses up to a deductible threshold. Null or none for guaranteed cost policies.']
  minimum_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The minimum premium amount guaranteed to the carrier regardless of actual payroll volume, as stipulated in the policy terms. Relevant for low-volume periods and ensures the carrier recovers minimum administrative costs.']
  modified_rate_per_100 decimal(10,4) [note: 'The effective WC premium rate per $100 of payroll after applying the experience modification factor (EMR) to the base rate. This is the rate used in actual premium calculations for payroll runs. Computed as base_rate_per_100 multiplied by experience_mod_factor.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next premium audit by the insurance carrier. Used for audit preparation planning, payroll record retention compliance, and proactive reconciliation of estimated versus actual premiums.']
  payroll_subject_to_wc decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross payroll dollar amount for the premium period that is subject to workers compensation premium calculation for this class code and state jurisdiction. Excludes non-covered remuneration per NCCI rules (e.g., tips, certain overtime premiums). Used as the basis for premium computation.']
  policy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, underwriting conditions, endorsements, or special program terms associated with this WC policy record. Used by payroll and risk management teams to document carrier-specific requirements or exceptions.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'The externally-assigned policy number issued by the insurance carrier that uniquely identifies this workers compensation insurance policy. Used for carrier reporting, premium audit reconciliation, and regulatory filings.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the workers compensation policy. Active policies are in force and covering workers; expired policies have passed their expiration date; cancelled policies were terminated before expiration; pending policies are bound but not yet effective.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the WC insurance policy structure. Guaranteed cost policies have fixed premiums; loss-sensitive policies (e.g., retrospective rated, large deductible) have premiums that vary based on actual loss experience. Drives premium calculation methodology and financial risk exposure.']
  policy_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit calendar or fiscal year of the workers compensation policy period. Used to group premium transactions for annual audit reconciliation, loss run reporting, and year-over-year cost trend analysis.']
  premium_basis varchar [note: 'The basis on which WC premiums are calculated. In staffing, the standard basis is payroll (gross remuneration subject to WC), but some specialty programs may use flat or per-capita bases. Determines how payroll_subject_to_wc is computed.']
  premium_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the payroll period for which this WC premium transaction was calculated. Together with premium_period_start_date, defines the coverage window for the premium accrual.']
  premium_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the payroll period for which this WC premium transaction was calculated. Defines the coverage window for the premium accrual and aligns with the pay run cycle.']
  schedule_credit_debit_pct decimal(6,4) [note: 'The schedule rating credit (negative value) or debit (positive value) percentage applied to the manual premium, reflecting underwriter judgment on risk characteristics not captured by the experience modification factor. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., -0.10 for a 10% credit).']
  state_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The two-letter US state code representing the state jurisdiction under which this workers compensation policy is filed and governed. Critical for multi-state WC compliance, SUTA alignment, and state-specific rate application for temporary and contract workers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this workers compensation policy record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  workers_comp_code_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the rating bureau or authority that issued the WC class code and rates for this policy record. Most states use NCCI; California uses WCIRB; a few states have independent bureaus. Critical for multi-state compliance and rate validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NCCI|WCIRB|NYBCOMP|DELAWARE|INDIANA|MICHIGAN|MINNESOTA|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  ytd_premium_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative workers compensation premium accrued from the start of the policy year through the current premium period for this class code and state jurisdiction. Used for premium audit reconciliation, carrier reporting, and financial forecasting of WC costs.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for workers compensation insurance policies and premium calculations administered through TempWorks Payroll — unified SSOT for both WC policy administration and premium accrual. Policy attributes: policy number, insurance carrier, policy effective and expiration dates, state jurisdiction, experience modification factor (EMR), policy type (guaranteed cost, loss-sensitive), premium basis (payroll-based), audit frequency, and policy status. Premium transaction attributes: WC class code, NCCI class description, state jurisdiction, payroll amount subject to WC, WC rate applied per $100 of payroll, computed premium amount, experience modification factor applied, YTD premium total, premium period, and pay run reference. Supports multi-state WC compliance for temporary and contract workers, carrier reporting, premium audit reconciliation, accurate burden rate application, and cost allocation to client assignments.'
}

Table payroll.tax_filing {
  tax_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for tax_filing']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Tax filings are subject to compliance audits by IRS, state tax authorities, and DOL. Audit scope includes payroll tax returns, deposit schedules, and withholding accuracy - audit trail links filings t']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: In EOR staffing arrangements (eor_arrangement_flag on tax_filing), tax filings are made on behalf of client accounts. Regulatory compliance and EOR cost allocation reporting require tracing tax filing']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements are specified in MSA terms and directly govern tax filing legal entity, EIN, and employer obligations. tax_filing has eor_arrangement_flag and legal_entity_name b']
  original_filing_tax_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original payroll_tax_filing_id that this amended filing corrects. Null for original (non-amended) filings. Enables amendment chain tracking for IRS audit readiness.']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll run or payroll batch that generated the tax liabilities captured in this filing. Links the tax filing back to the source payroll processing event in TempWorks Payroll. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE (to a MASTER_AGREEMENT/processing entity) for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Tax filings must comply with state-specific rules governing filing deadlines, deposit schedules, and rate requirements. Staffing firms link tax filings to the governing state compliance rule for state']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: In EOR (Employer of Record) arrangements, the supplier is the legal employer who files payroll taxes under their EIN. tax_filing must reference the EOR supplier for regulatory attribution, multi-EIN t']
  amended_filing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents an amended or corrected tax filing (True) versus an original filing (False). Amended filings (e.g., Form 941-X) correct errors in previously submitted returns and are tracked separately for audit purposes.']
  balance_due_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining tax balance owed to the tax authority after applying all deposits and credits (tax_liability_amount minus deposit_amount minus prior_deposit_amount). A positive value indicates underpayment; zero or negative indicates overpayment or credit.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Confirmation or acknowledgment number returned by the tax authority (e.g., EFTPS trace number, state portal confirmation) upon successful receipt of the tax deposit or filed return. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax filing record was first created in TempWorks Payroll and ingested into the Silver Layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this filing record (e.g., USD). Supports international operations and EOR arrangements. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual dollar amount deposited to the tax authority for this filing period. May differ from tax_liability_amount due to prior deposits, adjustments, or credits. Net amount remitted. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  deposit_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the tax deposit was transmitted to the tax authority via EFTPS or state equivalent. Used to determine timeliness and calculate any late deposit penalties. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  deposit_method varchar [note: 'Method used to transmit the tax deposit to the tax authority. EFTPS = IRS Electronic Federal Tax Payment System (mandatory for most employers); ACH = Automated Clearing House bank transfer for state deposits; check = paper check (rare); state_portal = state-specific online payment portal.']
  due_date date [note: 'Statutory deadline by which this tax deposit or return must be submitted to the tax authority to avoid penalties. Determined by IRS deposit schedule rules (semi-weekly or monthly) or statutory filing deadlines for annual/quarterly returns.']
  ein varchar [note: 'IRS-assigned Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the legal entity filing this return. Required on all federal and state payroll tax filings. In EOR (Employer of Record) and co-employment models, this may reflect the EOR entitys EIN rather than the clients EIN.']
  eor_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tax filing is processed under an Employer of Record (EOR) or co-employment arrangement (True), where the staffing firm acts as the legal employer for tax purposes on behalf of a client. Affects EIN used and liability allocation.']
  federal_income_tax_withheld decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total federal income tax withheld from worker wages and remitted to the IRS for this filing period. Reported on IRS Form 941 Line 3. Applies to W-2 employees; not applicable for 1099 independent contractors.']
  fica_employee_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee share of FICA taxes (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) withheld from worker paychecks and remitted as part of this filing. Applicable for W-2 employees; not applicable for 1099 independent contractors.']
  fica_employer_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employer matching share of FICA taxes (Social Security 6.2% + Medicare 1.45%) owed by the staffing firm or EOR entity as the employer of record. Equal to the employee FICA amount under standard rules.']
  filed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the tax return was electronically submitted to the tax authority. Provides precise audit trail for compliance and IRS/DOL audit readiness. Null if return has not yet been filed.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned to this tax filing or deposit transaction, used for reconciliation with the tax authority and TempWorks Payroll records. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  filing_period_type varchar [note: 'Frequency classification of the tax filing period. Determines deposit schedule (e.g., semi-weekly vs. monthly depositor rules under IRS Publication 15) and aligns with the form type (e.g., quarterly for 941, annual for 940).']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the payroll tax filing or deposit obligation. pending = liability identified but not yet deposited; deposited = funds transferred to tax authority; filed = return submitted; amended = corrected filing submitted; voided = filing cancelled; rejected = filing rejected by authority. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'The IRS or state tax form type associated with this filing obligation. Common values include: 941 (Employers Quarterly Federal Tax Return), 940 (Federal Unemployment Tax Act annual return), 944 (annual federal tax return for small employers), W-2 (Wage and Tax Statement), 1099-NEC (Non-Employee Compensation), state SUTA return, state income tax withholding return. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 941|940|944|W-2|1099-NEC|SUTA|state_income_tax|local_income_tax — promote to reference product]']
  futa_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Federal Unemployment Tax Act (FUTA) tax liability for this filing period. Calculated at 6.0% on the first $7,000 of each employees wages, net of SUTA credit (typically reducing effective rate to 0.6%). Reported on IRS Form 940.']
  interest_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of interest charged by the tax authority on late or underpaid tax deposits. Accrues from the deposit due date to the actual payment date. Zero if no interest assessed.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the employer entity as registered with the IRS and state tax authorities for this filing. Supports multi-entity payroll structures including EOR, PEO, and co-employment arrangements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by payroll staff regarding this tax filing, such as reasons for amendments, penalty dispute details, or special handling instructions. Supports operational context for compliance reviews.']
  overpayment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount overpaid to the tax authority for this filing period. May be applied as a credit to the next period or requested as a refund. Null or zero if no overpayment exists.']
  payroll_period_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the payroll period covered by this tax filing. Defines the end of the wage and tax liability window for this return.']
  payroll_period_start_date date [note: 'First date of the payroll period covered by this tax filing. Combined with payroll_period_end_date, defines the wage and tax liability window for this return.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of IRS or state-assessed penalties for late deposit, late filing, or underpayment of payroll taxes. Zero if no penalties assessed. Critical for compliance monitoring and SLA tracking.']
  prior_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax deposits made in prior periods within the same filing period that are applied against the current tax liability. Used for reconciliation between cumulative deposits and the final return balance due.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state or territory code (ISO 3166-2:US) for the jurisdiction of this filing. Null for federal-level filings. Critical for multi-state payroll tax compliance across all worker assignment states.']
  state_income_tax_withheld decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total state income tax withheld from worker wages and remitted to the state tax authority for this filing period. Applicable for states with income tax; null for states without state income tax (e.g., TX, FL, WA).']
  suta_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'State Unemployment Tax Act (SUTA) tax liability for this filing period. Rate and wage base vary by state and employer experience rating. Critical for multi-state staffing operations where workers are assigned across multiple states.']
  tax_authority_level varchar [note: 'Jurisdictional level of the tax authority receiving this filing: federal (IRS/DOL), state (state revenue or unemployment agency), or local (city/county tax authority). Supports multi-state payroll compliance reporting and audit readiness.']
  tax_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the federal, state, or local tax authority to which this filing or deposit is submitted (e.g., IRS, California EDD, New York State DTF, City of Philadelphia Revenue Department). Used for multi-jurisdiction payroll compliance tracking.']
  tax_liability_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax liability computed for this filing period based on taxable wages and applicable tax rates. Represents the gross amount owed to the tax authority before any prior deposits or credits are applied. Part of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  tax_quarter varchar [note: 'Calendar quarter (1–4) to which this filing applies. Applicable for quarterly filings such as IRS Form 941 and quarterly SUTA returns. Null for non-quarterly filing types.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit calendar year to which this tax filing applies (e.g., 2024). Used for annual reconciliation, W-2/1099 processing, and IRS/DOL audit readiness.']
  total_taxable_wages decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate gross taxable wages subject to the specific tax type covered by this filing for the filing period. Basis for computing the total tax liability. Excludes non-taxable fringe benefits and pre-tax deductions where applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this tax filing record, including status changes, amendments, or deposit updates. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  worker_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of workers covered by this filing: W-2 for employees subject to withholding and employer taxes, 1099 for independent contractors (IC) subject only to self-employment tax reporting, or both for filings covering mixed worker populations. Critical for IRS worker classification compliance.']
  worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of workers (W-2 employees or 1099 contractors) included in this tax filing period. Reported on IRS Form 941 Line 1 as the number of employees who received wages. Supports headcount analytics and workforce planning.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking payroll tax deposit and filing obligations submitted to federal, state, and local tax authorities. Captures filing type (941, 940, SUTA, state income tax), tax authority, filing period, total taxable wages, total tax liability, deposit amount, deposit date, filing due date, filing status (pending, deposited, filed, amended), confirmation number, and penalty/interest amounts if applicable. Supports multi-state tax compliance and IRS/DOL audit readiness.'
}

Table payroll.tax_form {
  tax_form_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each annual tax form record (W-2, W-2c, 1099-NEC, 1099-MISC) generated in TempWorks Payroll. Globally unique across all form types and tax years.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.assignment. Business justification: W-2 multi-state allocation and state tax filing require the assignment context (work state, client, job classification). tax_form has profile_id but no assignment linkage. State W-2 preparation and mu']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: W-2/1099 forms must reference MSA for co-employment determination and tax responsibility allocation. Critical for EOR/PEO arrangements where legal employer status affects tax filing obligations and de']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IRS 1099-NEC and 1099-MISC issuance for IC contractors is directly governed by the IC agreement\'s form_1099_reporting_flag and worker_classification_basis. Tax form generation for IC workers requires ']
  original_form_tax_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original tax_form record that this corrected form (W-2c or amended 1099) supersedes. Null for original (non-corrected) forms. Enables correction history chain tracking for audit and IRS reconciliation.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker or independent contractor (IC) for whom this tax form was generated. Links to the candidate/worker master record in Bullhorn ATS or TempWorks Payroll.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: 1099-NEC forms are issued to IC/SOW workers. IRS 1099 preparation requires tracing total payments to the specific SOW engagement (IC classification, total invoiced amount). A staffing tax compliance e']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: For 1099-NEC IC worker payments and W-2 EOR arrangements, the supplier is the payer/employer of record issuing the tax form. tax_form.supplier_id enables payer-of-record attribution for IRS filing, su']
  tax_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.tax_filing. Business justification: A tax_form (W-2 or 1099-NEC/MISC) is submitted to tax authorities as part of a tax filing event (annual W-2 transmittal via Form W-3, 1099 filing via Form 1096, or electronic FIRE/BSO submission). Add']
  work_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: W-2 and 1099 tax forms for visa-sponsored workers require work authorization details — nonresident alien treatment, treaty exemptions, and LCA compliance all depend on visa category. \'Tax Form Complia']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: W-2 vs. 1099-NEC tax form type is directly determined by worker classification. Staffing firms must link tax forms to the governing classification determination for IRS misclassification audit defense']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tax form record was first created in TempWorks Payroll during the year-end tax form generation process. Supports audit trail and SOC 2 compliance.']
  distribution_date date [note: 'Date the workers copy of the tax form was distributed (mailed, electronically delivered, or handed in person). Must be on or before January 31 of the year following the tax year per IRS requirements.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'Method used to distribute the workers copy of the tax form. Mail: physical paper mailed to worker address. Electronic: delivered via secure online portal (requires worker consent). In-person: handed directly to worker.']
  efile_accepted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the IRS FIRE system or SSA ACCUWAGE system accepted the e-file submission for this tax form. Null if not yet filed or if paper-filed. Critical for IRS filing deadline compliance.']
  efile_submission_reference varchar [note: 'IRS or SSA-assigned submission identifier returned upon successful e-file transmission. For W-2s: SSA ACCUWAGE submission ID. For 1099s: IRS FIRE system submission ID. Used to track filing status and retrieve acknowledgements.']
  employer_ein varchar [note: 'Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) of the staffing company or EOR entity issuing the tax form. Appears in Box c of W-2 and Payer TIN field of 1099 forms. Required for IRS e-filing and state tax agency submissions.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tax form. Draft: form generated but not yet submitted to IRS/SSA. Filed: successfully submitted. Corrected: a W-2c or amended 1099 has been issued. Voided: form cancelled. Accepted: IRS/SSA e-file acknowledgement received.']
  form_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator identifying the IRS tax form type. W-2 for W-2 employees and temporary workers; W-2c for corrected W-2s; 1099-NEC for independent contractor (IC) nonemployee compensation; 1099-MISC for miscellaneous income. Drives form-specific field population and filing rules.']
  is_corrected boolean [note: 'True if this record represents a corrected tax form (W-2c or amended 1099). The corrected checkbox is checked on the physical form. Links to the original form via original_form_id for correction history tracking.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this tax form record was most recently modified (e.g., correction applied, filing status updated, distribution recorded). Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  local_income_tax_withheld_box19 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 19: Total local income tax withheld from the workers wages. Reported to the applicable local tax authority. Null if no local tax applies.']
  local_wages_box18 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 18: Total wages subject to local income tax withholding (e.g., city or county taxes). Applicable in jurisdictions such as New York City, Philadelphia, or Ohio municipalities. Null if no local tax applies.']
  misc_other_income_box3 decimal(12,2) [note: '1099-MISC Box 3: Other income payments not reportable in other boxes, such as prizes, awards, or taxable per diem amounts exceeding IRS limits. Null for W-2 and 1099-NEC form types.']
  misc_rents_box1 decimal(12,2) [note: '1099-MISC Box 1: Rent payments of $600 or more paid to a landlord or property owner. Applicable when staffing company pays office or facility rent to a non-corporate payee. Null for W-2 and 1099-NEC form types.']
  nec_compensation_box1 decimal(12,2) [note: '1099-NEC Box 1: Total nonemployee compensation paid to an independent contractor (IC) during the tax year. Includes fees, commissions, prizes, and awards for services rendered. Null for W-2 form types. Threshold: $600 or more triggers filing requirement.']
  nec_federal_tax_withheld_box4 decimal(12,2) [note: '1099-NEC Box 4: Federal income tax withheld under backup withholding rules (24% rate) when the contractor failed to provide a valid TIN or IRS notified backup withholding is required. Null for W-2 form types.']
  paper_filed boolean [note: 'True if this tax form was submitted to the IRS/SSA via paper rather than electronic filing. Staffing companies filing 10 or more forms are required to e-file per IRS regulations. Tracks compliance with mandatory e-file thresholds.']
  payer_tin varchar [note: 'Payers Taxpayer Identification Number (TIN) as reported on 1099 forms. Typically the same as the employer EIN but may differ for EOR or co-employment arrangements where a third-party PEO is the payer of record.']
  recipient_tin_type varchar [note: 'Type of TIN used for the recipient/worker on this tax form. SSN for W-2 employees and individual contractors; EIN for contractors operating as a business entity; ITIN for foreign workers without an SSN. Drives IRS validation rules.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state abbreviation for the primary state of employment reported on this tax form (W-2 Box 15 state code). Drives state tax agency filing and multi-state payroll compliance. For workers with wages in multiple states, the primary state is recorded here.']
  state_employer_number varchar [note: 'State-assigned employer identification number used for state income tax withholding reporting (W-2 Box 15). Required for state e-filing submissions. Format varies by state.']
  state_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the state tax agency filing for this form. Not_required: state has no income tax or filing not required. Pending: prepared but not yet submitted. Filed: submitted to state agency. Accepted: state agency confirmed receipt. Supports multi-state payroll compliance tracking.']
  state_income_tax_withheld_box17 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 17: Total state income tax withheld from the workers wages. Reported to the applicable state tax agency. Null for 1099 form types or states with no income tax.']
  state_wages_box16 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 16: Total wages subject to state income tax withholding. May differ from Box 1 federal wages due to state-specific pre-tax exclusions. Supports multi-state payroll compliance. Null for 1099 form types.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'The calendar tax year (e.g., 2024) for which this form reports wages, compensation, and withholdings. Drives IRS filing deadlines and year-end reconciliation cycles.']
  w2_box12_code_a_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 12 Code A: Uncollected Social Security tax on tips. One of up to four Box 12 entries capturing pre-tax benefit and special compensation amounts. Null if not applicable.']
  w2_box12_codes varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of IRS Box 12 codes reported on this W-2 (e.g., D|DD|W). Code D = 401(k) elective deferrals; Code DD = employer-sponsored health coverage cost; Code W = HSA employer contributions; Code C = taxable cost of group-term life insurance. Supports multi-code scenarios common in staffing. Null for 1099 form types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|D|DD|E|F|G|H|J|K|L|M|N|P|Q|R|S|T|V|W|Y|Z|AA|BB|DD|EE|FF|GG|HH — promote to reference product]']
  w2_box12_total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate dollar amount across all Box 12 codes reported on this W-2. Used for year-end reconciliation of pre-tax benefit deductions (401k, HSA, health coverage). Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_federal_tax_withheld_box2 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 2: Total federal income tax withheld from the workers wages during the tax year. Reported to IRS and used by worker for federal tax return filing. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_medicare_tax_withheld_box6 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 6: Employees share of Medicare (FICA) tax withheld. Equals 1.45% of Box 5 wages, plus 0.9% Additional Medicare Tax for high earners. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_medicare_wages_box5 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 5: Total wages and tips subject to Medicare (FICA) tax. No wage base cap applies. Includes wages above the Social Security wage base. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_other_compensation_box14 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 14: Employer-reported informational amounts such as state disability insurance (SDI) withheld, union dues, educational assistance, or other items not captured in other boxes. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_retirement_plan_box13 boolean [note: 'W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker was an active participant in an employer-sponsored retirement plan (401k, SIMPLE IRA, etc.) during the tax year. Affects IRA deductibility on the workers personal return. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_ss_tax_withheld_box4 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 4: Employees share of Social Security (FICA) tax withheld. Equals 6.2% of Box 3 wages up to the wage base. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_ss_wages_box3 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 3: Total wages subject to Social Security (FICA) tax, capped at the annual Social Security wage base limit. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_statutory_employee_box13 boolean [note: 'W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker is classified as a statutory employee (e.g., certain drivers, traveling salespeople). Affects self-employment tax treatment. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_third_party_sick_pay_box13 boolean [note: 'W-2 Box 13 checkbox: True if the worker received third-party sick pay (e.g., from an insurance carrier) that is included in wages. Common in staffing for workers on short-term disability. Null for 1099 form types.']
  w2_wages_box1 decimal(12,2) [note: 'W-2 Box 1: Total taxable wages, tips, and other compensation subject to federal income tax withholding. Excludes pre-tax benefit deductions (401k, health insurance). Null for 1099 form types.']
  worker_address_city varchar [note: 'City of the workers mailing address as printed on the tax form. Used for form distribution and state tax filing jurisdiction determination.']
  worker_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the workers mailing address as printed on the tax form for distribution. Required for paper form mailing and IRS/SSA filing. Appears in Box f of W-2 and Recipient Address of 1099.']
  worker_address_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code of the workers mailing address. Drives state tax filing jurisdiction and multi-state payroll compliance rules.']
  worker_address_zip varchar [note: 'ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code of the workers mailing address as printed on the tax form.']
  worker_copy_distributed boolean [note: 'True if the workers copy of the tax form (Copy B for W-2, Copy B for 1099) has been distributed to the worker by the IRS deadline (January 31). Tracks compliance with worker distribution requirements.']
  worker_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the worker or independent contractor as it appears on the tax form (W-2 Box e or 1099 Recipient Name). Must match SSA records for W-2 employees to avoid name/SSN mismatch errors.']
  worker_ssn varchar [note: 'Workers Social Security Number (SSN) as reported on the tax form. Appears in Box a of W-2 and Recipient TIN field of 1099 forms. Masked in most operational views; full value required only for IRS e-filing and SSA submission. Governed by FCRA and IRS regulations.']
  workers_comp_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state code for the state under which workers compensation (Workers Comp) insurance was carried for this worker during the tax year. Relevant for W-2 employees on temporary assignments. Used in year-end workers comp audit reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Master record representing annual tax forms generated for both W-2 employees/temporary workers and 1099 independent contractors. For W-2 forms: captures Box 1 wages, Box 2 federal income tax withheld, Boxes 3-6 Social Security and Medicare wages and withholdings, state and local wages and withholdings, pre-tax benefit amounts (Box 12 codes), and W-2 filing status. For 1099 forms: captures 1099-NEC nonemployee compensation (Box 1), 1099-MISC miscellaneous income, backup withholding, and state income. Stores tax year, worker/contractor identifier, employer/payer EIN, form type discriminator (W-2, W-2c, 1099-NEC, 1099-MISC), filing status (draft, filed, corrected), e-file confirmation, and correction history. SSOT for ALL annual tax form reporting — unified W-2 and 1099 populations common in staffing operations where both employee and IC worker types coexist. Supports IRS e-filing, state filing, worker distribution, and year-end reconciliation.'
}

Table payroll.tax_withholding_election {
  tax_withholding_election_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each tax withholding election record. Primary key for the tax_withholding_election data product in the payroll domain.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment associated with this withholding election, enabling assignment-level tax configuration for multi-assignment workers.']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire employees submit W-4 elections when staffing firm processes payroll under PEO or transition arrangements. Links tax elections to permanent placement for withholding compliance.']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC agreement\'s worker_classification_basis directly governs withholding election setup — true IC workers are typically exempt from withholding. Staffing payroll teams must reference the IC agreement w']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Tax withholding elections are collected during onboarding. Linking to onboarding engagement supports IRS compliance verification that W-4/state tax forms were obtained before first paycheck, required ']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (W-2 employee or 1099 independent contractor) whose tax withholding elections are captured in this record. Links to the candidate or worker master record.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW/IC workers have distinct withholding elections (backup withholding for 1099, W-2 for EOR SOW). Tax withholding elections must reference the SOW engagement to determine correct tax treatment. IRS b']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Withholding elections must comply with state-specific rules (mandatory withholding states, flat rate states, exemption eligibility). Staffing firms link withholding elections to the governing state co']
  superseded_by_election_tax_withholding_election_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer tax_withholding_election record that replaced this one. Populated when election_status = superseded. Enables forward-chaining of the election history for audit trails.']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Tax withholding elections (W-4) are only applicable to W-2 employees, not independent contractors. Worker classification determines withholding eligibility and form type - misclassified workers trigge']
  allowances_claimed varchar [note: 'Number of withholding allowances claimed by the worker on pre-2020 W-4 forms. Each allowance reduces the amount of federal income tax withheld. Applicable only for W-4 forms prior to the 2020 redesign. Null for 2020+ W-4 forms which use the step-based approach.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the payroll supervisor or manager who reviewed and approved this withholding election entry. Required for IRS lock-in letter overrides and exempt status elections as part of internal controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this withholding election record was first created in the payroll system. Supports audit trail, SOC 2 Type II compliance, and IRS record retention requirements for withholding forms.']
  deductions_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of itemized or other deductions entered in Step 4b of the 2020+ W-4. Reduces withholding by accounting for deductions that exceed the standard deduction. Null for pre-2020 W-4 forms.']
  dependent_credit_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of qualifying dependent credits entered in Step 3 of the 2020+ redesigned W-4. Reduces the amount of income subject to withholding. Includes child tax credit ($2,000 per qualifying child under 17) and other dependent credits ($500 per other dependent). Null for pre-2020 W-4 forms.']
  document_reference_code varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the physical or electronic withholding form document stored in the document management system (e.g., DocuSign CLM or SAP SuccessFactors document repository). Enables retrieval of the original signed form for audit and compliance verification.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this withholding election becomes effective and begins driving tax withholding calculations in pay runs. Aligns with IRS requirement that new W-4 elections take effect no later than the start of the first payroll period ending on or after the 30th day after the form is received.']
  election_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by payroll staff regarding this withholding election. May include reasons for IRS lock-in overrides, documentation of verbal instructions, or notes on unusual withholding configurations.']
  election_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for this withholding election record, used for audit trails, payroll processing references, and worker communications (e.g., TWE-2024-00123).']
  election_source varchar [note: 'The channel or method through which the withholding election was submitted. Paper form = physical W-4 or state form submitted; Electronic self-service = worker submitted via online portal (e.g., SAP SuccessFactors ESS); Employer default = system default applied when no form received; IRS lock-in = IRS-mandated withholding rate overriding worker election; Verbal instruction = documented verbal request.']
  election_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this withholding election. Active = currently driving withholding calculations; Superseded = replaced by a newer election; Pending = submitted but not yet effective; Cancelled = voided before taking effect; Expired = past the expiration date.']
  election_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for withholding elections for the same worker and jurisdiction. Increments each time the worker submits a new withholding form. Enables tracking of election history and supersession chain for audit and compliance purposes.']
  entered_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee ID of the payroll staff member or system process that entered this withholding election into TempWorks Payroll. Supports audit trail requirements for payroll compliance and SOC 2 Type II controls.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this withholding election expires and is no longer valid. Applicable for exempt-from-withholding elections which expire annually (February 15 of the following year per IRS rules), or for state-specific elections with defined validity periods.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'The workers federal or state tax filing status as declared on the withholding form. Drives the applicable standard deduction and tax bracket used in withholding calculations. Values align with IRS filing status categories.']
  flat_withholding_rate_pct decimal(5,4) [note: 'Fixed percentage rate applied to gross wages for income tax withholding when the withholding method is percentage or flat_amount. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.2200 = 22%). Used for supplemental wage withholding at the IRS flat supplemental rate or for state flat-rate withholding.']
  form_type varchar [note: 'The specific tax withholding form type submitted by the worker. Examples include W-4 (federal), W-4P (pension), state equivalents (e.g., CA DE-4, NY IT-2104, IL W-4, TX no state income tax). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: W-4|W-4P|W-4S|W-4V|DE-4|IT-2104|IL-W-4|VA-4|NC-4|GA-G-4 — promote to reference product]']
  irs_lock_in_allowances varchar [note: 'The maximum number of withholding allowances permitted by the IRS lock-in letter, overriding the workers own W-4 election. Employer must not allow the worker to claim more allowances than this value.']
  irs_lock_in_filing_status varchar [note: 'The filing status mandated by the IRS lock-in letter, overriding the workers own W-4 election. Populated only when an IRS lock-in letter is in effect. Employer must use this status for withholding calculations.']
  irs_lock_in_letter_date date [note: 'Date of the IRS lock-in letter received by the employer mandating a specific withholding rate for this worker. When present, the employer must withhold at the IRS-specified rate regardless of the workers W-4 elections. Null if no lock-in letter applies.']
  is_exempt_from_withholding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker has claimed exemption from federal or state income tax withholding. True = worker claims exempt status and no income tax will be withheld. Exempt status expires February 15 each year and must be renewed. Employer must retain the exempt W-4 and notify IRS if required.']
  is_state_exempt_from_withholding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker has claimed exemption from state income tax withholding on the state withholding form. Exempt criteria and renewal requirements vary by state.']
  jurisdiction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this election applies to federal, state, or local tax withholding. Federal elections are governed by IRS W-4; state elections by equivalent state withholding forms; local elections by municipality-specific forms.']
  locality_name varchar [note: 'Name of the local taxing jurisdiction (city, county, or municipality) for local income tax withholding elections. Applicable in states with local income taxes such as Ohio, Pennsylvania, Kentucky, and New York City.']
  multiple_jobs_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker checked the Step 2 multiple jobs checkbox on the 2020+ W-4, signaling they hold multiple concurrent jobs or their spouse also works. When true, withholding is calculated using the higher withholding rate tables to prevent under-withholding.']
  nonresident_alien_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is a nonresident alien (NRA) for U.S. tax purposes. NRA workers are subject to special withholding rules under IRS Publication 15 and must follow specific W-4 instructions (cannot claim exempt, must add an additional amount to wages for withholding calculation). Relevant for staffing firms placing international workers.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The pay frequency associated with this withholding election, used to annualize wages for withholding table lookups. Must match the workers actual pay frequency in the pay run. Drives the correct withholding amount per period.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the withholding election form was received by the employer or payroll department. Used to determine the required effective date per IRS rules (must take effect no later than the first payroll period ending on or after the 30th day after receipt).']
  state_additional_withholding_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Additional flat dollar amount the worker requests to be withheld for state income tax beyond the calculated state withholding. Applied per pay period in addition to the standard state withholding calculation.']
  state_allowances_claimed varchar [note: 'Number of withholding allowances claimed on the state withholding form. Many states still use an allowance-based system independent of the federal W-4 redesign (e.g., California DE-4, New York IT-2104). Null for states with no income tax or states that have adopted the federal W-4.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter USPS state abbreviation identifying the state jurisdiction for state or local withholding elections. Null for federal elections. Supports multi-state payroll compliance across all states where Staffing Hr places workers.']
  state_filing_status varchar [note: 'The workers filing status as declared on the state withholding form, which may differ from the federal filing status. State-specific values vary by jurisdiction (e.g., California uses single, married, head of household with different withholding tables). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|married|married_higher_rate|head_of_household|exempt — promote to reference product]']
  supplemental_wage_method varchar [note: 'Method used to withhold income tax on supplemental wages (bonuses, commissions, overtime, per diem above IRS threshold). Flat rate = mandatory 22% federal flat rate for supplemental wages under $1M; Aggregate = combined with regular wages using withholding tables; Optional flat = employer elects flat rate when not mandatory.']
  treaty_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country with which the worker claims a U.S. tax treaty benefit, potentially reducing or eliminating withholding on certain income types. Applicable for nonresident alien workers claiming treaty exemptions via Form 8233 or W-8BEN.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this withholding election record was last modified. Tracks changes to election data for audit trail and data lineage purposes in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  w4_form_version_year varchar [note: 'The tax year version of the W-4 or equivalent withholding form submitted by the worker. Critical because the 2020 redesigned W-4 eliminated allowances and introduced a new step-based structure. Pre-2020 forms use allowances; 2020+ forms use Step 3 dependent credits and Step 4 adjustments.']
  withholding_method varchar [note: 'The calculation method used to compute income tax withholding for this worker. Percentage method uses IRS Publication 15-T tables; Wage bracket method uses bracket tables; Flat amount applies a fixed dollar amount; Annualized projects annual income for withholding; Cumulative adjusts for year-to-date withholding.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the worker for tax withholding purposes. W-2 = employee subject to income tax withholding and FICA; 1099 = independent contractor (IC) not subject to employer withholding; EOR = Employer of Record arrangement where the EOR entity handles withholding. Drives whether withholding elections are applicable.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing a workers federal and state tax withholding elections as submitted via W-4 (federal) and equivalent state withholding forms. Stores filing status (single, married, head of household), number of allowances or Step 3 dependent credits (post-2020 W-4), additional withholding amount, exempt from withholding flag, state withholding form type, state-specific elections, effective date, and election source (paper form, electronic self-service). Drives tax withholding calculation in each pay run.'
}

Table payroll.task_checklist {
  task_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the task checklist template. Primary key.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific client account if this is a client-specific checklist template. Nullable for general templates.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSA specifies background_check_required_flag, drug_screen_required_flag, everify_required_flag, and i9 requirements that directly drive task checklist configuration. Staffing onboarding teams build cl']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Onboarding task checklists are governed by credential packages — the package defines which BGC, drug screen, and credentialing tasks are required. Direct FK replaces denormalized boolean flags (requir']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: Onboarding checklists are configured by job category - healthcare roles require medical credentialing tasks, IT roles require system access provisioning, construction roles require safety certificatio']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Client-specific onboarding checklists vary by work site characteristics (union facilities, secure locations, remote sites). Staffing operations require location-specific task templates for compliance ']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP managed programs define program-specific onboarding compliance requirements. Task checklists (compliance_driven_flag, mandatory_flag) must be linked to the managed program to enable program-level ']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Onboarding and placement task checklists are configured based on state compliance rules (e.g., California-specific I-9 timing, state drug screen laws, paid sick leave notice requirements). Linking tas']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor agreements specify background_check_required_flag, drug_screen_required_flag, and everify_required_flag for supplier-provided workers. Task checklists for supplier worker onboarding must enforc']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.vms_program. Business justification: VMS programs specify compliance requirements (bgc_required, drug_screen_required, everify_required on vms_program) that directly drive task checklist configuration (requires_bgc_flag, requires_drug_sc']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: Onboarding task checklists are location-specific: site safety orientation, drug screen protocols, and badge requirements vary by work location. task_checklist has location_id (→client.location) but pl']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the checklist template: active (in use), inactive (temporarily disabled), draft (under development), or archived (retired but retained for historical reference).']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this checklist requires formal approval or sign-off before the worker can start assignment (True) or is informational only (False).']
  checklist_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the checklist template for system integration and reporting (e.g., HC-TEMP-ONB, IT-CTR-ONB).']
  checklist_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the task checklist template (e.g., Healthcare Temp Onboarding, IT Contractor Onboarding, Light Industrial Assignment End).']
  checklist_type varchar [note: 'Category of the checklist template indicating the business process it supports: onboarding (new worker start), offboarding (assignment end), redeployment (worker reassignment), or extension (assignment continuation).']
  client_specific_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist template is customized for a specific client account (True) or is a general template applicable across multiple clients (False).']
  compliance_driven_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist is driven by regulatory or compliance requirements (True) such as I-9, E-Verify, background checks, or is purely operational (False).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this checklist template version was first created in the system.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Business division or vertical this checklist is designed for (e.g., Healthcare, IT, Light Industrial, Professional). Nullable if checklist is division-agnostic.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this checklist template version becomes active and available for use in creating task assignments.']
  estimated_completion_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of business days required to complete all tasks in this checklist under normal circumstances. Used for planning and Service Level Agreement (SLA) management.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this checklist template version is no longer active. Nullable for templates with indefinite validity.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this checklist is mandatory (True) for the associated placement type and scenario, or optional (False).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this checklist template version.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this checklist template version was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or context about this checklist template that does not fit structured fields.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Business role or department responsible for maintaining and updating this checklist template (e.g., Onboarding Team, Compliance Department, Credentialing Team).']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Type of placement this checklist applies to: temporary, temp-to-perm, contract-to-hire, direct placement, or independent contractor (IC). Determines applicable compliance and onboarding requirements.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level of this checklist template indicating urgency and resource allocation: critical (blocking), high (time-sensitive), medium (standard), or low (best-effort).']
  requires_equipment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist includes equipment provisioning tasks (laptop, phone, badge, tools, Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), etc.).']
  requires_everify_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist includes E-Verify electronic employment eligibility verification as a required task, typically for federal contractors or specific state requirements.']
  requires_i9_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist includes I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification as a required task, per Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) requirements.']
  requires_lms_training_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist includes Learning Management System (LMS) training assignments via SAP SuccessFactors or similar platform.']
  requires_orientation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist includes worker orientation or onboarding training as a required task.']
  sla_hours varchar [note: 'Service Level Agreement (SLA) target in hours for completing all tasks in this checklist from initiation. Used for performance measurement and client commitments.']
  task_checklist_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the checklist template purpose, scope, and intended use cases. Provides context for users selecting appropriate templates.']
  task_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual tasks defined within this checklist template. Used for progress tracking and workload estimation.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this checklist template. Incremented when template is revised to track changes over time and support audit trails.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this checklist template version.']

  Note: 'Reusable template defining a named collection of required onboarding or offboarding tasks for a specific scenario (e.g., temp healthcare onboarding, IT contractor onboarding, light industrial assignment end). Includes applicable placement type, division, client-specific flag, version number, effective date, and active status. Task_assignment records are instantiated from these templates for each worker engagement. Driven by requirement_rule configuration.'
}

Table payroll.document_collection {
  document_collection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for document_collection']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the placement or assignment associated with this document collection, if applicable.']
  credential_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_document. Business justification: Onboarding document collection references credentialing documents (licenses, certifications, BGC reports) for unified document management and compliance verification. Enables single source of truth fo']
  direct_hire_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.direct_hire. Business justification: Direct hire onboarding requires document collection (offer letter, I-9, W-4, background check consent). document_collection links to assignment but not direct_hire. Direct hire compliance audits and o']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC agreements are collected as onboarding documents. Document_collection tracks IC agreement execution status, version control, signature dates, storage location. Essential for IC agreement document l']
  nda_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.nda. Business justification: NDAs are collected during onboarding (candidate NDAs) and offboarding (exit confidentiality agreements). Document_collection tracks NDA execution, expiration dates, storage. Critical for confidentiali']
  offboarding_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.offboarding_record. Business justification: Offboarding requires document collection (exit paperwork, equipment return receipts, final timesheets). This links documents to the offboarding workflow and enables tracking offboarding completion sta']
  onboarding_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to recruitment.onboarding_engagement. Business justification: Document collection is a primary onboarding activity. Linking documents to the onboarding engagement enables tracking document completion status as part of overall onboarding progress and supports onb']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Document collections (I-9, tax forms, NDAs, credentials) are gathered as part of the placement compliance check process. Staffing firms link collected documents to the governing compliance check recor']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the worker (candidate or employee) for whom this document was collected during onboarding or offboarding.']
  sow_engagement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.sow_engagement. Business justification: SOW engagements require IC agreement execution, NDA, and compliance documents (W-9, COI). document_collection links to assignment but not sow_engagement. IC compliance audits and SOW onboarding comple']
  task_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.task_checklist. Business justification: A document_collection record represents a specific document collected, signed, or acknowledged during onboarding or offboarding. The task_checklist is the reusable template that defines which document']
  tax_withholding_election_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payroll.tax_withholding_election. Business justification: A document_collection record for a W-4 or state equivalent tax withholding form directly produces a tax_withholding_election record. Adding tax_withholding_election_id to document_collection creates t']
  allowances_claimed varchar [note: 'Number of withholding allowances or exemptions claimed on tax form (W-4 or state withholding). Applicable to tax form document types. Used by TempWorks Payroll for withholding calculation. Note: Post-2020 W-4 uses different methodology; this field supports legacy forms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document collection record was first created in the system.']
  document_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the document collected (e.g., Master Service Agreement Acknowledgment, Non-Disclosure Agreement, W-4 Federal Withholding).']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document in the collection and signing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|sent|viewed|signed|completed|declined|expired|voided — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of document collected during onboarding or offboarding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: msa_acknowledgment|nda|non_compete|handbook_acknowledgment|safety_policy|drug_screen_consent|bgc_authorization|fcra_disclosure|offer_letter|assignment_confirmation|w4|state_withholding|w9|1099_setup|i9|direct_deposit_form|emergency_contact_form|equipment_acknowledgment|confidentiality_agreement|code_of_conduct|pto_policy|benefits_enrollment|cobra_notice|final_paycheck_acknowledgment|exit_interview|property_return_form — promote to reference product]']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the document template or form used (e.g., v2.1, 2024-Q1), ensuring compliance with the correct policy or legal version.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the document becomes effective or binding, particularly relevant for tax forms (W-4, state withholding) and policy acknowledgments.']
  exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker claimed exempt status from federal or state withholding on the tax form (True = exempt, False = not exempt). Applicable to W-4 and state withholding document types.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the document expires and may need to be renewed or re-signed (e.g., annual policy acknowledgments, time-limited consents).']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Federal tax filing status declared on W-4 form. Applicable only to W-4 document type. Used by TempWorks Payroll for federal withholding calculation.']
  form_submission_date date [note: 'Date the worker submitted the completed form to the employer or staffing agency. May differ from signed_date for forms that require additional processing or manual submission.']
  is_electronic_signature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document was signed electronically via DocuSign or similar platform (True) or via wet signature/manual process (False).']
  is_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is mandatory for the worker to complete onboarding or offboarding (True) or optional (False).']
  onboarding_phase varchar [note: 'Phase of the onboarding or offboarding lifecycle during which this document was collected (e.g., pre-hire, post-offer, first day, orientation, assignment start, offboarding).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason the document was rejected or declined, if applicable (e.g., incomplete information, incorrect form version, illegible signature).']
  review_date date [note: 'Date the document was reviewed and approved by HR or compliance staff.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the reviewer regarding the document review, any issues identified, or follow-up actions required.']
  sent_date date [note: 'Date the document was sent to the worker for review or signature.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the worker signed or acknowledged the document, completing the signature workflow.']
  signer_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the worker signed the document electronically, captured for audit and compliance purposes.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System or platform from which the document was originated or collected (e.g., DocuSign CLM, SAP SuccessFactors, TempWorks Payroll, manual upload).']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical or digital storage location where the signed document is archived (e.g., cloud storage URI, document management system path, file share location).']
  supersedes_prior_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document supersedes or replaces a previously submitted document of the same type (True) or is a new submission (False). Particularly relevant for tax forms (W-4, state withholding) when workers update their withholding elections.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Tax year for which the tax form document (W-4, state withholding, W-9, 1099 setup) is applicable. Null for non-tax documents.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this document collection record was last modified or updated.']
  viewed_date date [note: 'Date the worker first viewed or opened the document.']

  Note: 'Tracks each document collected, signed, or acknowledged during the onboarding or offboarding process. Covers document type (MSA acknowledgment, NDA, non-compete, handbook acknowledgment, safety policy, drug screen consent, BGC authorization, FCRA disclosure, offer letter, assignment confirmation, W-4, state withholding, W-9, 1099 setup), document source system (DocuSign CLM, SAP SuccessFactors, TempWorks, manual upload), DocuSign envelope ID, signed date, expiration date, storage location reference, document status, and tax-form-specific fields (tax year, filing status, allowances or exemptions claimed, additional withholding amount, effective date, supersedes prior flag, form submission date). Consolidates all onboarding document types including tax form submissions into a single collection entity differentiated by document_type. Feeds payroll tax configuration in TempWorks Payroll for tax form document types (W-4, state withholding, W-9, 1099).'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the invoice record in the Staffing Hr billing system. Primary key for the billing.invoice data product.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing placement or assignment that generated this invoice. Applicable for time-and-materials and per-diem billing models. Null for SOW-based or direct placement fee invoices.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: An invoice is issued to a client\'s billing account — the financial entity that owns the AR relationship. billing_account is the SSOT for payment terms, credit limits, invoice delivery preferences, and']
  billing_contact_client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: Invoices in staffing are addressed and delivered to a specific client billing/AP contact. Knowing which contact received each invoice is essential for dispute initiation, delivery confirmation, and co']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Invoices must reference governing MSA for payment terms, liability caps, dispute resolution, and indemnification scope. Critical for AR collections, legal disputes, and payment processing. MSA defines']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC invoices are governed by IC agreements specifying invoice_frequency and payment_terms_net_days. Linking invoice to ic_agreement enables IC billing compliance, 1099 reporting accuracy, and payment t']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP managed programs require program-level invoice aggregation for spend reporting, MSP fee calculation, and program P&L. billing_rate_schedule and bill_rate already carry managed_program_id; the invo']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Staffing billing teams must trace every invoice to its originating job order for revenue reconciliation, client billing audits, and AR reporting. An invoice is generated against a specific order; with']
  original_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice that this invoice replaces in a void-and-rebill scenario. Populated on the replacement invoice to maintain the audit chain linking corrected invoices back to their originals. Null for original invoices not created as part of a rebill.']
  pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.pay_period. Business justification: Staffing invoices are generated per billing/pay period. Linking invoice to pay_period enables period-based billing reconciliation — a core staffing finance operation where AR teams reconcile all invoi']
  pay_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payroll pay run associated with this invoice, enabling reconciliation between billing and payroll for spread and gross margin analysis.']
  placement_compliance_check_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.placement_compliance_check. Business justification: Compliance-gated invoicing is a standard staffing process: invoices are held until placement compliance checks clear.
