  list_b_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the List B document as recorded in Section 2. Null if the document has no expiration date.']
  list_c_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number from the List C employment authorization document presented in Section 2. Recorded by the employer/authorized representative during physical document inspection.']
  list_c_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of List C document (establishes employment authorization only) presented by the employee in Section 2 when no List A document is provided. Examples include Social Security card, birth certificate, employment authorization issued by DHS. Must be used in combination with a List B document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: social_security_card|us_birth_certificate|us_citizen_id_card|native_american_tribal_doc|us_id_card_for_use_in_us|dhs_employment_auth — promote to reference product]']
  list_c_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the List C document as recorded in Section 2. Null if the document has no expiration date.']
  onboarding_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system from which this I-9 record was sourced or initiated. Supports data lineage and reconciliation between SAP SuccessFactors onboarding workflows and Sterling BGC verification system.']
  preparer_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a preparer or translator assisted the employee in completing Section 1 of the I-9 form. When True, the preparer/translator certification fields must also be completed.']
  remote_verification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the I-9 document examination was conducted remotely using the DHS-authorized alternative procedure (video link, fax, or email) rather than in-person physical inspection. Remote verification requires additional documentation and is subject to specific DHS authorization.']
  reverification_due_date date [note: 'Date by which Section 3 reverification must be completed to maintain continuous employment authorization compliance. Typically set to the work authorization expiration date or document expiration date, whichever is earlier.']
  reverification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this I-9 record requires reverification of employment authorization documents before the work authorization expiration date. Drives compliance workflow alerts and Section 3 completion.']
  section1_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee completed and signed Section 1 (Employee Information and Attestation) of the I-9 form. Must be completed no later than the first day of employment per INA requirements.']
  section2_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the employer or authorized representative completed Section 2 (Employer or Authorized Representative Review and Verification) of the I-9 form. Must be completed within 3 business days of the hire date.']
  section3_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which Section 3 (Reverification and Rehires) was completed. Populated when a worker is rehired within 3 years of the original I-9 date or when work authorization documents require reverification.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (SAP SuccessFactors, Sterling BGC, or Bullhorn) for this I-9 record. Enables cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the Silver layer.']
  tnc_contested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee chose to contest the Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC). If True, the employee has 8 federal government working days to contact SSA or DHS to resolve the discrepancy.']
  tnc_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC) was issued by E-Verify for this case. When True, the employer must notify the employee and provide the Further Action Notice. Triggers a mandatory contest window.']
  tnc_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the employer notified the employee of the Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC) result. Employer must notify the employee promptly and cannot take adverse action during the contest period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this I-9 verification record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change detection in the Silver layer lakehouse. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the I-9 verification record. Tracks progression from initial employee attestation through employer document review to final closure or reverification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|section1_complete|section2_complete|reverification_required|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  work_auth_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the employees work authorization expires as attested in Section 1. Drives reverification scheduling. Null for U.S. citizens, nationals, and lawful permanent residents with no expiration.']

  Note: 'Master record for I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification for each worker. Tracks Section 1 (employee attestation), Section 2 (employer document review), Section 3 (reverification/rehire), document types presented, document numbers, expiration dates, E-Verify case number linkage, completion status, and re-verification due dates. SSOT for I-9 compliance per INA requirements. Sourced from onboarding workflows and Sterling BGC system.'
}

Table compliance.everify_case {
  everify_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each E-Verify case record in the Staffing Hr compliance data product. Primary key for the everify_case table in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing placement or assignment record for which this E-Verify case was initiated. Connects compliance verification to the specific job placement in Bullhorn ATS.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: E-Verify requirements are contractually mandated in MSAs, especially for federal contractors. Audit trail links E-Verify cases to MSA obligations for compliance reporting and client verification.']
  i9_verification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification record that triggered this E-Verify case submission. Links the E-Verify case to the underlying I-9 form completed during onboarding.']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: E-Verify cases for IC workers are tied to the ic_agreement\'s everify obligations and employer_of_record designation. Staffing compliance teams must link E-Verify cases to the governing IC agreement to']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate (worker) whose employment eligibility is being verified through this E-Verify case. Sourced from Bullhorn ATS candidate record.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOW-level E-Verify requirements (e.g., specific federal projects) drive case creation. Real business need: SOW compliance reporting and client-specific E-Verify audit trails.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: E-Verify cases may be processed by third-party compliance vendors or managed service providers. Track supplier for SLA monitoring, dispute resolution, case quality assessment, and vendor invoice recon']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: E-Verify cases for vendor-supplied workers are governed by the vendor_agreement\'s everify_required_flag and federal_contractor_flag. Staffing compliance teams must link E-Verify cases to the vendor_ag']
  verified_credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: E-Verify cases validate specific work authorization documents tracked as credential instances. Business process: E-Verify document validation and photo match reference the actual credential instance b']
  work_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.work_authorization. Business justification: E-Verify cases are initiated to verify the work authorization documents submitted during I-9. The work_authorization product has everify_case_number as a plain attribute — a denormalization signal. Li']
  case_closure_date date [note: 'The date on which the E-Verify case was officially closed in the federal portal. Marks the end of the case lifecycle. Used for compliance reporting, audit trails, and calculating case processing duration.']
  case_closure_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for closing the E-Verify case. Captures whether closure was due to authorization, final nonconfirmation, early termination, case error, or other administrative reasons. Required for federal audit trails and DOL regulatory reporting.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the E-Verify case as returned by the DHS/SSA federal portal. Drives compliance workflow actions including TNC resolution, referral, and closure processes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Employment Authorized|Tentative Nonconfirmation|Final Nonconfirmation|Case in Continuance|Closed|DHS Verification in Process — promote to reference product]']
  case_verification_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of E-Verify verification being performed. New Hire cases are standard; Rehire cases apply when a worker is rehired after a break in service; Federal Contractor cases apply under FAR E-Verify clause requirements for federal contract placements.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to document case-specific notes, exceptions, escalations, or remediation actions taken during the E-Verify process. Used for internal audit documentation and legal hold purposes. Sourced from Sterling BGC and manual compliance team entries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this E-Verify case record was first created in the Staffing Hr compliance data platform (Silver Layer). Represents the data ingestion audit trail timestamp, distinct from the case submission timestamp. Required for SOC 2 Type II audit controls.']
  dhs_case_number varchar [note: 'The official case number assigned by the DHS/SSA E-Verify federal portal upon case creation. This is the externally-known identifier used to track and reference the case with federal authorities. Sourced from Sterling BGC and E-Verify federal portal integration.']
  document_title_list_used varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the worker presented a List A document (establishes both identity and employment authorization) or a combination of List B (identity) and List C (employment authorization) documents during I-9 verification. Relevant to E-Verify case processing rules.']
  employee_first_day_of_work date [note: 'The date the employee began work for pay at Staffing Hr or the client site. E-Verify cases must be submitted within 3 business days of this date per federal program rules. Used to validate timely submission compliance.']
  employer_case_creator varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the Staffing Hr staff member (recruiter, onboarding specialist, or compliance officer) who created and submitted the E-Verify case. Required for internal audit trails and accountability tracking per SOC 2 Type II controls.']
  employment_authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the workers employment authorization document as recorded in the E-Verify case. Triggers re-verification requirements for workers with temporary employment authorization. Critical for ongoing compliance monitoring and assignment management.']
  federal_closure_code varchar [note: 'The standardized federal closure code assigned by the DHS E-Verify portal upon case closure. Used for regulatory reporting, federal audit reconciliation, and compliance tracking with DHS and DOL requirements.']
  federal_contractor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this E-Verify case was submitted under the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) E-Verify clause requirement for federal contractor placements. True = federal contractor case; False = standard commercial placement. Drives OFCCP compliance reporting.']
  final_case_result varchar [note: 'The conclusive result of the E-Verify case after all resolution steps (TNC contest, referral, continuance) have been completed. Used for final compliance determination and regulatory reporting to DOL and EEOC.']
  further_action_notice_date date [note: 'The date on which the Further Action Notice (FAN) was provided to the worker. Must be documented as part of the TNC resolution process. Null when no FAN was issued. Used for compliance audit trails and DOL regulatory reporting.']
  further_action_notice_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Further Action Notice (FAN) was issued to the worker as part of the TNC process. True = FAN was issued and provided to the worker; False = no FAN issued. Required documentation step for TNC compliance per DHS E-Verify program rules.']
  initial_case_result varchar [note: 'The first result returned by the E-Verify system upon initial case submission. Distinct from the final case status — captures the original determination before any TNC resolution or continuance. Key field for compliance audit trails required by DOL and IRS.']
  late_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the E-Verify case was submitted after the federally mandated 3-business-day window from the employees first day of work. True = late submission; False = timely submission. Used for compliance monitoring, SLA reporting, and DOL audit risk management.']
  photo_match_result varchar [note: 'The result of the E-Verify photo matching step, where the photo on the workers identity document is compared against DHS records. Applicable when the worker presents a document with a photo that E-Verify can match. Used for fraud detection and compliance documentation.']
  re_verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workers employment authorization requires re-verification before the expiry date. True = re-verification is required; False = no re-verification needed (e.g., U.S. citizen). Drives compliance workflow alerts for assignment managers.']
  referral_agency varchar [note: 'The federal agency (Social Security Administration or Department of Homeland Security) to which the worker was referred to resolve a Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC). Determines the resolution process and timeline. Null or None when no referral was required.']
  referral_date date [note: 'The date on which the worker was referred to the SSA or DHS to resolve a Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC). Must occur within the federally mandated timeframe after TNC issuance. Null when no TNC referral was required. Tracked for DOL and DHS audit compliance.']
  referral_deadline_date date [note: 'The federally mandated deadline by which the worker must be referred to SSA or DHS to contest a Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC). Calculated from the TNC issuance date per DHS E-Verify program rules. Used for SLA monitoring and compliance alerting.']
  resolution_deadline_date date [note: 'The federally mandated deadline by which the TNC must be resolved by the referring agency (SSA or DHS). Staffing Hr must not terminate employment before this date solely due to TNC status. Tracked for EEOC anti-discrimination compliance and DOL audit.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this E-Verify case record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between Sterling Background Check integration and the DHS E-Verify federal portal.']
  sterling_case_reference varchar [note: 'The case reference number assigned by Sterling Background Check system for this E-Verify submission. Used for reconciliation between Sterling BGC integration records and the DHS E-Verify federal portal. Enables cross-system audit trail.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the E-Verify case was submitted to the DHS/SSA federal portal. This is the principal business event timestamp for the case. Must occur within 3 business days of the employees first day of work per federal E-Verify program rules.']
  tnc_issuance_date date [note: 'The date on which the Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC) was issued by the E-Verify system. Triggers the mandatory referral and resolution timeline. Null when no TNC was issued. Critical for calculating referral and resolution deadlines.']
  tnc_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the workers response and resolution outcome for a Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC). Indicates whether the worker contested the TNC, did not contest, and the resulting resolution. Null when no TNC was issued. Required for OFCCP and DOL compliance audit trails.']
  tnc_type varchar [note: 'Identifies whether the Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC) was issued by the Social Security Administration (SSA), the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), or both. Null or None when no TNC was issued. Drives the appropriate referral agency and resolution workflow.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this E-Verify case record was last modified in the Staffing Hr compliance data platform. Tracks all status changes, TNC updates, and closure events for audit trail purposes. Required for SOC 2 Type II and DOL audit compliance.']
  worker_contested_tnc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker chose to contest the Tentative Nonconfirmation (TNC) with the issuing agency (SSA or DHS). True = worker contested; False = worker did not contest. Null when no TNC was issued. Critical for FLSA and EEOC anti-discrimination compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each E-Verify case submitted to DHS/SSA for worker employment eligibility confirmation. Captures case number, submission timestamp, initial case result (Employment Authorized, Tentative Nonconfirmation - TNC, Final Nonconfirmation), TNC resolution status, referral dates, closure reason, and federal case closure code. Links to i9_verification. Sourced from Sterling BGC and E-Verify federal portal integration.'
}

Table compliance.worker_classification {
  worker_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each worker classification determination record. Primary key for the worker_classification data product.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account associated with this worker engagement and classification determination. Relevant for co-employment and joint employment risk assessment.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs define permissible worker classifications (W-2, 1099, IC) and co-employment risk mitigation clauses. Classification decisions must align with MSA terms to avoid contractual breach and misclassifi']
  credential_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Worker classification type (IC, W2, temp) determines which credential requirements apply during onboarding. Compliance officers link classification outcomes to credential requirement sets to trigger t']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC classifications require corresponding IC agreements. Real business need: 1:1 mapping between classification determination and executed IC contract for audit trail and misclassification defense.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Worker classification determinations are jurisdiction-specific — the work location determines which state ABC test, IC test, or FLSA exemption applies. Linking worker_classification to location enable']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.order_header. Business justification: Worker classification determinations (W2 vs 1099, employee vs IC) are often made during job order intake based on client requirements, job structure, and MSA terms. Links classification decisions to o']
  position_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.position_requirement. Business justification: Worker classification determinations are made against specific position requirements that define the worker_classification type. Compliance officers trace misclassification risk back to the position_r']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (candidate or placed employee) whose classification is being determined. Links to the candidate or worker master record.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs specify engagement types (temp, temp-to-perm, IC) that drive classification. Real business need: SOW-driven classification validation ensures compliance with engagement-specific terms.']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Worker classification determinations follow state-specific rules (ABC test, IC tests vary by state). This normalizes state_law_jurisdiction which can be retrieved via JOIN to state_compliance_rule. Cl']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Worker classification determinations (IC vs. W-2) often require external legal counsel or compliance advisory firms. Track supplier for audit defense preparation, cost allocation to client/engagement,']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Worker classification determinations for vendor-supplied workers must reference the vendor_agreement\'s tier_classification and contractual terms. Staffing compliance teams performing ABC test and beha']
  abc_test_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the state-level ABC Test (stricter than IRS Common Law Test) applies to this classification determination based on the work jurisdiction. True = ABC Test governs; False = IRS Common Law or DOL Economic Realities Test applies.']
  abc_test_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the ABC Test evaluation where applicable. Pass = worker qualifies as independent contractor under all three prongs; Fail = worker must be classified as W-2 employee; Not Applicable = ABC Test does not govern this jurisdiction.']
  behavioral_control_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the degree of behavioral control the staffing firm or client exercises over the worker, per the IRS Common Law Test. Higher scores indicate greater control and stronger W-2 classification support.']
  classification_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this classification determination record, used in audit correspondence, legal filings, and IRS inquiries. Format: WC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  classification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this classification determination. Pending = under review; Active = in force; Superseded = replaced by a newer determination; Appealed = under formal challenge; Revoked = withdrawn due to error or legal order.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'The determined classification type for the worker engagement. W2 = W-2 employee (temporary or permanent); 1099_IC = Independent Contractor (1099-MISC); 1099_NEC = Non-Employee Compensation contractor; C2C = Corp-to-Corp engagement; EOR = Employer of Record arrangement. This is the SSOT for IC vs. W-2 decisions.']
  client_requested_classification varchar [note: 'The worker classification type explicitly requested by the client in the job order, MSA, or SOW. May differ from the compliance-determined classification. Used to track client preference vs. legal determination and flag misalignment.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers or legal counsel to document additional observations, risk factors, remediation actions, or contextual information relevant to this classification determination. Retained for audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this worker classification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field required for DOL, IRS, and SOC 2 compliance.']
  determination_basis varchar [note: 'The legal or regulatory test framework used to arrive at the classification determination. Behavioral Control = IRS test on instruction/training; Financial Control = IRS test on investment/profit/loss; Relationship Type = IRS test on written contracts/benefits/permanency; ABC Test = state-level test (e.g., California AB5); Economic Realities = DOL FLSA test; Multi-Factor = combination of tests applied.']
  determination_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal worker classification determination was made and recorded. This is the principal business event date for this record.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this classification determination is binding and in effect for the worker engagement. May differ from determination_date if retroactive or prospective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date on which this classification determination expires or is superseded. Null for open-ended classifications. Populated when a new determination replaces this one or the engagement ends.']
  financial_control_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the degree of financial control the staffing firm or client exercises over the worker, per the IRS Common Law Test. Considers investment in tools, opportunity for profit/loss, and services available to the general market.']
  flsa_exemption_status varchar [note: 'FLSA overtime exemption classification for W-2 employees. Non-Exempt = entitled to overtime pay; Exempt categories follow the FLSA white-collar exemption tests (Executive, Administrative, Professional, Highly Compensated, Outside Sales). Drives OT and DT pay rule application. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_exempt|exempt_executive|exempt_administrative|exempt_professional|exempt_highly_compensated|exempt_outside_sales — promote to reference product]']
  ic_agreement_date date [note: 'Date on which the Independent Contractor Agreement was executed. Used to verify agreement predates the engagement start and is current for the classification period.']
  ic_agreement_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signed Independent Contractor Agreement is on file for this worker engagement. Required for 1099/IC classifications. Absence of a signed agreement increases misclassification risk.']
  irs_audit_exposure_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing compliance officer or attorney notes regarding IRS audit exposure risk for this classification. Documents specific factors that could attract IRS scrutiny, misclassification penalties, or back-tax liability under IRC Section 3509.']
  misclassification_risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall assessed risk level that the current classification determination could be challenged or reversed by the IRS, DOL, or state labor authority. Drives compliance monitoring frequency and legal review escalation.']
  nda_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signed Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is on file for this worker engagement. Tracked as part of the compliance documentation package for the classification record.']
  non_compete_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signed Non-Compete Agreement is on file for this worker. Relevant to classification because non-compete clauses are more consistent with employee relationships and may affect IC classification defensibility.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the final classification determination overrides the system-recommended or compliance-officer-recommended classification. True = an override was applied; False = determination follows standard process. Triggers mandatory documentation and senior review.']
  override_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation documenting the business or legal justification for overriding the standard classification recommendation. Required when override_flag is True. Retained for audit trail and IRS inquiry response.']
  previous_classification_type varchar [note: 'The prior classification type for this worker on this engagement, captured when a reclassification event occurs. Enables audit trail of classification changes and supports IRS back-period liability analysis.']
  reclassification_date date [note: 'Date on which a workers classification was changed from a prior type to the current classification_type. Null if this is the original classification with no prior change.']
  reclassification_trigger varchar [note: 'The event or authority that triggered a reclassification of the worker. Captures whether the change was driven by an IRS audit, DOL investigation, state labor audit, client request, proactive internal compliance review, legal counsel advice, or court order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: irs_audit|dol_audit|state_audit|client_request|internal_review|legal_counsel|court_order — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  relationship_type_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (0–100) reflecting the nature of the relationship between the worker and the engaging party, per the IRS Common Law Test. Considers written contracts, employee benefits, permanency of relationship, and integral business activity.']
  reviewing_officer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the compliance officer, HR attorney, or legal counsel who reviewed and approved this classification determination. Required for audit trail and regulatory defense.']
  reviewing_officer_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the individual who reviewed and approved the classification determination (e.g., Chief Compliance Officer, Employment Attorney, HR Director).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this classification determination originated or was last updated. Supports data lineage and reconciliation across Bullhorn ATS/CRM, SAP SuccessFactors, TempWorks Payroll, Beeline VMS, or manual entry.']
  ss8_determination_result varchar [note: 'The IRSs official determination result from Form SS-8 review. Employee = IRS determined worker is an employee; Independent Contractor = IRS confirmed IC status; Pending = IRS review in progress; Withdrawn = SS-8 filing was withdrawn.']
  ss8_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRS Form SS-8 (Determination of Worker Status for Purposes of Federal Employment Taxes and Income Tax Withholding) has been filed for this worker engagement. True = SS-8 filed with IRS; False = not filed.']
  ss8_filed_date date [note: 'Date on which IRS Form SS-8 was filed with the IRS for this worker engagement. Null if ss8_filed is False.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this worker classification record was most recently modified. Audit trail field required for DOL, IRS, and SOC 2 compliance. Tracks reclassification events and override changes.']
  voluntary_classification_settlement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the staffing firm has enrolled this worker classification under the IRS Voluntary Classification Settlement Program (VCSP), which allows prospective reclassification of workers as employees with reduced tax liability.']
  workers_comp_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker is exempt from Workers Compensation coverage based on their classification (e.g., 1099 IC workers are typically exempt). Drives payroll and insurance configuration in TempWorks Payroll.']

  Note: 'Master record capturing FLSA worker classification determinations for each worker engagement — W-2 employee vs. 1099 independent contractor (IC). Stores classification type, determination basis (behavioral control, financial control, relationship type per IRS common law test), classification date, reviewing attorney or compliance officer, client-requested classification, override flags, co-employment risk rating, and IRS audit exposure notes. SSOT for IC vs. W-2 classification decisions.'
}

Table compliance.eeoc_filing {
  eeoc_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each EEOC regulatory filing record in the Staffing Hr compliance system. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  amended_filing_eeoc_filing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original EEOC filing record that this amendment supersedes. Populated only when is_amendment is True, enabling amendment history tracking.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_account. Business justification: EEO-1 filings are submitted per employer entity. In co-employment staffing arrangements, the client account is the reporting establishment. Compliance teams must link each EEOC filing to the client ac']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs with federal contractors include EEOC/OFCCP compliance obligations. Real business need: MSA-driven filing requirements for federal contractor clients, linking filings to contractual obligations.']
  job_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.job_category. Business justification: EEO-1 Component 1 filings require headcount breakdowns by EEO-1 job category. Linking eeoc_filing to the authoritative job_category record enables regulatory reporting by pulling order/placement data ']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: EEOC multi-establishment reporting requires each filing row to reference a specific client worksite. Staffing firms filing EEO-1 Component 1 for co-employed workers at client locations must link filin']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP/EOR managed programs are distinct employer-of-record arrangements requiring separate EEOC filings. Compliance teams track EEO-1 submissions by managed program to fulfill program-level federal cont']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: EEO-1 filings in staffing are submitted by the staffing supplier as employer of record. Linking eeoc_filing to the supplier entity enables supplier-level OFCCP/EEOC regulatory reporting, diversity aud']
  accepted_date date [note: 'Date on which the EEOC officially accepted and validated this filing submission. Populated when filing_status transitions to accepted. Key date for audit readiness and compliance records.']
  certifying_official_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the company official who certified the EEOC filing. Typically a senior HR, legal, or executive officer with authority to attest to workforce data accuracy.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Official confirmation number issued by the EEOC upon successful receipt and acceptance of the filing submission. Used for audit readiness and correspondence with the EEOC.']
  count_american_indian_alaska_native varchar [note: 'Total number of employees self-identified as American Indian or Alaska Native (not Hispanic or Latino) across all job categories in this filing. One of the seven EEOC-mandated race/ethnicity categories.']
  count_asian varchar [note: 'Total number of employees self-identified as Asian (not Hispanic or Latino) across all job categories in this filing. One of the seven EEOC-mandated race/ethnicity categories.']
  count_black_african_american varchar [note: 'Total number of employees self-identified as Black or African American (not Hispanic or Latino) across all job categories in this filing. One of the seven EEOC-mandated race/ethnicity categories.']
  count_hispanic_latino varchar [note: 'Total number of employees self-identified as Hispanic or Latino across all job categories in this filing. One of the seven EEOC-mandated race/ethnicity categories.']
  count_native_hawaiian_pacific_islander varchar [note: 'Total number of employees self-identified as Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander (not Hispanic or Latino) across all job categories in this filing. One of the seven EEOC-mandated race/ethnicity categories.']
  count_two_or_more_races varchar [note: 'Total number of employees self-identified as Two or More Races (not Hispanic or Latino) across all job categories in this filing. One of the seven EEOC-mandated race/ethnicity categories.']
  count_white_non_hispanic varchar [note: 'Total number of employees self-identified as White (not Hispanic or Latino) across all job categories in this filing. One of the seven EEOC-mandated race/ethnicity categories.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EEOC filing record was first created in the compliance system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED audit field.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which the workforce headcount and demographic data was extracted to populate this EEOC filing. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  establishment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of physical establishments (locations) included in this EEOC filing. Multi-establishment employers must report headcount data for each location.']
  filing_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory deadline date by which this EEOC filing must be submitted to remain in compliance. Set annually by the EEOC.']
  filing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the compliance team regarding this EEOC filing. May include explanations for data anomalies, amendment rationale, or internal review observations.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this EEOC filing submission, used for tracking and correspondence with the EEOC. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the EEOC filing submission. Tracks progression from draft preparation through EEOC acceptance or rejection. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this transaction.']
  filing_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this EEOC report is being filed (e.g., 2023). Determines the applicable reporting period and regulatory deadline.']
  is_amendment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this filing is an amendment to a previously submitted EEOC report. Amendments correct errors or update data in an accepted original filing.']
  is_federal_contractor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing entity is a federal contractor or subcontractor subject to OFCCP regulations and Executive Order 11246, which imposes additional EEO obligations beyond standard EEOC requirements.']
  is_first_time_filer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the first EEOC EEO-1 filing submitted by this employer. First-time filers may have different onboarding requirements and EEOC outreach procedures.']
  ofccp_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EEOC filing has been flagged for or is subject to an OFCCP compliance audit or review. Enables proactive audit readiness tracking for federal contractor obligations.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason provided by the EEOC for rejecting this filing submission. Populated only when filing_status is rejected. Used to guide corrective action and resubmission.']
  report_component varchar [note: 'Specifies the EEO-1 component being filed. Component 1 covers race/ethnicity and gender by job category; Component 2 (historically) covered pay data by race/ethnicity and gender. Relevant for EEO-1 filers.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the EEOC report form being filed. EEO-1 is the primary form for private employers and federal contractors; other types apply to unions, state/local governments, and educational institutions. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this transaction.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the workforce data reporting period covered by this EEOC filing. Defines the close of the window from which the workforce snapshot is drawn.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the workforce data reporting period covered by this EEOC filing. Defines the beginning of the window from which the workforce snapshot is drawn.']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'The specific pay period date used as the workforce count snapshot for the EEO-1 filing, typically a pay period in October through December of the filing year. Required by EEOC instructions.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Method used to submit the EEOC filing to the EEOC. Options include the EEOC online portal, bulk data upload, API integration, or legacy paper submission.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the EEOC filing was officially submitted to the EEOC portal. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP) for this transaction.']
  total_female_count varchar [note: 'Total number of female employees across all job categories and establishments included in this EEOC filing. Required gender dimension for EEO-1 reporting.']
  total_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of employees across all establishments included in this EEOC filing during the snapshot pay period. Represents the aggregate workforce count reported to the EEOC.']
  total_male_count varchar [note: 'Total number of male employees across all job categories and establishments included in this EEOC filing. Required gender dimension for EEO-1 reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EEOC filing record was last modified in the compliance system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED audit field.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for EEOC (Equal Employment Opportunity Commission) regulatory filings and reports submitted on behalf of the staffing firm or client. Covers EEO-1 Component 1 report submissions, filing period, establishment count, total headcount by race/ethnicity/gender/job category, submission confirmation number, filing status, and amendment history. Supports EEOC and OFCCP audit readiness.'
}

Table compliance.osha_incident {
  osha_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each OSHA recordable workplace safety incident record. Primary key for this entity. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — represents a discrete workplace safety event with its own lifecycle.']
  assignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active placement or assignment record under which the worker was deployed at the client site at the time of the incident. Establishes the staffing assignment context for co-employment liability determination.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account (employer of record for the worksite) where the incident occurred. Used for client-level OSHA reporting, SLA compliance, and co-employment risk tracking.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs define safety standards, incident reporting obligations, and liability allocation. Real business need: MSA terms govern incident handling, client notification requirements, and liability determin']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: When an IC worker suffers a workplace incident, the ic_agreement governs indemnification scope, workers\' comp obligations, and liability. Staffing HR must determine OSHA recordability and financial li']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: OSHA incidents occur at specific client worksites; OSHA 300 logs are establishment-specific. Normalizing client_site_location text to location_id FK enables accurate incident rollup, site safety metri']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP managed programs track OSHA recordable incident rates as a program-level safety KPI reported in QBRs. Linking osha_incident to managed_program enables program-level OSHA 300 log aggregation, TRIR/']
  offboarding_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to timesheet.offboarding_record. Business justification: When workplace injuries occur near separation date, linking incident to offboarding record captures workers comp claim continuity, return-to-work vs separation decisions, and rehire eligibility impact']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contingent worker (temporary employee or contractor) who was involved in the workplace safety incident. Links to the worker/candidate master record in the staffing system.']
  regulatory_violation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_violation. Business justification: When an OSHA incident results in a citation or regulatory violation, this FK links to the regulatory_violation record. This normalizes osha_citation_number which can be retrieved via JOIN to regulator']
  safety_credential_instance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_instance. Business justification: OSHA incident investigations review whether worker held required safety credentials (OSHA 10/30, forklift cert, etc.) at time of incident. Business process: Root cause analysis validates credential st']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOW-specific worksites may have unique safety requirements. Real business need: SOW-level incident tracking for client-specific safety reporting and liability management.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: OSHA incident investigations may involve third-party safety consultants, industrial hygienists, or expert witnesses. Track supplier for cost recovery from client/carrier, vendor quality assessment, an']
  training_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.training_record. Business justification: OSHA incident root cause investigations examine whether required safety training was completed and current. Linking osha_incident to the relevant training_record enables safety compliance managers to ']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor_agreement defines workers_comp_minimum_usd and indemnification_scope for supplier-provided workers. When a vendor-supplied worker has an OSHA incident, the vendor_agreement governs liability al']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to placement.work_location. Business justification: OSHA 300 log entries are tied to specific work locations (establishments). Staffing firms track incidents by placement work_location for their own OSHA recordkeeping and safety program management. osh']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'The specific body part injured or affected by the illness (e.g., lower back, right hand, left knee, eye, head). Required for OSHA Form 301 and used for ergonomic risk analysis and workers comp claim management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: head|neck|back|shoulder|arm|hand|finger|leg|knee|foot|eye|multiple — promote to reference product]']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the corrective and preventive actions taken or planned in response to the incident (e.g., retraining, equipment repair, process change, PPE provision, worksite modification). Required for OSHA compliance program documentation and client safety reporting.']
  corrective_action_completed_date date [note: 'The actual date on which all corrective and preventive actions were fully implemented and verified. Used to close the incident record and confirm compliance with the corrective action plan.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The target completion date by which all corrective and preventive actions identified in the incident investigation must be implemented. Used for compliance tracking and client safety SLA management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this OSHA incident record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days the worker was unable to work due to the injury or illness, as counted per OSHA recordkeeping rules (capped at 180 days for OSHA 300 log purposes). Required for OSHA 300 log Column K and DART rate calculation.']
  days_restricted_duty varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days the worker was placed on restricted work activity or job transfer due to the injury or illness, as counted per OSHA recordkeeping rules (capped at 180 days). Required for OSHA 300 log Column L and DART rate calculation.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The type of event or exposure that directly produced the injury or illness, per BLS Occupational Injury and Illness Classification Manual (OIICS) event categories. Used for trend analysis, root cause classification, and OSHA 300A summary reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fall|struck_by|caught_in|overexertion|exposure|transportation|violence|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incident_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the workplace injury or illness event occurred. This is the principal real-world event date used for OSHA 300 log entry, workers comp filing deadlines, and annual 300A summary reporting. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for this transaction.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how the injury or illness occurred, including the sequence of events, equipment involved, and environmental conditions. Required for OSHA Form 301 and used for root cause analysis and corrective action planning.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the incident (e.g., INC-2024-000123). Used in OSHA 300 log entries, workers comp filings, client communications, and regulatory correspondence. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the OSHA incident record. open indicates active investigation; under_review indicates pending recordability determination; closed indicates fully resolved with corrective actions documented; appealed indicates contested recordability; voided indicates duplicate or erroneous entry.']
  incident_time timestamp [note: 'The time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) at which the workplace incident occurred. Used for shift-pattern analysis, root cause investigation, and OSHA 300 log documentation.']
  injury_illness_type varchar [note: 'OSHA-defined classification of the nature of the workplace injury or illness. Maps directly to the OSHA Form 300 column headers: injury, skin disorder, respiratory condition, poisoning, hearing loss, or all other illness. Required for OSHA 300 log and 300A annual summary.']
  injury_nature varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific nature of the injury or illness (e.g., laceration, fracture, sprain, burn, contusion, amputation, concussion). Provides granularity beyond the OSHA Form 300 type classification for root cause analysis and trend reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: laceration|fracture|sprain|strain|burn|contusion|amputation|concussion|puncture|abrasion|dislocation|crush_injury — promote to reference product]']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal incident investigation was completed and the investigation report was finalized. Used to track investigation timeliness and ensure compliance with internal safety program SLAs.']
  is_fatality boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident resulted in the death of the worker. True = fatality. Triggers mandatory OSHA reporting within 8 hours of employer knowledge under 29 CFR Part 1904.39 and activates escalation protocols.']
  is_hospitalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the worker was admitted to a hospital (inpatient) as a result of the incident. True = inpatient hospitalization. Triggers mandatory OSHA reporting within 24 hours under 29 CFR Part 1904.39.']
  is_osha_recordable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident meets OSHA recordability criteria under 29 CFR Part 1904.7 (i.e., results in days away from work, restricted duty, medical treatment beyond first aid, loss of consciousness, or diagnosis of a significant injury/illness). True = recordable; False = first-aid only. Drives OSHA 300 log entry requirement.']
  is_privacy_case boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this incident qualifies as a privacy case under OSHA recordkeeping rules (e.g., sexual assault, mental illness, HIV/AIDS, needlestick, tuberculosis). True = privacy case. When true, the workers name must be withheld from the OSHA 300 log and replaced with privacy case.']
  ita_submission_reference varchar [note: 'The confirmation identifier returned by OSHAs Injury Tracking Application (ITA) upon electronic submission of the OSHA 300A annual summary data. Required for establishments with 250+ employees or in high-hazard industries under the OSHA Electronic Recordkeeping Rule.']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'The six-digit NAICS code for the client establishment where the incident occurred. Required for OSHA 300A annual summary and OSHA electronic submission (ITA/OSHA Data Collection) to classify the industry for benchmarking incidence rates.']
  osha_300_log_entry_number varchar [note: 'The sequential entry number assigned to this incident on the OSHA Form 300 (Log of Work-Related Injuries and Illnesses) for the applicable calendar year. Required for OSHA 300A annual summary reconciliation and regulatory audit. Null if incident is not recordable.']
  osha_300_log_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year of the OSHA Form 300 log in which this incident is recorded. Used to partition OSHA 300A annual summary reporting and multi-year trend analysis.']
  osha_report_submitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required OSHA severe injury report (fatality, amputation, loss of eye, or inpatient hospitalization) has been submitted to OSHA within the mandated timeframe (8 hours for fatalities, 24 hours for other severe injuries).']
  osha_report_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time at which the OSHA severe injury report was submitted to OSHA (via phone or online). Used to verify compliance with the 8-hour (fatality) and 24-hour (severe injury) reporting deadlines under 29 CFR Part 1904.39.']
  outcome_type varchar [note: 'The most severe outcome classification of the incident per OSHA recordkeeping hierarchy: fatality, days away from work, restricted duty or job transfer, medical treatment beyond first aid, or first aid only. Maps to OSHA Form 300 columns G through J. Drives recordability and severity classification.']
  recordability_determination_date date [note: 'The date on which the safety officer or compliance team made the final determination of whether the incident is OSHA recordable. Required for audit trail and to ensure timely OSHA 300 log entry within the 7-calendar-day requirement.']
  reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the incident was formally reported to the staffing agency or client safety officer. Used to measure reporting lag compliance against OSHA 24-hour and 8-hour reporting requirements for severe injuries.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'The date on which the injured worker returned to full or modified duty following the incident. Used to calculate actual days away from work, manage assignment continuity, and track workers comp claim closure.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative or structured description of the underlying root cause(s) identified through incident investigation (e.g., inadequate training, equipment failure, unsafe work practice, environmental hazard, lack of PPE). Required for corrective action planning and OSHA compliance program documentation.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized categorical classification of the primary root cause of the incident. Enables structured trend analysis and corrective action program tracking across the contingent workforce portfolio. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: human_error|equipment_failure|environmental|process_deficiency|training_gap|ppe_failure|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this OSHA incident record was last modified. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for audit trail, change tracking, and data lineage purposes.']
  worker_job_title varchar [note: 'The job title or occupation of the contingent worker at the time of the incident. Required for OSHA Form 301 and used for occupation-level risk analysis and EEOC/OFCCP workforce safety reporting.']
  worker_tenure_days varchar [note: 'The number of days the worker had been on the current assignment at the client site at the time of the incident. Used for new-worker safety risk analysis, as OSHA data consistently shows elevated injury rates in the first 90 days of an assignment.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for OSHA recordable workplace safety incidents involving contingent workers on client sites. Captures incident date, client site location, injury/illness type, body part affected, OSHA recordability determination (recordable vs. first-aid only), days away from work, restricted duty days, OSHA 300 log entry number, workers compensation claim linkage, root cause, and corrective action taken. Supports OSHA 300/300A annual summary reporting.'
}
