Staffing compliance teams must ref']
  affected_client_count varchar [note: 'Number of client accounts impacted by this regulatory violation. Relevant for systemic violations spanning multiple client engagements (e.g., widespread I-9 non-compliance, multi-site OSHA violations). Supports enterprise-wide compliance risk assessment.']
  affected_worker_count varchar [note: 'Number of workers (temporary associates, contractors, or permanent employees) impacted by this regulatory violation. Used for severity assessment, penalty calculation, and EEOC/DOL class-action risk evaluation. Supports board-level compliance reporting.']
  agency_case_number varchar [note: 'External case or docket number assigned by the regulatory agency (e.g., EEOC charge number, OSHA inspection number, DOL case reference, IRS audit ID). Required for tracking formal enforcement actions and agency correspondence. Nullable for internally-discovered violations not yet reported to an agency.']
  agency_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency was formally notified of the violation or initiated contact with the organization. Triggers mandatory response timelines under OSHA, EEOC, and GDPR breach notification requirements. Nullable for violations not yet reported to an agency.']
  applicable_statute varchar [note: 'The specific federal, state, or local statute, regulation, or code section that was violated (e.g., 29 CFR Part 1904, FLSA Section 13(a)(1), Title VII of the Civil Rights Act, INA Section 274A, FCRA Section 604). Supports precise legal tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  board_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the board of directors or executive leadership was formally notified of this regulatory violation. Nullable when board_notification_required is false or notification has not yet occurred. Supports governance audit trails.']
  board_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation meets the threshold requiring notification to the board of directors or executive leadership. Typically triggered by critical severity, significant financial penalty, or class-action exposure. Supports board-level compliance reporting cadence.']
  co_employment_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation involves or creates co-employment or joint employment risk between the staffing firm and the client. Triggers enhanced legal review and client notification protocols. Critical for MSP/VMS program compliance and liability management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory violation record was first created in the compliance system. Supports audit trail requirements mandated by DOL, OSHA, and IRS regulatory frameworks. Distinct from discovery_date which reflects the real-world event time.']
  data_privacy_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation constitutes or involves a personal data breach requiring notification under GDPR Article 33, CCPA, or FCRA. Triggers the 72-hour GDPR breach notification clock and state-specific breach notification timelines.']
  discovery_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the regulatory violation was first identified or discovered. Serves as the principal business event date for the violation record. Critical for calculating regulatory response deadlines, statute of limitations tracking, and trending analysis.']
  discovery_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the regulatory violation was identified: internal audit, external complaint, agency notice, whistleblower report, client notification, or self-assessment. Critical for root cause analysis and improving proactive compliance controls.']
  eeoc_charge_basis varchar [note: 'The protected class or basis for an EEOC discrimination charge associated with this violation (e.g., race, color, sex, national origin, religion, age, disability, genetic information). Nullable for non-EEOC violations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: race|color|sex|national_origin|religion|age|disability|genetic_information|retaliation|other — promote to reference product]']
  financial_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of fines, penalties, or settlements assessed or anticipated as a result of this regulatory violation, expressed in USD. Includes OSHA citations, DOL back-pay orders, EEOC settlements, and IRS tax penalties. Supports financial risk reporting and board-level compliance dashboards.']
  i9_everify_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation specifically involves I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification or E-Verify non-compliance under the Immigration and Nationality Act. Triggers mandatory ICE/DHS reporting protocols and I-9 audit remediation workflows.']
  jurisdiction_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the jurisdiction in which the violation occurred (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN). Supports international compliance tracking under GDPR and other cross-border regulatory frameworks for staffing firms with global operations.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code (or equivalent jurisdiction identifier) where the violation occurred or where the applicable law is enforced. Supports state labor board reporting, state-specific wage and hour compliance, and CCPA/state privacy law tracking.']
  legal_counsel_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether internal or external legal counsel has been engaged in connection with this regulatory violation. Triggers attorney-client privilege protections and routes the record to the legal hold workflow. Required for board-level compliance reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional compliance officer notes, legal observations, or contextual information not captured in structured fields. Supports nuanced documentation of complex multi-framework violations and ongoing investigation details.']
  ofccp_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation is associated with an OFCCP compliance review or audit of federal contractor obligations. Triggers affirmative action plan review and OFCCP conciliation agreement workflows for federal contractor clients.']
  osha_citation_type varchar [note: 'OSHA-specific citation classification for safety violations: willful, serious, other-than-serious, repeat, or de minimis. Determines penalty calculation under OSHA 29 CFR Part 1903. Nullable for non-OSHA violations.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the financial penalty amount (e.g., USD for U.S. Dollar, EUR for Euro). Supports international operations under GDPR and multi-jurisdiction compliance tracking.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'The governing regulatory authority or agency associated with this violation (e.g., DOL, EEOC, OSHA, OFCCP, IRS, FCRA, GDPR, CCPA, state labor board). Drives routing to appropriate legal counsel and determines mandatory response timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DOL|EEOC|OSHA|OFCCP|IRS|FCRA|GDPR|CCPA|state_labor_board|other — promote to reference product]']
  remediation_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the remediation plan was fully executed and the violation was resolved. Used to calculate remediation cycle time KPIs and to confirm closure eligibility. Nullable until remediation is complete.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the remediation plan must be fully executed and the violation resolved. Drives SLA tracking on the compliance dashboard and triggers escalation alerts when approaching or past due.']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action plan developed to address and resolve the regulatory violation. Includes specific steps, process changes, training requirements, and system corrections required. Supports DOL, OSHA, and EEOC corrective action documentation requirements.']
  repeat_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this violation is a recurrence of a previously identified and remediated violation of the same type. Repeat violations carry significantly higher OSHA and DOL penalty multipliers and indicate systemic compliance control failures.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory violation record, tracking progress from initial identification through remediation and closure. Drives compliance dashboard KPIs and board-level reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_remediation|pending_review|resolved|closed|escalated — promote to reference product]']
  response_deadline_date date [note: 'Mandatory deadline by which the organization must respond to the regulatory agency or complete required filings. Derived from agency notification date plus statutory response windows (e.g., OSHA 15-day informal conference, EEOC 180-day charge response). Critical for SLA compliance tracking.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the underlying root cause driving the violation (e.g., process failure, training gap, system error, policy gap, vendor non-compliance, documentation failure, worker misclassification error). Supports systemic compliance improvement and regulatory risk trending. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: process_failure|training_gap|system_error|policy_gap|vendor_noncompliance|documentation_failure|misclassification_error|other — promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Standardized severity classification of the regulatory violation: minor (low risk, administrative), moderate (process failure, correctable), major (significant regulatory exposure), critical (immediate legal/financial risk, board notification required). Drives escalation workflows and KPI dashboards.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this violation record originated or was first captured (e.g., Bullhorn ATS/CRM, SAP SuccessFactors, Sterling Background Check, DocuSign CLM, TempWorks Payroll, Kronos Workforce Ready, or manual entry by compliance team). Supports data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bullhorn|sap_successfactors|sterling_bgc|docusign_clm|tempworks|kronos|manual_entry|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the regulatory violation record. Required for audit trail integrity and change tracking across the violation lifecycle. Supports SOC 2 Type II audit log requirements.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the regulatory violation, including the specific non-compliant act or omission, the circumstances under which it occurred, and the regulatory requirement that was breached. Supports legal counsel review and regulatory agency correspondence.']
  violation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the violation record (e.g., RV-2024-000123). Used in correspondence with regulatory agencies, legal counsel, and board-level reporting. Sourced from the compliance management workflow.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the regulatory violation by type (e.g., I-9 non-compliance, FLSA misclassification, EEOC discrimination, OSHA safety breach, FCRA background check violation, GDPR data breach, CCPA privacy violation, wage and hour violation, co-employment breach). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: i9_noncompliance|flsa_misclassification|eeoc_discrimination|osha_safety|fcra_violation|gdpr_breach|ccpa_violation|wage_hour|co_employment|other — promote to reference product]']
  worker_classification_type varchar [note: 'The IRS/FLSA worker classification applicable to the affected worker at the time of the violation: W-2 (employee), 1099 (independent contractor), EOR (Employer of Record), PEO (Professional Employer Organization), FTE (Full-Time Equivalent), or contract-to-hire. Central to FLSA misclassification violation tracking and IRS audit defense.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for identified regulatory violations, compliance breaches, and enforcement actions across all regulatory frameworks (DOL, EEOC, OSHA, IRS, FCRA, GDPR, CCPA, state labor boards). Captures violation type, regulatory body, applicable regulation/statute, discovery date, discovery method (internal audit, external complaint, agency notice, whistleblower report, client notification, self-assessment), severity level (minor/moderate/major/critical), affected worker or client count, root cause category, remediation plan, remediation owner, remediation due date, resolution status, financial penalty amount, legal counsel involvement flag, and board notification flag. SSOT for compliance incident tracking. Supports compliance dashboard KPIs, regulatory risk trending, and board-level compliance reporting.'
}

Table compliance.audit {
  audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each compliance audit record. Primary key for the compliance_audit data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete compliance audit event with a defined lifecycle from initiation through closure.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account associated with this audit, if the audit is client-specific (e.g., a client compliance audit or VMS-driven audit). Null for enterprise-wide or regulatory audits not tied to a single client.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Audits (OFCCP, DOL) scope MSA-covered engagements. Real business need: MSA defines audit scope, linking audit findings to contractual obligations for client reporting and remediation.']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Compliance audits (OFCCP, DOL, client audits) are scoped to specific credential packages used for a workforce program. Linking audit to credential_package enables auditors to pull all credential requi']
  credential_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: Compliance audits scoped to specific client credential requirements (e.g., license verification practices, drug screen panel compliance) reference the exact credential_requirement being audited. Enabl']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IRS SS-8 audits and DOL IC misclassification audits require examining specific IC agreements. The audit\'s worker_classification_scope directly maps to ic_agreement terms. Staffing compliance teams mus']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Compliance audits (OSHA site audits, wage-hour investigations, I-9 audits) are frequently scoped to a specific client worksite. Audit teams must link audits to the exact location for site-level correc']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP/RPO program audits (supplier compliance, worker classification, SLA adherence) are common in staffing. Managed_program_id FK enables program-level audit scope definition, corrective action trackin']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: An audit is typically scoped to verify compliance against one or more specific regulatory requirements. Adding requirement_id to audit links each audit record to the primary governing requirement bein']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOW-specific audits (e.g., federal contract compliance review). Real business need: SOW-level audit tracking for engagement-specific findings and remediation.']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Audits are frequently triggered by or scoped to specific state and local jurisdiction compliance rules (e.g., California AB5, New York paid sick leave, Illinois IDOL requirements). Adding state_compli']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Compliance audits are frequently conducted by external audit firms, legal counsel, or specialized compliance consultants. Track supplier for independence verification, cost management, vendor quality ']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: OFCCP, DOL, and state audits of vendor/supplier compliance programs require examining the vendor_agreement\'s background_check_standard, everify_required_flag, and drug_screen_required_flag. Audit scop']
  actual_penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual monetary penalty, fine, or back-wage settlement amount assessed as a result of the audit, expressed in USD. Populated upon audit closure when a regulatory determination or settlement has been reached. Distinct from estimated_financial_exposure.']
  affected_population_size varchar [note: 'Total number of workers, placements, timesheets, or records within the audit scope that were subject to review. Used to assess the breadth of compliance exposure and calculate error rates (e.g., I-9 deficiency rate per 100 workers).']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the audit (e.g., AUD-2024-000123). Used for cross-referencing with regulatory agencies, external auditors, and internal tracking systems. Sourced from the compliance management module.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the audit scope, including the business processes, organizational units, client accounts, placement types, or regulatory requirements under review. Defines the boundaries of the audit engagement.']
  audit_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the audit was initiated internally (by the compliance team), externally (by a regulatory agency or external auditor), at client request, or self-initiated as part of a proactive compliance program.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the compliance audit. Tracks progression from planning through closure. Valid values: planned (scheduled but not started), in_progress (fieldwork underway), findings_issued (draft or final report delivered), remediation (corrective actions in progress), closed (all findings resolved and audit concluded), cancelled (audit voided before completion).']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compliance audit by regulatory or operational domain. Indicates the primary subject matter of the audit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: i9_audit|payroll_tax_audit|flsa_audit|fcra_audit|soc2_audit|client_compliance_audit|dol_audit|osha_audit|eeoc_audit|ofccp_audit|workers_comp_audit|state_wage_audit — promote to reference product]']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date on which the audit was formally closed, meaning all findings have been remediated and accepted by the auditing party. Null if the audit is still open.']
  corrective_action_plan_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the formal Corrective Action Plan (CAP) must be submitted to the auditing party or regulatory agency. Drives remediation scheduling and escalation workflows.']
  corrective_action_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Corrective Action Plan (CAP) associated with this audit. Tracks whether a CAP is required, has been submitted, accepted by the auditing party, rejected, or is overdue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance audit record was first created in the system. Represents the record audit trail creation point. Sourced from the compliance management platform or manual entry in the Silver layer.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings rated as critical severity within this audit. Critical findings typically require immediate escalation and expedited remediation. Tracked separately for executive and regulatory reporting.']
  estimated_financial_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary exposure or liability associated with the audit findings, expressed in USD. Includes potential penalties, back wages, fines, or remediation costs. Used for financial risk management and executive reporting. Not a calculated aggregate — represents the compliance teams point-in-time risk estimate.']
  external_auditor_firm varchar [note: 'Name of the external auditing firm, regulatory agency, or third-party organization conducting the audit. Null for purely internal audits. Examples: KPMG, Deloitte, DOL Wage and Hour Division, EEOC field office.']
  fieldwork_end_date date [note: 'The date on which active audit fieldwork was completed. Marks the transition from evidence gathering to findings analysis and report drafting.']
  fieldwork_start_date date [note: 'The date on which active audit fieldwork (evidence collection, interviews, document review) commenced. Represents the principal real-world business event timestamp for the audit engagement start. Used for SLA and TTF (Time to Fill) compliance tracking.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic coverage of the audit, such as specific states, regions, or enterprise-wide. Relevant for state wage and hour audits (SUTA, state FLSA equivalents), CCPA (California), and multi-state compliance reviews.']
  initiating_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency, external auditing firm, or client organization that initiated or requested the audit. Examples: U.S. Department of Labor, EEOC, OSHA, IRS, client legal counsel, or an external CPA firm conducting a SOC 2 review.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary auditor responsible for conducting the audit. May be an internal compliance officer or an external auditor from a regulatory agency or third-party firm. Used for accountability and communication tracking.']
  legal_counsel_engaged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether external or internal legal counsel has been engaged in connection with this audit. True when legal representation is involved, which may affect document privilege and response strategy.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'The approach used to conduct the audit. Statistical sample audits review a representative subset; full population audits review all records; targeted sample focuses on high-risk records; desk review is document-only; on-site involves physical inspection; hybrid combines methods.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance team notes, observations, or contextual information about the audit that does not fit structured fields. May include background on regulatory context, client relationship considerations, or escalation history.']
  open_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings that remain open (not yet remediated or closed) at the time of record update. Supports real-time compliance dashboard monitoring and escalation tracking.']
  overall_disposition varchar [note: 'Final outcome or disposition of the audit. Indicates whether the organization passed, passed with findings requiring remediation, failed, or whether the audit was inconclusive or withdrawn. Key metric for executive compliance dashboards and regulatory reporting.']
  overall_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Aggregate risk rating assigned to the audit based on the severity and volume of findings. Rolled up from individual finding risk ratings. Drives escalation protocols and executive reporting. Values: low, medium, high, critical.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The ending date of the time period under audit review. Defines the latest date of business activity or records subject to examination. Together with audit_period_start_date, establishes the audit window.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The beginning date of the time period under audit review. Defines the earliest date of business activity or records subject to examination. Distinct from the audit execution start date.']
  placement_type_scope varchar [note: 'The type of worker placement covered by this audit. Indicates whether the audit applies to temporary workers, permanent placements, contract-to-hire (temp-to-perm), direct hire, SOW (Statement of Work) engagements, or all placement types. Relevant for FLSA worker classification and co-employment risk audits.']
  prior_audit_reference varchar [note: 'Audit number of the most recent prior audit of the same type, used to establish continuity and track repeat findings. Enables year-over-year compliance trend analysis without creating a hard FK dependency at this stage.']
  privilege_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine has been asserted over audit materials. When True, certain audit documents are restricted from disclosure. Relevant for regulatory audits where legal exposure exists.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'The primary federal or state regulatory citation, statute, or rule that is the subject of the audit. Examples: 29 CFR Part 516 (FLSA recordkeeping), 8 CFR 274a (I-9), 29 CFR 1904 (OSHA recordkeeping), IRC Section 3509 (worker misclassification). Supports regulatory mapping and citation-level trend analysis.']
  regulatory_notice_reference varchar [note: 'Official case number, docket number, or notice reference assigned by the regulatory agency initiating the audit. Examples: DOL case number, EEOC charge number, IRS examination reference. Used for official correspondence and regulatory tracking.']
  repeat_finding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this audit contains one or more findings that were also identified in a prior audit cycle (repeat findings). Repeat findings signal systemic control failures and typically result in elevated regulatory scrutiny and penalties.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal audit report (draft or final) was issued to stakeholders. Triggers the remediation clock for findings with regulatory deadlines.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of records, transactions, or workers included in the audit sample. Applicable when audit_methodology is statistical_sample or targeted_sample. Used to calculate deficiency rates and statistical confidence levels.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing additional specificity within the audit type. For example, within a payroll_tax_audit: FICA, FUTA, SUTA, or worker classification (W-2 vs 1099/IC). Enables granular reporting and trend analysis by compliance sub-domain.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of audit findings identified during the audit engagement. Includes all severity levels. Used for KPI reporting and trend analysis across audit cycles.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance audit record was last modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements for the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  worker_classification_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the audit covers W-2 employees, 1099 independent contractors (IC), both classifications, or is not applicable to worker classification. Critical for IRS worker misclassification audits and FLSA compliance reviews.']

  Note: 'Master record for internal and external compliance audits conducted across the staffing firms operations, with embedded audit finding detail records. Covers audit type (I-9 audit, payroll tax audit, FLSA audit, FCRA audit, SOC 2 audit, client compliance audit, DOL audit), audit scope, auditor (internal team or external firm), audit period, and final audit disposition. Each audit contains one-to-many finding records capturing: finding category (documentation gap, process failure, regulatory breach, control weakness), regulatory citation, affected population size, risk rating (low/medium/high/critical), recommended corrective action, responsible owner, due date, remediation evidence, and closure status. SSOT for all compliance audit activity and finding lifecycle management from identification through closure. Distinct from ofccp_compliance which covers OFCCP-specific federal contractor reviews and Affirmative Action Plans.'
}

Table compliance.wage_hour_determination {
  wage_hour_determination_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each wage and hour compliance determination record. Primary key for this entity. TRANSACTION_HEADER role — covers FLSA overtime eligibility, prevailing wage, exempt/non-exempt classification, and pay equity assessments.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account at whose worksite or under whose contract the worker is engaged. Client context may affect prevailing wage applicability, state-specific minimum wage, and co-employment risk.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs define pay rate approval processes, markup caps, and FLSA compliance obligations. Real business need: MSA terms govern wage determinations for contractual compliance and client approval.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Wage determinations validate against contracted rate schedules. Real business need: rate compliance validation ensures pay rates align with MSA/SOW rate cards for audit defense.']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: Wage/hour determinations for IC workers must reference the ic_agreement\'s payment_rate, payment_structure, and worker_classification_basis. DOL FLSA compliance and prevailing wage analysis for ICs req']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Prevailing wage (Davis-Bacon, SCA) and local minimum wage ordinances are location-specific (city/county level). Location_id FK provides precise jurisdiction for rate validation, compliance with local ']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: Prevailing wage and SCA wage determinations are often program-specific in government contract MSP arrangements. Linking wage_hour_determination to managed_program enables program-level wage compliance']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job order or requisition associated with this determination. Provides the job classification context used to assess FLSA exemption status and applicable wage rates.']
  position_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to joborder.position_requirement. Business justification: Wage and hour determinations (FLSA classification, prevailing wage, OT thresholds) are made at the position level. position_requirement holds pay_rate_min, pay_rate_max, employment_type, and worker_cl']
  prior_determination_wage_hour_determination_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous wage and hour determination record that this determination supersedes or replaces. Supports determination versioning and audit trail for changes in classification or pay rate over time.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the worker (candidate) subject to this wage and hour determination. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction, linking to the individual whose classification and pay rate are being assessed.']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: A wage and hour determination is made in response to or governed by a specific compliance requirement (e.g., FLSA minimum wage, Davis-Bacon prevailing wage, state overtime rules). Adding requirement_i']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs specify labor categories, bill rates, and prevailing wage requirements. Real business need: SOW-driven wage determination for engagement-specific rate validation and compliance.']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Wage and hour determinations follow state-specific compliance rules (minimum wage, overtime rules vary by state). When applicable_law references state law, this FK links to the specific state rule. Cl']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Wage/hour classifications (FLSA exemption, prevailing wage) often require external legal review or DOL compliance specialists. Track supplier for audit defense preparation, cost allocation to client/e']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Wage/hour determinations for vendor-supplied workers reference the vendor_agreement\'s markup_cap_percentage and fee_schedule_type to validate pay equity and prevailing wage compliance. Staffing compli']
  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. Business justification: Wage and hour determination references the worker classification determination. This normalizes the worker_classification field which can be retrieved via JOIN to worker_classification. Clear 1:N rela']
  applicable_law varchar [note: 'The primary federal or state law or regulation governing this determination (e.g., FLSA, Davis-Bacon Act, Service Contract Act, California Labor Code §510, New York Minimum Wage Order). Supports multi-jurisdiction compliance tracking.']
  co_employment_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed level of co-employment or joint employment risk for this worker engagement based on the degree of client control over the worker. High or critical risk may require EOR arrangement or contract restructuring to mitigate joint employer liability.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officer annotations, determination rationale, exceptions, or special circumstances relevant to this wage and hour determination. Supports DOL audit documentation and FLSA litigation defense narrative.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wage and hour determination record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements for DOL, IRS, and SOC 2 Type II compliance.']
  determination_date date [note: 'The principal business event date on which the wage and hour determination was formally made. This is the operative date for compliance purposes, DOL audit readiness, and FLSA litigation defense — distinct from record creation timestamp.']
  determination_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this determination record, used in DOL audit correspondence, compliance officer sign-off documentation, and FLSA litigation defense filings. Format: WHD-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  determination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the wage and hour determination record. draft indicates initial assessment in progress; pending_review awaits compliance officer sign-off; approved is finalized and operative; superseded replaced by a newer determination; appealed under formal challenge; closed no longer active.']
  dol_audit_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this determination record has been flagged as part of an active or anticipated DOL Wage and Hour Division audit. True triggers enhanced documentation retention and compliance officer review.']
  dol_wage_determination_number varchar [note: 'The official U.S. Department of Labor wage determination number (from SAM.gov or DOL Wage Determinations Online) applicable to this engagement under Davis-Bacon or Service Contract Act. Null if prevailing wage does not apply.']
  dt_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The daily or weekly hour threshold beyond which double-time (DT) pay is required. Applicable in states such as California where hours over 12 in a day or over 8 on the 7th consecutive day trigger DT. Null if DT does not apply to this engagement.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this wage and hour determination expires or is superseded. Null indicates the determination remains in effect indefinitely. Used to manage determination versioning and ensure current applicability.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this wage and hour determination becomes operative for the worker engagement. Pay rates, overtime thresholds, and classification rules apply starting this date.']
  exemption_basis varchar [note: 'The specific FLSA white-collar or other exemption category applied when the worker is classified as exempt. none applies to non-exempt workers. Required for DOL audit documentation and FLSA litigation defense to demonstrate the basis for exemption. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: executive|administrative|professional|computer_employee|outside_sales|highly_compensated|motor_carrier|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'FLSA overtime exemption classification for the worker. exempt workers are not entitled to overtime pay; non_exempt workers must receive 1.5x pay for hours over 40/week; highly_compensated_exempt meets the HCE threshold; partial_exempt applies to specific industry exemptions (e.g., motor carrier). Core field for FLSA litigation defense.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'The job title of the worker at the time of this determination. Used to validate FLSA exemption basis (e.g., executive, professional), prevailing wage job classification, and pay equity peer group comparisons.']
  litigation_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this determination record is subject to a litigation hold due to active or threatened FLSA litigation. Records under litigation hold must not be modified or deleted.']
  minimum_wage_applicable decimal(10,4) [note: 'The minimum wage rate (per hour, USD) applicable to this worker based on federal, state, or local law — whichever is highest. Used to validate that pay_rate_validated meets or exceeds the legal floor. Supports state-specific minimum wage compliance tracking.']
  minimum_wage_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The jurisdiction (federal, state, or local municipality) whose minimum wage rate was applied as the floor for this determination. Examples: Federal, California, New York City. Supports multi-jurisdiction compliance reporting.']
  ot_rate_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'The pay rate multiplier applied to hours worked beyond the OT threshold. Standard FLSA rate is 1.5x (time-and-a-half); double-time (DT) states or contracts may require 2.0x. Used in payroll processing and timesheet OT rule configuration.']
  ot_threshold_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The weekly (or daily, for states like California) hour threshold beyond which overtime pay is required for this worker. Standard federal FLSA threshold is 40 hours/week; California requires daily OT after 8 hours. Drives timesheet OT calculation rules.']
  pay_equity_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the pay equity review for this worker engagement. compliant indicates no pay disparity found; gap_identified flags a potential pay equity issue requiring remediation; remediated indicates a gap was corrected; not_applicable when no pay equity review was required.']
  pay_equity_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pay equity assessment was conducted as part of this determination. True triggers documentation of the pay equity analysis outcome. Supports EEOC and OFCCP pay equity compliance reporting.']
  pay_rate_type varchar [note: 'The compensation structure type for the validated pay rate. hourly for time-based workers; salary for exempt salaried employees; daily for day-rate engagements; piece_rate for output-based pay; per_diem for daily allowance-based arrangements. Affects overtime calculation methodology.']
  pay_rate_validated decimal(10,4) [note: 'The workers pay rate (per hour) that was validated against applicable minimum wage, prevailing wage, or FLSA salary threshold requirements as part of this determination. Expressed in USD per hour. Core field for DOL audit readiness and pay equity assessments.']
  prevailing_wage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a prevailing wage requirement (Davis-Bacon Act for federal construction contracts or Service Contract Act for federal service contracts) applies to this worker engagement. True triggers prevailing wage rate validation.']
  prevailing_wage_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the prevailing wage framework applicable to this engagement. davis_bacon for federal construction projects; service_contract_act for federal service contracts; state_prevailing_wage for state-mandated prevailing wage laws; none when no prevailing wage applies.']
  review_trigger varchar [note: 'The business event or reason that initiated this wage and hour determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_placement|rate_change|law_change|periodic_review|dol_audit|client_request|worker_complaint — promote to reference product]']
  salary_threshold_met boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the workers salary meets the current FLSA minimum salary threshold required for white-collar exemption ($684/week as of 2024 federal standard). True is required for exempt classification under executive, administrative, or professional exemptions.']
  sign_off_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance officer formally approved and signed off on this wage and hour determination. Required for DOL audit trail documentation.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification code for the workers role. Used for prevailing wage determination lookups (Davis-Bacon, Service Contract Act wage schedules), EEOC occupational category reporting, and OFCCP compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this determination was originated or validated. Supports data lineage tracking across the lakehouse silver layer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this wage and hour determination record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and silver layer incremental processing.']
  work_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code where the worker performs services. Determines applicable state minimum wage, state overtime rules, state prevailing wage laws, SUTA jurisdiction, and workers compensation requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for wage and hour compliance determinations made for worker engagements, covering FLSA overtime eligibility, state-specific minimum wage applicability, prevailing wage determinations (Davis-Bacon, Service Contract Act), exempt vs. non-exempt classification, and pay equity assessments. Captures determination date, applicable law/regulation, pay rate validated, overtime threshold, and compliance officer sign-off. Supports DOL audit readiness and FLSA litigation defense.'
}

Table compliance.requirement {
  requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each compliance requirement record in the reference master catalog. Primary key for the compliance_requirement data product.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom this requirement is contractually mandated. Populated only when client_specific_flag is True. Links to the client account master for requirement-to-placement enforcement scoping.']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: MSAs incorporate compliance requirements by reference (e.g., Supplier shall comply with all applicable federal contractor obligations). Real business need: MSA-driven requirement applicability for c']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Compliance requirements define which credentials are mandatory for regulatory compliance (e.g., state-specific licenses, federal contractor requirements). Business process: Compliance rule engine maps']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: IC-specific compliance requirements (insurance minimums, 1099 reporting, background checks) are contractually mandated in ic_agreements. Staffing compliance teams must link requirements to the governi']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Compliance requirements are often site-specific — city-level paid leave posting, site-specific safety training mandates, or union-site requirements. Linking requirement to location enables automated c']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.managed_program. Business justification: MSP managed programs define program-specific compliance requirements (e.g., mandatory drug screens, specific BGC tiers, EOR requirements). Linking requirement to managed_program enables program-level ']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs specify engagement-specific requirements (e.g., All workers on this SOW must hold Secret clearance). Real business need: SOW-level requirement tracking for engagement-specific compliance.']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: When a compliance requirement is state-specific (jurisdiction_scope = state/local), it references the specific state compliance rule. This creates a 1:N relationship where one state rule can be refere']
  superseded_by_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance_requirement_id of the newer requirement that replaces this record when status is superseded. Enables forward-chaining of requirement version history for audit and compliance lineage.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Compliance requirements (background check standards, drug screen panels, everify mandates) are contractually specified in vendor_agreements. Staffing vendor onboarding and compliance monitoring requir']
  applicable_placement_type varchar [note: 'Specifies which placement types this requirement governs (e.g., temporary, permanent, contract_to_hire, direct_placement, all). Drives requirement-to-placement enforcement checks in the compliance engine. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temporary|permanent|contract_to_hire|direct_placement|temp_to_perm|sow|all — promote to reference product]']
  applicable_worker_type varchar [note: 'Specifies which worker classification this requirement applies to: W-2 employees, 1099 independent contractors (IC), both, or all workers regardless of classification. Critical for FLSA worker classification enforcement and IRS co-employment risk management.']
  applies_to_offboarding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement must be fulfilled during the worker offboarding process (True), such as final I-9 retention checks, COBRA notification, or non-compete enforcement at assignment end.']
  applies_to_onboarding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement must be fulfilled during the worker onboarding process (True). Drives onboarding checklist generation in SAP SuccessFactors and Bullhorn ATS/CRM for new placements.']
  audit_trail_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement mandates a formal audit trail of compliance actions (True). When True, all enforcement checks, document submissions, and status changes must be logged for DOL, OSHA, IRS, or EEOC audit defense purposes.']
  client_specific_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement is specific to a particular clients contractual mandate (True) rather than a universal federal or industry standard (False). Client-specific requirements are typically defined in the clients MSA or SOW.']
  compliance_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this requirement must be fulfilled or re-verified: one_time (at hire/placement), per_placement (each new assignment), annual (yearly renewal), quarterly, monthly, or ongoing (continuous monitoring). Drives scheduling of compliance checks.']
  compliance_owner_role varchar [note: 'The organizational role responsible for ensuring this requirement is met and enforced (e.g., compliance_team, legal_counsel, hr_operations, recruiter, branch_manager, payroll_team). Used for workflow routing and accountability assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this compliance requirement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance logging.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this compliance requirement becomes binding and enforceable for Staffing Hr operations. Used to determine which requirements apply to placements and assignments active on or after this date.']
  enforcement_action_required varchar [note: 'Specifies the system or operational action to take when this requirement is not met: block_placement (prevent assignment activation), notify_compliance (alert compliance team), flag_for_review (queue for manual review), document_only (record non-compliance without blocking), escalate_to_legal (route to legal counsel).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this compliance requirement ceases to be enforceable or is superseded. Null indicates the requirement is open-ended with no defined expiration. Used to filter active requirements in enforcement checks.']
  external_reference_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the official regulatory text, governing body publication, or client contract clause that defines this requirement (e.g., link to eCFR, EEOC.gov guidance, OSHA standard). Enables compliance officers to access authoritative source documentation.']
  federal_contractor_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this requirement applies specifically or additionally to federal contractor placements (True), as governed by OFCCP, E-Verify Executive Order 13465, or FAR clauses. Drives enhanced compliance checks for federal contract assignments.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority, agency, or organization that issued or enforces this requirement (e.g., U.S. Department of Labor, EEOC, OSHA, OFCCP, IRS, ASA, SHRM, USCIS). Used for audit trail and regulatory reporting alignment.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days after a triggering event (e.g., hire date, placement start) within which this requirement must be fulfilled before enforcement action is triggered. For example, I-9 must be completed within 3 business days of hire.']
  industry_sector_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies the industry sector(s) to which this requirement applies (e.g., Healthcare, Finance, Government, All). Some compliance requirements are sector-specific (e.g., OSHA 300 log for manufacturing, drug screening for DOT-regulated industries). Pipe-delimited for multi-sector applicability.']
  job_category_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies the job categories or occupational classifications to which this requirement applies (e.g., All, CDL Drivers, Healthcare Workers, IT Professionals). Drives targeted enforcement for role-specific compliance mandates such as drug screening or credentialing.']
  jurisdiction_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or contractual scope of applicability for this requirement: federal (applies nationwide), state (applies within specific states — detail in state_compliance_rule), local (city/county level), client_specific (applies only to placements under a specific client contract), international (GDPR, CCPA cross-border), multi_jurisdiction (spans multiple levels).']
  mandatory_documentation_checklist varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited or structured list of required documents that must be collected and retained to satisfy this compliance requirement (e.g., I-9 Form|Passport Copy|E-Verify Case Number or EEO-1 Report|Workforce Census Data). Drives document collection workflows in onboarding and placement management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers to record additional context, interpretive guidance, exceptions, or pending regulatory changes related to this requirement. Not used for structured enforcement logic.']
  penalty_amount_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty (in USD) that can be assessed per violation of this requirement, as published by the governing regulatory body. Used in risk scoring and compliance prioritization. Expressed in USD.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the regulatory or contractual penalties for failing to meet this requirement (e.g., Civil penalty up to $2,320 per I-9 violation per USCIS schedule, Contract termination per MSA Section 12.3). Used in risk assessment and compliance training.']
  re_verification_interval_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required re-verifications when re_verification_required is True. Used to calculate next due dates for compliance tracking and automated reminders. Null when re_verification_required is False.']
  re_verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance requirement mandates periodic re-verification or renewal (True) or is satisfied once at initial compliance (False). For example, I-9 re-verification for workers with temporary employment authorization.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific legal or regulatory citation for this requirement (e.g., 8 U.S.C. § 1324a, 29 CFR Part 1602, 29 CFR 1904, IRC § 3401, FLSA 29 U.S.C. § 201). Enables precise legal traceability and audit defense.']
  requirement_category varchar [note: 'Functional compliance domain grouping this requirement belongs to (e.g., I-9 / E-Verify, EEOC Reporting, OFCCP Reporting, FLSA Classification, OSHA Safety, Background Check / FCRA, Co-Employment Risk, Non-Compete / NDA, Tax / IRS, Workers Compensation). Drives requirement-to-enforcement routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: i9_everify|eeoc_reporting|ofccp_reporting|flsa_classification|osha_safety|bgc_fcra|co_employment|non_compete_nda|tax_irs|workers_comp — promote to reference product]']
  requirement_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying this compliance requirement (e.g., I9-FED-001, EEOC-RPT-002, OSHA-300-LOG). Used as the business key across systems and in compliance checklists.']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the compliance requirement, including what is mandated, who it applies to, and the business process it governs. Serves as the authoritative definition in the compliance rule catalog for enforcement checks and training.']
  requirement_name varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title of the compliance requirement (e.g., I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification, EEOC Annual EEO-1 Report, FLSA Worker Classification Determination). Used in dashboards, checklists, and audit reports.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this compliance requirement record: active (currently enforceable), inactive (no longer applicable), pending_review (under legal/compliance review), superseded (replaced by a newer version), draft (not yet published for enforcement).']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the requirement source: federal_regulation (mandated by U.S. federal law), client_contractual (mandated by client MSA/SOW), industry_standard (ASA, SIA, SHRM best practice), state_regulation (state-level law — note: detailed state rules live in state_compliance_rule), internal_policy (Staffing Hr internal mandate).']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that compliance documentation for this requirement must be retained per regulatory mandate (e.g., I-9 forms must be retained for 3 years after hire or 1 year after termination, whichever is later; OSHA 300 logs for 5 years). Drives records management and data lifecycle policies.']
  review_date date [note: 'The next scheduled date for the compliance team to review this requirement for regulatory changes, updates, or continued applicability. Supports proactive compliance management and audit readiness.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity classification for non-compliance with this requirement: critical (immediate legal/financial exposure, placement must be blocked), high (significant regulatory risk), medium (moderate risk requiring timely remediation), low (administrative or best-practice requirement).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this compliance requirement was originated or is primarily managed (e.g., Bullhorn ATS/CRM, SAP SuccessFactors, DocuSign CLM). Supports data lineage and system-of-record traceability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Bullhorn|SAP_SuccessFactors|DocuSign_CLM|Sterling_BGC|Beeline_VMS|Manual|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_course_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the training course in the Learning Management System (LMS) that satisfies the training requirement for this compliance item. Populated when training_required is True. Links to SAP SuccessFactors LMS course catalog.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of a specific training program is required to satisfy this compliance requirement (True). For example, OSHA safety training, EEOC harassment prevention training, or FLSA classification training for recruiters.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this compliance requirement record was most recently modified. Used to detect regulatory updates, track version changes, and support audit trail requirements.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this compliance requirement record, incremented when the requirement is updated due to regulatory changes or policy revisions (e.g., 1.0, 2.3). Supports audit trail and version history tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference master for federal regulations and client-specific contractual compliance requirements that govern staffing operations. Each record defines a requirement type (federal regulation, client contractual mandate, industry standard), applicable jurisdiction scope, effective date, expiration date, governing body, requirement description, and mandatory documentation checklist. Serves as the compliance rule catalog driving requirement-to-worker and requirement-to-placement enforcement checks. Distinct from state_compliance_rule which owns state-specific and local jurisdiction requirements that vary by geography.'
}

  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Program-level compliance pass/fail report']

The placement_compliance_check has no current link to skills_assessment despite tracking overall complianc']

  worker_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.worker_classification. This normalizes worker_classification and flsa_exemption_status which can be retrieved via JOIN to worker_classification.']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'The scheduled or actual start date of the worker assignment or placement. Compliance clearance must be achieved prior to this date. Used to flag placements where clearance was obtained after assignment start.']
  bgc_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the background check screening was completed and a final result was returned by Sterling Background Check. Used to assess BGC turnaround time and compliance with client SLA requirements.']
  bgc_status varchar [note: 'Status of the background check (BGC) screening for this placement as reported by Sterling Background Check. clear indicates no disqualifying findings. adverse_action indicates findings requiring pre-adverse and adverse action notices per FCRA. waived indicates client or internal authorization to proceed without BGC.']
  blocking_check_count varchar [note: 'Count of individual compliance check items that are currently in a failed or pending status that would block placement activation. A value of 0 with check_status = cleared confirms full compliance. Used in real-time compliance dashboards to prioritize remediation.']
  check_initiated_date date [note: 'The business date on which the compliance gate-check process was initiated for this placement. Represents the principal real-world event time for this transaction. Used to measure compliance processing cycle time and SLA adherence.']
  check_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this compliance check record, used in audit trails, compliance dashboards, and cross-system references. Typically formatted as CHK-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  check_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the placement compliance gate-check. cleared indicates all required checks passed and placement may go active. failed blocks placement from generating billable hours. waived indicates authorized exception granted. expired indicates a previously cleared check has lapsed and requires renewal.']
  check_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the compliance gate-check. initial applies to new placements, extension to assignment extensions, reactivation to returning workers, annual_renewal for ongoing long-term assignments, client_change when a worker transfers to a new client.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'The date on which all required compliance checks were satisfied and the placement was granted compliance clearance to go active. Null if clearance has not yet been granted. Used to calculate time-to-clearance metrics.']
  client_specific_req_status varchar [note: 'Status of any additional compliance requirements mandated by the clients MSA or VMS program beyond standard federal and state requirements. Examples include client-specific background check panels, additional drug test panels, site-specific safety certifications, or vendor credentialing requirements in Beeline VMS.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance team annotations, exception documentation, escalation notes, or additional context related to this placement compliance check. Supports audit trail and case management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this placement compliance check record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports SOC 2 Type II audit logging requirements.']
  credential_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of required professional credential and license verification for this placement. Applies to placements requiring specific certifications (e.g., nursing licenses, CDL, OSHA certifications, IT certifications). Verified through Sterling Background Check and internal credentialing processes.']
  drug_screen_date date [note: 'The date on which the drug screen specimen was collected and/or the final result was reported. Used to validate that drug screening was completed within the required pre-placement window.']
  drug_screen_status varchar [note: 'Status of the pre-placement drug screening for this worker. negative clears the worker for placement. positive blocks placement and may trigger adverse action. not_required applies when client or placement type does not mandate drug screening. Managed through Sterling Background Check.']
  eeoc_self_id_collected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EEOC voluntary self-identification form (race, ethnicity, gender, disability, veteran status) has been collected from the worker for this placement. Required for EEOC and OFCCP reporting compliance for federal contractor clients.']
  noncompete_nda_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the review for existing non-compete agreements and NDAs that could restrict the workers placement with the target client. conflict_identified requires legal review before placement proceeds. Tracked via DocuSign CLM.']
  ofccp_applicability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this placement is subject to OFCCP affirmative action and equal employment opportunity requirements because the client is a federal contractor or subcontractor. Drives additional compliance obligations including AAP tracking and adverse impact analysis.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Status of the worker onboarding process in SAP SuccessFactors, including completion of required forms, policy acknowledgments, direct deposit setup, and benefits enrollment. complete is required before a placement can generate billable hours.']
  overall_compliance_result varchar [note: 'The final aggregated compliance determination for this placement. pass authorizes the placement to go active and generate billable hours. fail blocks activation. conditional_pass indicates clearance granted with documented exceptions or waivers. pending_review indicates escalation to compliance team.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'The type of placement being compliance-checked. Determines which compliance requirements are applicable (e.g., I-9 requirements differ for SOW vs. direct placement; workers comp requirements differ for temporary vs. direct hire). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: temporary|contract_to_hire|direct_placement|per_diem|sow|rpo — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this compliance check record originated or was primarily managed. Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver layer lakehouse and cross-system reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this placement compliance check record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest state change for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  waiver_authorization varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized approver who granted a compliance waiver for this placement (if applicable). Required when check_status = waived or overall_compliance_result = conditional_pass. Provides audit trail for waiver accountability.']
  waiver_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which a granted compliance waiver expires. After this date, the outstanding compliance item must be completed or the placement may be suspended. Drives automated compliance expiry alerts.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Documented business justification for any compliance waiver granted for this placement. Required for audit trail integrity. Examples include client-approved exceptions, emergency placements with post-start completion commitments, or regulatory exemptions.']
  workers_comp_code varchar [note: 'The NCCI workers compensation classification code assigned to this placement based on the job duties and work environment. Drives workers comp premium calculation in TempWorks Payroll and ensures correct coverage is in place before assignment start.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the compliance gate-check results for each worker placement prior to assignment start or extension. Validates that all applicable federal, state, and client-specific compliance requirements are satisfied: I-9 complete, E-Verify authorized, BGC cleared, drug screen passed, required credentials verified, safety training complete, worker classification determined, co-employment risk assessed, wage and hour determination completed, and client-specific requirements met. Each check item has a pass/fail/waived/pending status, check date, reviewer, and waiver authorization (if applicable). Acts as the compliance clearance record before a placement goes active. Supports real-time compliance dashboards and prevents non-compliant placements from generating billable hours.'
}

Table compliance.state_compliance_rule {
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the state-specific compliance rule record. Primary key.']
  credential_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_requirement. Business justification: State compliance rules (licensing laws, AB5, state drug testing mandates) directly mandate specific credential requirements for placements in that jurisdiction. Linking state_compliance_rule to creden']
  primary_superseded_by_rule_state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the state_compliance_rule_id of the newer rule that supersedes this rule. Null if this rule is current and not superseded.']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.template. Business justification: State compliance rules directly drive contract template clause selection (includes_flsa_compliance_clause, includes_eeoc_compliance_clause, includes_workers_comp_clause). Staffing contract teams must ']
  applicability_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed business logic and conditions that determine when this compliance rule must be applied, including employer size, industry, worker type, and other qualifying factors.']
  applicable_industry_sector varchar [note: 'Industry sector or North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code range to which this rule applies. Null if applies to all industries.']
  applicable_worker_type varchar [note: 'Type of worker classification to which this compliance rule applies (e.g., all workers, W-2 only, 1099 independent contractors, temporary placements). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: all|W-2|1099|temporary|permanent|contract|direct hire|temp-to-perm — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for internal compliance team notes, implementation guidance, or special considerations for this state rule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this state compliance rule record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_required varchar [note: 'Description of documentation, records, or forms that must be maintained to demonstrate compliance with this state rule.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the state compliance rule becomes legally enforceable and must be applied to staffing operations.']
  employer_size_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of employees or Full-Time Equivalents (FTE) that triggers applicability of this compliance rule. Null if applies to all employer sizes.']
  enforcement_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the state or local government agency responsible for enforcing this compliance rule (e.g., California Labor Commissioner, New York Department of Labor).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the state compliance rule ceases to be enforceable. Null for rules with no sunset provision.']
  ic_test_type varchar [note: 'Type of legal test used by this jurisdiction to determine independent contractor classification (e.g., ABC test in California, common law test in other states). Only applicable for independent contractor classification rules.']
  jurisdiction_level varchar [note: 'Geographic scope level of the compliance rule indicating whether it applies at state, county, city, or municipal level.']
  jurisdiction_locality varchar [note: 'City, county, or local jurisdiction name where this rule applies if more granular than state-level (e.g., San Francisco, Cook County). Null for state-wide rules.']
  jurisdiction_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter state abbreviation where this compliance rule applies (e.g., CA, NY, TX).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this compliance rule was last reviewed by the compliance team to verify accuracy and currency.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this compliance rule to ensure it remains current with legislative changes.']
  penalty_amount_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary penalty amount in USD that may be assessed for violation of this compliance rule. Null if no maximum specified.']
  penalty_amount_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary penalty amount in USD that may be assessed for violation of this compliance rule. Null if no minimum specified.']
  penalty_description varchar [note: 'Description of penalties, fines, or sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance with this state rule.']
  penalty_per_violation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether penalties are assessed per individual violation (True) or as a single penalty regardless of violation count (False).']
  posting_requirement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance rule requires physical posting of notices at worksites or inclusion in job postings (True) or not (False).']
  preempts_local_law boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this state-level rule preempts or supersedes more restrictive local ordinances (True) or allows local jurisdictions to impose stricter requirements (False).']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Official legal citation to the statute, regulation, or ordinance that establishes this compliance requirement (e.g., California Labor Code Section 432.3).']
  requirement_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of what the compliance rule requires staffing operations to do or prohibits them from doing.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Category of state-specific compliance requirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ban-the-box|pay transparency|salary history ban|state e-verify mandate|drug testing restriction|portable background check|paid sick leave mandate|predictive scheduling|wage theft prevention notice|state mini-WARN|independent contractor test variation|workers comp mandate|unemployment insurance requirement|disability insurance requirement|family leave mandate — promote to reference product]']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that documentation related to this compliance rule must be retained. Null if no specific retention period mandated.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Internal risk rating assigned to this compliance rule based on penalty severity, enforcement frequency, and business impact of non-compliance.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the state compliance rule, typically formatted as state abbreviation followed by rule category code (e.g., CA-BTB001 for California Ban-the-Box).']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the state compliance rule (e.g., California Ban-the-Box Law, New York Salary Transparency Act).']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the state compliance rule indicating whether it is currently enforceable.']
  source_url varchar [note: 'Web URL link to the authoritative source document, legislative text, or regulatory guidance for this compliance rule.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance rule mandates specific training for recruiters, managers, or workers (True) or not (False).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this state compliance rule record was last modified in the system.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this compliance rule, incremented when amendments or updates are made to the underlying statute or regulation.']

  Note: 'Reference master for state-specific and local jurisdiction compliance requirements that vary by geography and impact multi-state staffing operations. Captures state/locality, effective date, expiration date, requirement type (ban-the-box, pay transparency, salary history ban, state E-Verify mandate, state-specific drug testing restrictions, portable background check laws, paid sick leave mandate, predictive scheduling, wage theft prevention notice, state mini-WARN Act, independent contractor classification test variations such as ABC test vs. common law test), applicability criteria (employer size threshold, industry sector, worker type), penalty for non-compliance, enforcement agency, and source citation with URL. Drives jurisdiction-aware compliance enforcement for multi-state staffing operations. Distinct from compliance_requirement which owns federal regulations and client-specific contractual mandates.'
}

Table credentialing.credential {
  credential_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each credential definition record. This is the primary key for the credential master catalog. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — this product defines the taxonomy of credential types recognized by Staffing Hr, not a candidate-held instance.']
  credential_type_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_type. Business justification: The credential master record must be classified by credential_type. credential_type is the reference table defining categories, validity periods, verification methods, and compliance frameworks for ea']
  advance_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before expiration that the system should trigger a renewal notification to the candidate and recruiter (e.g., 30, 60, 90 days). Enables proactive credential management to prevent placement disruptions due to lapsed credentials.']
  assessment_passing_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum passing score required for skills assessments or competency tests associated with this credential type (e.g., 70.00 for 70%). Null for non-assessment credential types. Used to evaluate candidate assessment results against the required threshold.']
  assessment_score_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the assessment result is measured and reported for this credential type. Percentage (0-100%), raw score (numeric count), pass/fail (binary outcome), or grade (letter grade). Determines how passing_score is interpreted.']
  bgc_check_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the type of background check (BGC) associated with this credential when it involves a Sterling Background Check screening. Drives the BGC order type submitted to Sterling. None indicates the credential does not involve a background check component. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: criminal|drug_screen|employment_verification|education_verification|license_verification|credit|none|reference_check|sex_offender|mvr — promote to reference product]']
  ceu_hours_required decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of Continuing Education Unit (CEU) or professional development hours required per renewal cycle for this credential. Null if CEU is not required. Used to validate renewal completeness before credential re-issuance.']
  ceu_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this credential requires completion of Continuing Education Units (CEU) or Continuing Professional Development (CPD) hours as part of the renewal process. When True, the renewal workflow must capture CEU completion evidence.']
  clearance_level varchar [note: 'For security clearance credential types, specifies the clearance level (e.g., Confidential, Secret, Top Secret, Top Secret/SCI). Null or none for non-clearance credentials. Critical for government staffing vertical placement eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credential definition record was first created in the Staffing Hr data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SOC 2 Type II compliance requirements.']
  credential_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying this credential type across all systems (e.g., RN_LICENSE, OSHA_30, CDL_CLASS_A, CPA_CERT). Used as the business key in ATS, VMS, and compliance systems. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  credential_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the credential, including its purpose, scope, and any special requirements or conditions. Provides context for recruiters, compliance officers, and candidates about what the credential entails and why it is required.']
  credential_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the credential, certification, license, or qualification (e.g., Registered Nurse License, OSHA 30-Hour Construction Safety, Commercial Driver License Class A, Certified Public Accountant). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.']
  credential_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this credential definition in the Staffing Hr catalog. Active credentials are available for assignment to candidates and placement eligibility checks. Deprecated credentials are retained for historical reference only. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
