  redeployment_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of workers placed by this vendor who were successfully redeployed to a new assignment upon completion of their prior assignment during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal. Measures vendors ability to retain and redeploy talent.']
  scorecard_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible business identifier for the scorecard, used in vendor communications, dispute resolution, and program reporting. Format: SC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the vendor performance scorecard. Draft: under preparation; Published: shared with vendor; Disputed: vendor has raised a formal challenge; Finalized: accepted and locked; Archived: historical record.']
  sla_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score measuring the vendors overall adherence to all program-defined SLA requirements during the evaluation period. Scored on a 0–100 scale. Encompasses submittal timeliness, onboarding compliance, and reporting obligations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this scorecard data originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Beeline VMS is the primary source for MSP/VMS program scorecards.']
  submittal_count varchar [note: 'Total number of candidate submittals made by this vendor during the evaluation period. Denominator for submittal-to-interview and QoS ratio calculations.']
  submittal_to_interview_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of candidate submittals that resulted in a client interview during the evaluation period. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4000 = 40%). Measures the relevance and quality of vendor submissions.']
  tier_change_recommended boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this scorecard evaluation has triggered a recommendation to reclassify the vendors tier designation. True if the overall score warrants a tier upgrade or downgrade per program thresholds.']
  ttf_avg_days decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average number of calendar days from job order receipt to first qualified submittal by this vendor during the evaluation period. Raw metric underlying the TTF score.']
  ttf_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'KPI score measuring the vendors performance on Time to Fill — the elapsed time from job order receipt to candidate submittal or placement confirmation. Scored on a 0–100 scale relative to program SLA benchmarks.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this scorecard record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trails, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vendor_tier varchar [note: 'Vendor tier designation at the time of this scorecard evaluation. Captures the tier in effect during the evaluation period and may trigger reclassification upon finalization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tier_1|tier_2|tier_3|preferred|approved|probation — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Periodic vendor performance scorecard capturing quantitative and qualitative KPIs for each supplier within an MSP/VMS program. Tracks evaluation period, overall score, TTF (Time to Fill) score, QoS (Quality of Submission) score, submittal-to-interview ratio, interview-to-offer ratio, offer-to-start ratio, fall-off rate, assignment completion rate, compliance rate, NPS score, client satisfaction rating, redeployment rate, and scorecard status (draft, published, disputed, finalized). Drives tier reclassification and program allocation decisions.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_contact {
  vendor_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the vendor contact record. Primary key for the vendor_contact data product.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: On-site MSP programs assign vendor contacts (on-site coordinators) to specific client locations. Staffing operations track which client site each vendor representative is stationed at for workforce ma']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor/supplier organization this contact represents. Links the contact to the parent vendor record in the vendor domain.']
  vms_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vms_enrollment. Business justification: A vendor contact who is a VMS portal user (is_vms_portal_user = TRUE, vms_portal_username populated) is associated with a specific VMS enrollment record. Adding vms_enrollment_id links the contact to ']
  communication_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor contact has opted out of non-essential communications such as marketing, newsletters, or program announcements. Mandatory for GDPR/CCPA compliance.']
  contact_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or method through which this vendor contact record was created. Used for data lineage tracking and source system reconciliation.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor contact record. Drives whether the contact is included in active communication routing and program operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor contact record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record age analytics.']
  department varchar [note: 'The department or business unit within the vendor organization where this contact works (e.g., Recruiting, Operations, Finance, Compliance). Supports organizational mapping and routing.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the vendor contact. Used for MSP program communications, submittal notifications, compliance alerts, and billing correspondence.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which this vendor contacts relationship with the MSP/VMS program was terminated or deactivated. Null for currently active contacts. Used for offboarding tracking and access revocation.']
  external_contact_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for this vendor contact as recorded in the originating source system (e.g., Beeline VMS contact ID, Bullhorn contact ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data integration.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first (given) name of the vendor contact individual. Used for personalized communication and formal correspondence.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full display name of the vendor contact (first + last name). Used in UI displays, reports, and communication templates within MSP program operations.']
  is_billing_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive billing-related communications including invoices, payment remittances, and billing dispute notifications.']
  is_compliance_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive compliance-related communications including I-9 verification notices, credentialing alerts, and regulatory audit requests.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this individual is the designated primary contact for the vendor organization within the MSP/VMS program. Only one contact per vendor should be flagged as primary at any given time.']
  is_vms_portal_user boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor contact has an active user account in the Beeline VMS portal for submitting candidates, managing timesheets, and accessing program data.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title of the vendor contact at their organization (e.g., Account Manager, Director of Staffing, VP of Operations). Used for relationship management and escalation routing.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the MSP program team had a documented interaction with this vendor contact. Used for relationship health monitoring and engagement cadence management.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last (family/surname) name of the vendor contact individual. Used for formal identification and correspondence.']
  linkedin_url varchar [note: 'LinkedIn professional profile URL for the vendor contact. Used for professional verification, relationship management, and talent acquisition partnership activities.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile/cell phone number for the vendor contact. Used for urgent communications, SMS notifications, and after-hours contact in time-sensitive staffing situations.']
  nda_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signed Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) is on file for this vendor contact. Required for contacts who have access to client confidential information or candidate data.']
  nda_signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor contact executed the Non-Disclosure Agreement. Used for compliance tracking and NDA renewal management.']
  next_scheduled_contact_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled outreach or business review with this vendor contact. Supports proactive relationship management and QBR (Quarterly Business Review) scheduling.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for MSP program team notes about the vendor contact, including relationship context, communication preferences, escalation history, or special handling instructions.']
  office_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the vendor contacts office location. Used for formal correspondence, site visit coordination, and geographic program mapping.']
  office_city varchar [note: 'City of the vendor contacts office location. Supports geographic segmentation and regional program management.']
  office_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the vendor contacts office location (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR). Supports international MSP program operations and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  office_postal_code varchar [note: 'ZIP/postal code of the vendor contacts office location. Used for geographic routing and regional program management.']
  office_state varchar [note: 'U.S. state or territory code (2-letter USPS abbreviation) of the vendor contacts office location. Used for regional program assignment and compliance jurisdiction determination.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary office/desk phone number for the vendor contact. Used for direct business communication within MSP program operations.']
  phone_extension varchar [note: 'Office phone extension number for the vendor contact. Required when the vendor organization uses a PBX system and direct dial is not available.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'The vendor contacts preferred channel for receiving communications from the MSP program team. Drives automated notification routing and outreach strategy.']
  program_access_level varchar [note: 'Level of access granted to this vendor contact within the Beeline VMS program portal. Controls what program data, rate cards, and candidate submissions the contact can view or manage.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Functional role classification of the vendor contact within the MSP/VMS program relationship. Determines communication routing and program responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: account_manager|recruiter|compliance_officer|billing_contact|executive_sponsor|operations_manager|technical_contact|onboarding_coordinator — promote to reference product]']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which this vendor contact became active in the MSP/VMS program relationship. Used for relationship tenure tracking and program onboarding analytics.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the vendor contacts primary work location (e.g., America/New_York, America/Chicago). Used to schedule communications and meetings at appropriate local times.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor contact record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  vms_portal_username varchar [note: 'Username assigned to the vendor contact for accessing the Beeline VMS supplier portal. Used for portal access tracking and account management.']

  Note: 'Master record for individual contacts associated with a vendor/supplier organization. Captures contact full name, job title, role type (account manager, recruiter, compliance officer, billing contact, executive sponsor), email, phone, mobile, preferred communication method, contact status (active, inactive), primary contact flag, and the vendor organization they represent. Supports relationship management and communication routing for MSP program operations.'
}

Table vendor.program_allocation {
  program_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each vendor program allocation record within the Beeline VMS-managed contingent workforce program. Primary key for this entity.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that owns the VMS program for which this allocation is configured. Supports multi-client MSP program management.']
  contract_rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_rate_schedule. Business justification: Allocations specify max bill/pay rates. Business needs to link to the rate schedule to enforce rate caps during requisition distribution, validate rate exceptions, and ensure allocated rates comply wi']
  credential_package_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential_package. Business justification: Program allocations restricted to vendors meeting specific credentialing packages. Required for vendor eligibility rules, allocation criteria enforcement, compliance gates, and automated vendor qualif']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Large MSP programs allocate supplier capacity by client site — different suppliers cover different locations within the same program. Site-level allocation drives requisition routing rules, fill-rate ']
  msa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Master Service Agreement (MSA) under which this vendor is engaged within the program. The MSA governs the overarching contractual terms including liability, indemnification, and compliance obligations.']
  performance_scorecard_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the most recent vendor performance scorecard that informed this allocation assignment or tier classification. Links to the vendor scorecard record used in the last reclassification decision cycle.']
  rate_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.rate_agreement. Business justification: A program allocation operates under a formally executed rate agreement that governs the markup percentages and rate terms for the vendor\'s participation in the program. Adding rate_agreement_id links ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing supplier or subcontractor assigned this allocation within the VMS program. Identifies the vendor entity in the vendor master.']
  tier_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.tier_classification. Business justification: Program allocations (requisition fill-rate quotas) are assigned per vendor tier within a program. The program_allocation table carries vendor_tier (STRING) as a denormalized tier reference. Adding tie']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Allocations (submission caps, priority rank, max rates) are contractually defined in vendor agreements. Business needs to enforce allocation rules per agreement terms and validate allocation changes a']
  vendor_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_rate_card. Business justification: A program allocation governs vendor submission quotas and fill-rate targets within a program, and operates under a specific vendor rate card that defines the applicable bill/pay rates for that allocat']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client VMS program under which this allocation is defined. Links to the Beeline VMS program record governing the contingent workforce engagement.']
  allocation_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this allocation record within the Beeline VMS program. Used in MSP reporting and vendor communications to reference a specific fill-rate assignment.']
  allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The requisition fill-rate allocation percentage assigned to this vendor for the specified job category within the VMS program. Represents the share of job orders this vendor is expected to fill relative to total program volume. Sum of all active vendor allocations per program and job category should equal 100%.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this vendor program allocation. Active: vendor is receiving job order distributions. Suspended: temporarily halted due to compliance or performance issues. Exhausted: submission quota has been fully consumed. Pending: awaiting activation. Expired: past expiration date. Terminated: permanently removed from program.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the MSP program manager or client stakeholder who approved this allocation assignment. Provides an audit trail for allocation governance and change management.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation was formally approved by the MSP program manager or client stakeholder. Establishes the governance record for allocation activation.']
  auto_distribute boolean [note: 'Indicates whether job orders matching this allocations job category are automatically distributed to this vendor by Beeline VMS without manual MSP intervention. When True, the VMS auto-routes requisitions based on tier and allocation percentage. When False, manual distribution is required.']
  compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has completed all required compliance verifications (insurance certificates, E-Verify enrollment, background check program agreement, OFCCP compliance attestation) to be active under this program allocation.']
  compliance_verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendors compliance credentials were last verified and confirmed as meeting program requirements. Used to track compliance currency and trigger re-verification workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this program allocation record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and SOC 2 compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this vendor program allocation becomes active and the vendor is eligible to receive job order distributions under the specified terms. Aligns with MSP program cycle start or tier reclassification decision date.']
  exclusive_category boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor holds exclusive rights to fill requisitions in the specified job category within this VMS program. When True, no other vendor may receive job order distributions for this category. Typically reserved for niche or specialized labor categories.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this vendor program allocation expires and the vendor is no longer eligible to receive job order distributions under the specified terms. Nullable for open-ended allocations. Typically aligned with program review cycles or scorecard-driven reclassification events.']
  first_look_days varchar [note: 'The number of business days this vendor has exclusive or priority access to a new requisition before it is released to lower-tier vendors. Enforces preferred supplier first-look access per MSP program rules. Applicable only to Tier 1 and Preferred vendors.']
  ic_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is authorized to supply Independent Contractors (ICs) under this program allocation. When True, the vendor may submit 1099 or Corp-to-Corp workers. Requires additional IC compliance validation per IRS worker classification rules.']
  job_category varchar [note: 'The labor category or job family for which this allocation applies within the VMS program (e.g., IT, Engineering, Administrative, Light Industrial, Healthcare). Governs which requisition types the vendor is authorized to fill under this allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IT|Engineering|Administrative|Light Industrial|Healthcare|Finance|Legal|Creative|Operations|Scientific — promote to reference product]']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation was last reviewed by the MSP program manager, typically as part of a periodic scorecard-driven tier reclassification cycle. Supports governance audit trails and ensures allocations are not stale.']
  max_bill_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum hourly bill rate the vendor is authorized to charge the client for placements made under this allocation and job category. Enforced by Beeline VMS rate card controls. Expressed in the programs operating currency (USD).']
  max_concurrent_submissions varchar [note: 'The maximum number of candidate submittals this vendor is permitted to have active simultaneously for a single requisition within this program and job category. Controls submission quota per Beeline VMS program configuration to enforce Quality of Submission (QoS) standards.']
  max_markup_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum markup percentage (bill rate minus pay rate, divided by pay rate) the vendor is permitted to apply for placements under this allocation. Enforces MSP program margin controls and prevents rate gouging. Expressed as a percentage.']
  max_pay_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum hourly pay rate the vendor is authorized to pay workers placed under this allocation and job category. Used to enforce spread and markup controls within the MSP program. Expressed in USD.']
  min_fill_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum requisition fill rate percentage the vendor is contractually required to achieve for this job category within the program period. Used as a performance threshold in scorecard evaluations and tier reclassification decisions. Expressed as a percentage of assigned requisitions successfully filled.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation is next scheduled for review and potential reclassification based on vendor scorecard results. Drives MSP program management workflows and ensures timely tier adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for MSP program managers to capture contextual notes about this allocation, such as rationale for tier assignment, special client instructions, or conditions attached to the allocation. Not used for structured data.']
  ot_bill_rate_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'The multiplier applied to the standard bill rate for overtime hours billed by the vendor under this allocation. Typically 1.5x for OT and 2.0x for double time (DT). Governs how overtime billing is calculated for workers placed under this program.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank of this vendor within its assigned tier for the job category. Lower values indicate higher priority for job order distribution when multiple vendors share the same tier. Used by Beeline VMS to sequence first-look access among same-tier suppliers.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workforce program under which this allocation operates. MSP: Managed Service Provider program. VMS: Vendor Management System-only program. RPO: Recruitment Process Outsourcing. SOW: Statement of Work-based engagement. IC: Independent Contractor supplier program. Direct: direct client-managed program.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this vendor program allocation is reviewed and potentially updated based on scorecard performance and tier reclassification decisions. Governs the cadence of MSP program management activities.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for why this allocation was suspended, if applicable. Populated when allocation_status is suspended. Supports MSP program governance and vendor remediation tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: performance|compliance|rate_violation|contract_breach|client_request|audit_hold|other — promote to reference product if additional values needed]']
  target_time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'The target number of business days within which the vendor is expected to submit qualified candidates for a requisition under this allocation. Represents the Time to Fill (TTF) SLA commitment for this vendor-program-job category combination.']
  target_time_to_start_days varchar [note: 'The target number of calendar days from requisition approval to worker start date for placements made under this allocation. Represents the Time to Start (TTS) SLA commitment for this vendor-program-job category combination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this program allocation record was most recently modified. Tracks the last change event for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  vms_allocation_ref varchar [note: 'The native allocation or supplier program enrollment reference number assigned by Beeline VMS for this vendor-program-job category combination. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the Beeline VMS operational system.']
  worker_type varchar [note: 'The worker classification type this allocation governs. W-2: temporary employee on agency payroll. 1099: independent contractor. Corp-to-Corp: vendor-supplied contractor via their own entity. EOR: Employer of Record arrangement. SOW: Statement of Work-based resource. Impacts tax treatment, co-employment risk, and IRS worker classification compliance.']

  Note: 'Defines the requisition fill-rate allocation and submission quota assigned to each vendor within a specific client VMS program and job category. Captures program ID, vendor ID, job category, tier classification reference, allocation percentage, maximum concurrent submissions allowed, priority rank within tier, allocation effective date, expiration date, and allocation status (active, suspended, exhausted). Governs how job orders are distributed across the vendor supply chain in Beeline VMS to ensure preferred suppliers receive first-look access per MSP program rules. Updated periodically based on scorecard results and tier reclassification decisions.'
}

Table vendor.vendor_compliance {
  vendor_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each vendor compliance record in the MSP/VMS program. Primary key for the vendor_compliance data product.']
  amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.amendment. Business justification: Contract amendments frequently introduce new compliance requirements (e.g., increased insurance minimums, new background check standards). Linking vendor_compliance records to the triggering amendment']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_audit. Business justification: Vendor compliance records should link to formal compliance audits that reviewed vendor documentation (insurance, certifications, policies). Enables audit trail, finding remediation tracking, and vendo']
  contract_msa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.msa. Business justification: Compliance requirements (insurance limits, certifications, background checks) are defined in MSAs. Business needs to link compliance records to the MSA that mandates them for audit trails, breach dete']
  credential_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to credentialing.credential. Business justification: Vendor compliance items reference specific credential types (insurance certificates, business licenses, etc.). Enables document classification, compliance mapping, audit trails, and standardized compl']
  diversity_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.diversity_certification. Business justification: vendor_compliance tracks compliance items including diversity certification obligations. When compliance_category = \'diversity_certification\', the compliance record should reference the specific diver']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Vendor compliance requirements (workers comp class codes, everify, drug-free workplace, OSHA) are location-specific in multi-site programs. Staffing compliance teams verify supplier compliance against']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Managed Service Provider (MSP) or Vendor Management System (VMS) program under which this compliance obligation is tracked. A vendor may participate in multiple programs with distinct compliance requirements.']
  nda_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.nda. Business justification: vendor_compliance tracks NDA execution status (nda_executed boolean, non_compete_policy_on_file) but has no FK to the actual NDA record. Linking to the specific NDA enables compliance teams to verify ']
  requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.requirement. Business justification: vendor_compliance records (insurance COIs, everify enrollment, SOC2 reports) must satisfy specific compliance requirements. Linking to compliance.requirement identifies which regulatory or contractual']
  sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.sla. Business justification: Compliance items (e.g., time-to-submit, on-time document delivery) may be SLA-driven. Business needs to link compliance records to the SLA they satisfy for SLA breach tracking, penalty enforcement, an']
  state_compliance_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.state_compliance_rule. Business justification: Vendor compliance obligations are often jurisdiction-specific (e.g., California AB5 IC rules, state-specific insurance minimums). Linking vendor_compliance to state_compliance_rule identifies which ju']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (staffing supplier, subcontractor, or service provider) to which this compliance record belongs. Links to the vendor master profile in the vendor domain.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor agreements specify compliance obligations (insurance, E-Verify, diversity certifications). Business needs to link compliance records to the agreement that mandates them for SLA enforcement, tie']
  alert_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before document expiration at which a compliance alert is triggered and sent to the assigned compliance officer and vendor. Enables proactive renewal management to prevent lapses in coverage or certification.']
  alert_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance expiration alert has been triggered for this record based on the configured alert threshold. True when the current date is within alert_threshold_days of document_expiration_date.']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the insurance policy is set to auto-renew upon expiration. When True, the compliance team may rely on automatic renewal; when False, proactive renewal tracking is required to prevent coverage lapses. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the insurance carrier or underwriter that issued the policy documented in this compliance record. Used to verify carrier financial strength ratings and validate certificate authenticity. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  certificate_holder varchar [note: 'Name of the entity listed as the certificate holder on the COI — typically the MSP, the end client, or both. The certificate holder is notified by the carrier in the event of policy cancellation or material change. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate number printed on the Certificate of Insurance (COI) issued by the vendors insurance carrier. Used to uniquely identify and track the specific COI document. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  coi_document_reference varchar [note: 'File path, document management system reference, or DocuSign CLM document ID for the actual Certificate of Insurance (COI) PDF submitted by the vendor. Distinct from the general document_reference field; specifically tracks the COI artifact. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the compliance obligation being tracked. Covers insurance certificates, E-Verify enrollment, EEOC/OFCCP obligations, OSHA safety programs, FCRA background check policies, I-9 audit readiness, workers compensation registration, FUTA/SUTA tax registration, non-compete policy, and data security (SOC 2). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insurance|e_verify|eeoc|ofccp|osha|fcra|i9_audit|workers_comp|futa_suta|non_compete|data_security|other — promote to reference product]']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance disposition for this obligation. compliant indicates all requirements are met; non_compliant indicates a deficiency exists; pending_review indicates documents have been submitted and are under evaluation; waived indicates the MSP or client has formally waived this requirement for the vendor.']
  coverage_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum dollar amount of coverage provided under the insurance policy per occurrence or in aggregate, as stated on the Certificate of Insurance (COI). Expressed in USD. Used to verify that vendor coverage meets MSP/client minimum requirements. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  coverage_limit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the coverage_limit_amount represents a per-occurrence limit, an aggregate annual limit, or a per-claim limit. Critical for accurately assessing whether vendor coverage meets program requirements. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor compliance record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  document_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the submitted compliance document or certificate becomes effective and valid. For insurance certificates, this is the policy inception date. For regulatory filings, this is the filing effective date.']
  document_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance document or certificate expires and must be renewed. Triggers automated compliance alerts when the expiration date is within the configured alert threshold. Critical for insurance COIs, SOC 2 reports, and E-Verify MOUs.']
  everify_employer_number varchar [note: 'The E-Verify employer identification number assigned by USCIS to the vendor upon enrollment in the E-Verify program. Required for vendors operating under federal contracts subject to FAR E-Verify clause. Applicable for compliance_category = e_verify.']
  everify_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendor enrolled in the E-Verify program with USCIS. Used to verify continuous enrollment and compliance with federal contractor E-Verify requirements. Applicable for compliance_category = e_verify.']
  insurance_type varchar [note: 'Type of insurance coverage documented in this compliance record. Covers the five standard insurance types required in MSP/VMS programs: general liability, professional liability (Errors & Omissions/E&O), workers compensation, cyber liability, and umbrella/excess liability. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  is_insurance_record boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance record is an insurance-category record requiring additional insurance-specific fields (certificate number, carrier, policy number, coverage limit, etc.). When True, all insurance sub-fields must be populated.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the compliance obligation, regulatory basis, and what the vendor must demonstrate to satisfy the requirement. Provides context for compliance officers and auditors reviewing the record.']
  item_name varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable name of the specific compliance item or obligation (e.g., General Liability Insurance Certificate, E-Verify Employer Enrollment, OSHA 300 Log Submission, SOC 2 Type II Report). Used as the display label in compliance dashboards and audit reports.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent compliance audit or review was conducted for this compliance item. Used to track audit cadence and identify overdue reviews.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance review or audit of this item. Drives compliance calendar and workload planning for compliance officers. May be set automatically based on document expiration or audit frequency rules.']
  non_compete_policy_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor has a documented non-compete or non-solicitation policy on file with the MSP program, as required by the Master Service Agreement (MSA). Prevents vendors from directly soliciting placed workers or end clients.']
  policy_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the insurance policy becomes effective, as stated on the Certificate of Insurance. May differ from document_effective_date if the COI was issued after the policy inception date. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  policy_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the insurance policy expires, as stated on the Certificate of Insurance. Used as the primary expiration trigger for insurance-category compliance alerts. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Unique policy number assigned by the insurance carrier for the coverage documented in this compliance record. Used to verify coverage with the carrier and cross-reference against the COI. Applicable only when is_insurance_record is True.']
  remediation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting the remediation plan, corrective actions taken, or outstanding steps required to bring a non-compliant vendor into compliance. Captured by the assigned compliance officer and visible in audit trails.']
  required_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of document the vendor must submit to satisfy this compliance item (e.g., Certificate of Insurance (COI), E-Verify MOU, OSHA 300 Log, SOC 2 Report, W-9, I-9 Audit Report). Drives document collection workflows in the VMS program.']
  soc2_report_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the vendors data security compliance is evidenced by a SOC 2 Type I (point-in-time design assessment) or SOC 2 Type II (operational effectiveness over a period) report. Type II is the standard requirement for MSP/VMS data security compliance. Applicable for compliance_category = data_security.']
  source_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Beeline VMS compliance record ID, DocuSign envelope ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and deduplication in the Silver Layer.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this compliance record originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and multi-system integration in the Silver Layer. Primary source is Beeline VMS; manual entries and DocuSign CLM are secondary sources.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor compliance record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale records and support incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized individual (compliance director, MSP program manager, or client representative) who approved the waiver of this compliance requirement.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which an approved compliance waiver expires and the vendor must re-demonstrate compliance or obtain a new waiver. Prevents indefinite waivers from masking persistent compliance gaps.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Documented business justification for waiving a compliance requirement for this vendor. Required when compliance_status is waived. Must be approved by an authorized compliance officer or program director.']

  Note: 'Tracks vendor-level compliance status, regulatory obligation fulfillment, and insurance certificate records for each supplier in the MSP/VMS program. Captures compliance category (insurance, E-Verify, EEOC, OFCCP, OSHA, FCRA, I-9 audit, workers comp, FUTA/SUTA registration, non-compete policy, data security SOC 2), compliance item description, required document, document expiration date, compliance status (compliant, non-compliant, pending review, waived), last audit date, next review date, remediation notes, and compliance officer assigned. For insurance-category records, additionally captures certificate number, insurance type (general liability, professional liability/E&O, workers compensation, cyber liability, umbrella), carrier name, policy number, coverage limit, certificate holder (MSP/client), additional insured status, auto-renewal flag, and COI document reference. Triggers compliance alerts when documents or certificates approach expiration. Distinct from the credentialing domain which tracks worker-level credentials.'
}

Table vendor.preferred_supplier_list {
  preferred_supplier_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for a Preferred Supplier List (PSL) entry. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: JUNCTION — associates a vendor with a client program and job category within a governed PSL framework, carrying its own lifecycle attributes (status, dates, approval authority, version).']
  client_rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client-specific rate card that governs the bill rates and pay rates applicable to this vendor for the job category within the client program. Ensures rate compliance when the vendor submits candidates.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Preferred supplier lists are frequently location-specific in regional and on-site staffing programs — local staffing firms may be preferred for specific manufacturing plants or distribution centers. P']
  managed_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client VMS program for which this PSL entry governs vendor eligibility. Links the PSL entry to the specific managed service program under which requisitions are issued.']
  msa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Master Service Agreement (MSA) governing the vendors participation in this client program. The MSA must be executed before a vendor can be placed on the PSL. Links to the contract lifecycle management record.']
  performance_scorecard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.performance_scorecard. Business justification: A PSL entry\'s performance metrics (performance_score, fall_off_rate_pct, fill_rate_pct, avg_time_to_fill_days) are denormalized copies of data from the most recent performance scorecard. Adding perfor']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the staffing supplier or subcontractor record authorized (or excluded) under this PSL entry. Identifies which vendor the PSL status applies to within the client program.']
  tier_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.tier_classification. Business justification: A Preferred Supplier List entry carries a psl_tier (STRING) designating the vendor\'s tier on the PSL. tier_classification is the authoritative tier record. Adding tier_classification_id normalizes psl']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: PSL inclusion is contractually governed by vendor agreements. Business needs to link PSL entry to the vendor agreement that grants preferred status for validating PSL eligibility, enforcing PSL-specif']
  vms_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'The vendors enrollment or registration identifier within the Beeline VMS for this specific client program. Used to cross-reference the PSL entry with the vendors VMS program participation record and to confirm active enrollment status.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the approval authority formally approved the vendors PSL status for this entry. Distinct from inclusion_date, which reflects when the vendor became operationally active on the PSL.']
  client_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for client-specific instructions, preferences, or annotations related to this vendors PSL entry. May include preferred contact protocols, special billing instructions, or program-specific requirements communicated by the client.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the vendor for this PSL entry, reflecting whether all required credentialing, insurance, and regulatory requirements have been met. compliant = all requirements satisfied; non_compliant = one or more requirements unmet; pending_review = under evaluation; waived = client-approved exception granted.']
  conditional_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing client-specific conditions, restrictions, or instructions applicable to this PSL entry when the status is conditional. Examples include geographic restrictions, role-level caps, or specific compliance prerequisites the vendor must meet.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PSL entry record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  diversity_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor holds a current, verified diversity certification applicable to this PSL entry. Supports client diversity spend reporting and OFCCP compliance without requiring the full classification detail.']
  diversity_classification varchar [note: 'Diversity certification category of the vendor as recognized under this PSL entry (e.g., MBE, WBE, SDVOSB, HUBZone, LGBTBE, SBE). Used for client diversity spend reporting and OFCCP compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MBE|WBE|SDVOSB|HUBZone|LGBTBE|SBE|DBE|VOSB — promote to reference product]']
  exclusion_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor was removed or excluded from the Preferred Supplier List for this client program and job category. Null if the vendor remains active on the PSL. Marks the end of the vendors eligibility window.']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for the vendors exclusion or suspension from this PSL entry. Required when psl_status is excluded or suspended. Supports audit trails, vendor dispute resolution, and governance reporting.']
  exclusive_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor holds exclusive rights to supply workers for the specified job category within the client program. When true, no other vendor on the PSL may submit candidates for that category.']
  ic_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor is classified as an Independent Contractor (IC) supplier, providing 1099 workers rather than W-2 employees. Affects worker classification compliance checks and tax treatment for placements under this PSL entry.']
  inclusion_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor was added to the Preferred Supplier List for this client program and job category. Marks the start of the vendors eligibility window under this PSL entry.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the vendors insurance coverage expires. Used to trigger renewal alerts and compliance reviews. If insurance lapses, the vendors PSL status may be automatically suspended pending re-verification.']
  insurance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendors required insurance certificates (general liability, workers compensation, professional liability) have been verified and are current for this PSL entry. A prerequisite for PSL active status in most client programs.']
  job_category varchar [note: 'The labor category or skill classification for which this PSL entry governs vendor eligibility (e.g., IT, Engineering, Administrative, Clinical, Light Industrial). Determines which requisition types the vendor is authorized to fulfill within the client program.']
  job_category_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code representing the job category within the VMS or client program. Used for system-level routing of requisitions to eligible vendors and for cross-program reporting.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this PSL entry was most recently reviewed by the approval authority or program governance team. Used to track review cadence compliance and identify stale PSL entries.']
  nda_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) has been executed between the vendor and the client for this program. Required for vendors accessing sensitive client information as part of the staffing engagement.']
  non_compete_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a non-compete or non-solicitation agreement has been executed with the vendor for this client program. Protects the clients workforce and candidate pipeline from direct solicitation by the vendor.']
  psl_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this PSL entry, used in VMS communications, audit trails, and client-facing documentation. Follows the format PSL-{alphanumeric code}.']
  psl_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor on this PSL entry. preferred = top-tier vendor with priority routing; approved = eligible to receive requisitions; conditional = eligible with restrictions or conditions; excluded = blocked from receiving requisitions; suspended = temporarily ineligible pending review.']
  psl_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the PSL under which this entry was created or last revised (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Supports PSL versioning and audit trails when the client program updates its approved vendor roster.']
  requisition_visibility varchar [note: 'Controls whether the vendor can see and respond to open requisitions in the VMS for this client program and job category. visible = vendor sees all eligible reqs; hidden = vendor is on PSL but not shown reqs; restricted = vendor sees only specific reqs per conditional rules.']
  review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this vendors PSL status for the client program and job category. Supports governance cadence for PSL maintenance and vendor performance evaluation cycles.']
  right_to_represent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor must obtain a Right to Represent (RTR) confirmation from the candidate before submitting to this client program. When true, the VMS enforces RTR capture prior to submittal acceptance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this PSL entry originated or was last synchronized. Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse and reconciliation between VMS and ATS records.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Beeline VMS PSL record ID). Enables traceability from the Silver Layer lakehouse record back to the operational system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  sow_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is authorized to deliver Statement of Work (SOW) engagements under this PSL entry, in addition to or instead of contingent staffing. Governs SOW requisition routing in the VMS.']
  submission_cap varchar [note: 'Maximum number of candidate submittals the vendor is permitted to make per requisition for this client program and job category. Enforced by the VMS to manage submittal volume and ensure quality of submission (QoS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PSL entry record was most recently modified. Tracks the last change event for audit trail purposes and supports incremental data loading in the Silver Layer lakehouse pipeline.']

  Note: 'Manages the Preferred Supplier List (PSL) — the curated, approved roster of vendors authorized to supply contingent workers or SOW services for a specific client program or job category. Captures client program ID, vendor ID, job category, PSL status (preferred, approved, conditional, excluded), inclusion date, exclusion date, PSL version, approval authority, and client-specific notes. Governs which vendors are visible and eligible to receive requisitions in the VMS for each client engagement.'
}

Table vendor.diversity_certification {
  diversity_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each vendor diversity certification record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  previous_certification_diversity_certification_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the prior certification record that this record supersedes or renews. Enables certification history chain tracking for audit and compliance reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor (staffing supplier, subcontractor, or service provider) that holds this diversity certification. Links to the vendor master record in the vendor domain.']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: Vendor agreements reference diversity certification status for OFCCP reporting and diversity spend commitments. vendor_agreement has diversity_certification_status (denormalized). Linking diversity_ce']
  vms_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Beeline VMS contingent workforce program under which this diversity certification is tracked and reported. Enables diversity spend analytics at the program level for MSP-managed client programs.']
  annual_revenue_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'The vendors annual gross revenue in US dollars as reported at the time of certification, used to verify small business size standard eligibility for applicable certification types (HUBZone, 8(a), SDVOB).']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification type supports automatic renewal processing through the certifying bodys online portal or VMS integration. True = auto-renewal available; False = manual renewal required.']
  certification_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance officers or supplier diversity program managers to record observations, exceptions, conditions, or special circumstances related to this diversity certification (e.g., conditional approval, pending documentation, audit findings).']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'The official certificate number or registration identifier issued by the certifying body (e.g., NMSDC, WBENC, SBA) for this diversity designation. Used for verification and OFCCP supplier diversity reporting.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic scope of this diversity certification. National = recognized across the US; Regional = recognized within a multi-state region; State = state-level certification only; Local = city or county level certification.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the diversity certification. Active = valid and in good standing; Expired = past expiration date without renewal; Pending Renewal = renewal application submitted and under review; Revoked = certification withdrawn by certifying body; Suspended = temporarily inactive pending investigation or documentation.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'The category of diversity designation held by the vendor. MBE = Minority Business Enterprise; WBE = Women Business Enterprise; SDVOB = Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Business; HUBZone = Historically Underutilized Business Zone; 8(a) = SBA 8(a) Business Development Program; LGBTBE = LGBT Business Enterprise; VOSB = Veteran-Owned Small Business. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MBE|WBE|SDVOB|HUBZone|8(a)|LGBTBE|VOSB|AABE|DBE|SBE — promote to reference product]']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'The organization that issued and validates this diversity certification. NMSDC = National Minority Supplier Development Council; WBENC = Womens Business Enterprise National Council; SBA = Small Business Administration; USVBA = U.S. Veteran Business Alliance; NGLCC = National LGBT Chamber of Commerce; NVBDC = National Veteran Business Development Council; DOT = Department of Transportation; STATE = State-level certifying authority. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NMSDC|WBENC|SBA|USVBA|NGLCC|NVBDC|DOT|STATE|CITY|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  certifying_body_region varchar [note: 'The regional chapter or affiliate of the certifying body that issued this certification (e.g., NMSDC regional council name, WBENC regional partner organization). Relevant for multi-regional supplier diversity programs.']
  client_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is eligible to be reported as qualifying diverse spend to the client under their supplier diversity program requirements. True = reportable; False = not reportable (e.g., expired, unverified, or excluded by client program rules).']
  control_and_operations boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the diverse owner(s) exercise day-to-day control and management of the business operations, as required by certifying body standards. True = confirmed control; False = control not confirmed or under review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this diversity certification record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Audit trail field for data governance and SOC 2 Type II compliance.']
  diversity_spend_category varchar [note: 'The tier classification of diversity spend associated with this certification. Tier 1 = direct spend with certified diverse vendor; Tier 2 = spend through prime vendor with certified diverse subcontractor; Tier 3 = indirect diversity spend. Used for client diversity spend reporting and OFCCP compliance.']
  document_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supporting certification document has been reviewed and verified by the MSP compliance team or supplier diversity program manager. True = document verified; False = pending verification.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this certification record is considered effective for diversity spend reporting and supplier diversity program participation. May differ from issue date if backdated or prospectively applied.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'The number of employees at the vendor organization as reported at the time of certification. Used for SBA size standard verification and diversity program eligibility assessment.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which this diversity certification expires and must be renewed to remain valid. Critical for compliance tracking, renewal alerts, and OFCCP supplier diversity spend reporting eligibility.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the certifying body officially issued or granted this diversity certification to the vendor. Used to establish certification tenure and eligibility windows.']
  minority_group varchar [note: 'For MBE (Minority Business Enterprise) certifications, the specific minority group classification as defined by NMSDC and EEOC guidelines. Applicable only when certification_type = MBE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Asian|Black|Hispanic|Native_American|Pacific_Islander|Multi|Other — promote to reference product]']
  naics_code varchar [note: 'The six-digit NAICS code representing the primary industry classification of the vendor at the time of diversity certification. Used for SBA size standard determination and diversity spend categorization by industry.']
  ofccp_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification qualifies for inclusion in OFCCP federal contractor supplier diversity compliance reporting. True = qualifies for OFCCP reporting; False = does not qualify.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the vendor business owned by the qualifying diverse individual(s) as documented in the certification application. Must meet minimum thresholds (typically 51%) set by certifying bodies for certification eligibility.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact at the vendor organization responsible for managing this diversity certification. Used for renewal notifications and compliance communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact at the vendor organization responsible for managing and renewing this diversity certification.']
  renewal_alert_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the certification expiration date at which automated renewal alerts should be triggered to the vendor and compliance team. Configurable per certification type or program requirements (e.g., 90 days for WBENC, 60 days for NMSDC).']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which the vendor submitted or completed the renewal application for this diversity certification. Distinct from expiration date; tracks the actual renewal action taken.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'The stated reason for revocation or suspension of this diversity certification, as communicated by the certifying body. Populated only when certification_status = revoked or suspended. Required for OFCCP audit trail.']
  small_business_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor also qualifies as a small business under SBA size standards at the time of certification. Relevant for HUBZone, 8(a), and SDVOB certifications that require small business status.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The originating system from which this diversity certification record was ingested or created. Beeline = Beeline VMS integration; Manual = manually entered by compliance team; NMSDC_Portal/WBENC_Portal/SBA_Portal = direct portal integration; Other = other source.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code for state-level or regional diversity certifications (e.g., DOT Disadvantaged Business Enterprise state programs, state MWBE certifications). Null for national certifications.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this diversity certification record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit trail compliance.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the compliance officer or supplier diversity program manager who verified the certification document and confirmed its authenticity.']
  verified_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification document was verified by the compliance team. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks vendor diversity certifications required for OFCCP compliance, client diversity spend reporting, and supplier diversity program participation. Captures certification type (MBE, WBE, SDVOB, HUBZone, 8(a), LGBTBE, VOSB), certifying body (NMSDC, WBENC, SBA, USVBA), certification number, issue date, expiration date, certification status (active, expired, pending renewal, revoked), and supporting document reference. Enables diversity spend analytics and OFCCP supplier diversity reporting.'
}

Table contract.msa {
  msa_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for msa']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account that this MSA governs. Links to the client account hierarchy.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: MSA signatory management is a core staffing contract lifecycle process. Normalizing the client signatory to a client_contact record enables renewal outreach, signatory change tracking, and DocuSign ro']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.template. Business justification: MSAs are drafted from contract templates. The template domain owns the clause library and standard structure. Linking msa.template_id → template enables tracking which template version was used for ea']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal amendments executed against this MSA since original execution.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MSA automatically renews at the end of each term unless terminated.']
  background_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether background checks are mandatory for all candidates placed under this MSA.']
  confidentiality_term_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that confidentiality obligations survive after MSA termination.']
  conversion_fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the candidates annual salary owed to the staffing firm if the client converts a temporary placement to permanent hire.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MSA record was first created in the system.']
  dispute_resolution_method varchar [note: 'Primary method specified in the MSA for resolving disputes between parties.']
  drug_screen_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether drug screening is mandatory for all candidates placed under this MSA.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the MSA becomes legally binding and enforceable. Start of the contract term.']
  errors_omissions_minimum_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required errors and omissions (professional liability) insurance coverage amount in USD that the staffing firm must maintain.']
  everify_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether E-Verify employment eligibility verification is required for all placements under this MSA.']
  executed_date date [note: 'Date when all parties signed the MSA and it became fully executed. May differ from effective date.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the MSA term ends. Nullable for evergreen agreements with no fixed end date.']
  general_liability_minimum_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required general liability insurance coverage amount in USD that the staffing firm must maintain.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'State or country whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of the MSA (e.g., State of Delaware, England and Wales).']
  indemnification_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope and limitations of indemnification obligations between parties, including covered claims and exclusions.']
  initial_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the initial contract term in months before any renewal periods.']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for delivering invoices to the client as specified in the MSA.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment to this MSA. Nullable if no amendments have been executed.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this MSA record was last updated in the system.']
  liability_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate liability amount that either party can be held liable for under the MSA, excluding certain carve-outs.']
  liability_cap_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the liability cap amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  msa_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the MSA. Used in all client communications and legal references.']
  msa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MSA. Tracks the agreement from draft through execution to termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|pending_signature|executed|active|suspended|expired|terminated|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  nda_attached_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a separate NDA document is attached to or incorporated into this MSA.']
  non_solicitation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MSA includes a non-solicitation clause prohibiting direct hire of placed candidates.']
  non_solicitation_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months during which the client is prohibited from directly hiring placed candidates without paying a conversion fee.']
  payment_terms_net_days varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms in days (e.g., Net 30, Net 45) for invoices issued under this MSA.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration of each automatic renewal period in months, if auto-renewal is enabled.']
  sla_attached_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal SLA document with performance metrics and penalties is attached to this MSA.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date when the MSA was terminated by either party or mutual agreement. Nullable if still active.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance written notice required by either party to terminate the MSA.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or cause for MSA termination (e.g., breach of contract, mutual agreement, client request, business closure).']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the MSA, typically including client name and agreement type.']
  vendor_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized staffing firm representative who signed the MSA on behalf of the vendor organization.']
  vendor_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title of the vendor signatory (e.g., President, VP Sales, Regional Director).']
  workers_comp_minimum_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum required workers compensation insurance coverage amount in USD that the staffing firm must maintain.']

  Note: 'Master Service Agreement — the primary commercial contract governing the overall client-staffing firm relationship. Stores MSA header data including effective dates, governing law, jurisdiction, auto-renewal terms, termination notice periods, liability caps, indemnification clauses, insurance requirements (GL, WC, E&O minimums), and MSA status lifecycle (draft, under_review, executed, expired, terminated). Links to client account hierarchy. SSOT for all client MSA terms managed via DocuSign CLM. Parent contract for SOWs, rate schedules, SLAs, and amendments.'
}

Table contract.sow {
  sow_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Statement of Work. Primary key.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account for whom this SOW is executed.']
  client_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.client_contact. Business justification: SOW authorization tracking requires knowing which client contact signed each SOW. Normalizing replaces denormalized client_signatory_name/title, enabling amendment and renewal workflows to route to th']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to client.location. Business justification: Project SOWs in staffing are frequently scoped to a single client facility (e.g., a plant ramp-up SOW). Linking SOW to location enables site-level SOW reporting, headcount tracking per facility, and c']
  msa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Master Service Agreement under which this SOW is executed. Every SOW is governed by an MSA.']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.template. Business justification: SOWs are drafted from contract templates. Linking sow.template_id → template enables tracking which template was used for each SOW engagement, supporting template governance, clause consistency enforc']
  acceptance_criteria varchar [note: 'Conditions and standards that must be met for the client to accept deliverables and authorize payment.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of formal amendments or modifications that have been executed against this SOW since original signing.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOW automatically renews unless either party provides notice of termination. True: auto-renews. False: requires explicit renewal action.']
  background_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether background checks are required for all workers assigned to this SOW. True: BGC required. False: BGC not required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SOW record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all financial amounts in this SOW (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP, EUR).']
  deliverables_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of key deliverables, milestones, or outputs expected from this SOW engagement.']
  drug_screen_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether drug screening is required for all workers assigned to this SOW. True: drug screen required. False: drug screen not required.']
  end_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual end date when work under this SOW is completed or the contract term expires. Nullable for open-ended SOWs.']
  estimated_duration_days varchar [note: 'Estimated total duration of the SOW engagement in calendar days, from start to completion.']
  estimated_fte_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of full-time equivalent resources required to deliver this SOW. May be fractional (e.g., 2.5 FTEs).']
  estimated_headcount varchar [note: 'Estimated number of individual workers or consultants to be assigned to this SOW, regardless of full-time or part-time status.']
  governing_law_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Legal jurisdiction and governing law that applies to this SOW (e.g., State of California, USA).']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of insurance coverage requirements (e.g., general liability, professional liability, workers compensation) that the staffing firm must maintain for this SOW.']
  invoice_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are issued for this SOW. Weekly: every 7 days. Biweekly: every 14 days. Monthly: once per calendar month. Milestone: upon completion of defined milestones. Upon completion: single invoice at project end. Custom: non-standard schedule.']
  nda_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether workers assigned to this SOW must sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement. True: NDA required. False: NDA not required.']
  net_payment_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due (e.g., Net 30, Net 45).']
  non_compete_clause varchar [note: 'Description of any non-compete restrictions applicable to workers or the staffing firm during and after the SOW engagement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form internal notes, comments, or special instructions related to this SOW engagement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Description of payment schedule and terms, including invoicing frequency, net payment days, and milestone-based payment triggers.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Financial structure of the SOW. Time and materials: hourly/daily rates. Fixed fee: predetermined total price. Cost plus: actual costs plus markup. Retainer: recurring periodic fee. Milestone-based: payment tied to deliverable completion. Hybrid: combination of models.']
  renewal_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOW is eligible for renewal or extension upon expiration. True: renewal allowed. False: one-time engagement only.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiration that renewal or termination notice must be provided, as specified in the SOW terms.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the work scope, deliverables, services, and responsibilities covered by this SOW.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date when the SOW was fully executed (signed by all parties) and became legally binding.']
  sla_terms varchar [note: 'Summary of service level commitments, performance metrics, response times, and quality standards defined in this SOW.']
  sow_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the SOW, typically formatted as SOW-XXXXXX. Used in client communications, invoicing, and contract references.']
  sow_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SOW. Draft: being prepared. Submitted: sent to client for review. Under review: client evaluation in progress. Approved: signed and ready to execute. Active: work in progress. On hold: temporarily paused. Completed: deliverables finished. Closed: administratively closed. Cancelled: terminated before completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|active|on_hold|completed|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sow_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SOW engagement model. Project-based: fixed deliverables and timeline. Staff augmentation: temporary workforce placement. RPO: Recruitment Process Outsourcing. MSP: Managed Service Provider program. Consulting: advisory services. Managed services: ongoing operational support.']
  start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when work under this SOW is authorized to begin.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Description of conditions, notice periods, and penalties under which either party may terminate the SOW prior to completion.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required for either party to terminate the SOW without cause.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title or name of the SOW engagement, summarizing the project or service scope.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the SOW over its entire duration, in USD. For time-and-materials SOWs, this represents the not-to-exceed cap or estimated value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SOW record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Statement of Work — a child contract under an MSA that defines a specific engagement scope, deliverables, milestones, project timelines, fixed-fee or time-and-materials pricing, and acceptance criteria. Tracks SOW status (draft, submitted, approved, active, closed), SOW type (project-based, staff augmentation, RPO, MSP), and links to the governing MSA. Managed via DocuSign CLM.'
}

Table contract.nda {
  nda_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the non-disclosure agreement record. Primary key.']
  client_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the client account party to this NDA. Links to the client master data.']
  ic_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.ic_agreement. Business justification: NDAs are commonly executed with independent contractors as part of their engagement setup, alongside the IC agreement. Linking nda.ic_agreement_id → ic_agreement allows the NDA to be associated with t']
  parent_msa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent MSA if this NDA is ancillary to or incorporated into a broader master service agreement. Nullable for standalone NDAs.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the candidate party to this NDA. Nullable if NDA is with client or vendor only.']
  sow_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a specific SOW if this NDA was executed in connection with a particular project or engagement. Nullable for general NDAs.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor party to this NDA. Nullable if NDA is with client or candidate only.']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.template. Business justification: NDAs are drafted from standard NDA templates. Linking nda.template_id → template enables tracking which template version was used for each NDA, supporting clause standardization (confidentiality scope']
  vendor_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.vendor_agreement. Business justification: NDAs are frequently executed as ancillary agreements alongside vendor/subcontractor agreements during vendor onboarding. While nda already links to supplier (cross-domain), a direct FK to vendor_agree']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the NDA automatically renews for successive terms unless terminated by either party. True if auto-renewal clause is present.']
  carve_outs varchar [note: 'Exceptions to confidentiality obligations. Standard carve-outs include information that is publicly available, independently developed, already known, or required to be disclosed by law.']
